```text
1	import { ALL_REGIONS } from './levels';
2	import type {
3	  CurriculumEntry,
4	  DraftEntry,
5	  FilterResult,
6	  Provenance,
7	  Region,
8	  WordCore,
9	  WordFilter,
10	  WordHit,
11	} from './types';
12	
13	const inRange = (
14	  n: number,
15	  [min, max]: readonly [number, number],
16	): boolean => n >= min && n <= max;
17	
18	export const isLevelInFilter = (
19	  filter: WordFilter,
20	  level: number,
21	): boolean => {
22	  if (filter.level !== undefined && filter.level !== level)
23	    return false;
24	  if (filter.levels && !filter.levels.includes(level)) return false;
25	  if (filter.levelRange && !inRange(level, filter.levelRange))
26	    return false;
27	  return true;
28	};
29	
30	export const entryMatches = (
31	  hit: WordHit,
32	  filter: WordFilter,
33	): boolean => {
34	  if (hit.region !== filter.region) return false;
35	
36	  if (filter.hasVisual && !hit.emoji && !hit.image) return false;
37	
38	  if (filter.level !== undefined && hit.level !== filter.level)
39	    return false;
40	  if (filter.levels && !filter.levels.includes(hit.level)) return false;
41	  if (filter.levelRange && !inRange(hit.level, filter.levelRange))
42	    return false;
43	
44	  if (
45	    filter.syllableCountEq !== undefined &&
46	    hit.syllableCount !== filter.syllableCountEq
47	  )
48	    return false;
49	  if (
50	    filter.syllableCountRange &&
51	    !inRange(hit.syllableCount, filter.syllableCountRange)
52	  )
53	    return false;
54	
55	  const hasGraphemeFilter =
56	    filter.graphemesAllowed !== undefined ||
57	    filter.graphemesRequired !== undefined ||
58	    filter.phonemesAllowed !== undefined ||
59	    filter.phonemesRequired !== undefined;
60	
61	  if (!hasGraphemeFilter) return true;
62	  if (!hit.graphemes) return false;
63	
64	  if (filter.graphemesAllowed) {
65	    const allowed = new Set(
66	      filter.graphemesAllowed.map((u) => `${u.g}|${u.p}`),
67	    );
68	    if (!hit.graphemes.every((g) => allowed.has(`${g.g}|${g.p}`)))
69	      return false;
70	  }
71	  if (filter.graphemesRequired) {
72	    const required = new Set(
73	      filter.graphemesRequired.map((u) => `${u.g}|${u.p}`),
74	    );
75	    if (!hit.graphemes.some((g) => required.has(`${g.g}|${g.p}`)))
76	      return false;
77	  }
78	  if (filter.phonemesAllowed) {
79	    const allowed = new Set(filter.phonemesAllowed);
80	    if (!hit.graphemes.every((g) => allowed.has(g.p))) return false;
81	  }
82	  if (filter.phonemesRequired) {
83	    const required = new Set(filter.phonemesRequired);
84	    if (!hit.graphemes.some((g) => required.has(g.p))) return false;
85	  }
86	
87	  return true;
88	};
89	
90	const coreLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: WordCore[] }>(
91	  './core/level*.json',
92	);
93	const ausLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>(
94	  './curriculum/aus/level*.json',
95	);
96	const ukLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>(
97	  './curriculum/uk/level*.json',
98	);
99	const usLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>(
100	  './curriculum/us/level*.json',
101	);
102	const brLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>(
103	  './curriculum/br/level*.json',
104	);
105	
106	const loadersForRegion = (
107	  region: Region,
108	): Record<string, () => Promise<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>> => {
109	  switch (region) {
110	    case 'aus': {
111	      return ausLoaders;
112	    }
113	    case 'uk': {
114	      return ukLoaders;
115	    }
116	    case 'us': {
117	      return usLoaders;
118	    }
119	    case 'br': {
120	      return brLoaders;
121	    }
122	  }
123	};
124	
125	let coreCache: Map<string, WordCore> | null = null;
126	const curriculumCache: Partial<Record<Region, CurriculumEntry[]>> = {};
127	
128	export const __resetChunkCacheForTests = (): void => {
129	  coreCache = null;
130	  for (const r of ALL_REGIONS) delete curriculumCache[r];
131	};
132	
133	const loadCore = async (): Promise<Map<string, WordCore>> => {
134	  if (coreCache) return coreCache;
135	  const map = new Map<string, WordCore>();
136	  const chunks = await Promise.all(
137	    Object.values(coreLoaders).map((load) => load()),
138	  );
139	  for (const chunk of chunks) {
140	    for (const entry of chunk.default) map.set(entry.word, entry);
141	  }
142	  coreCache = map;
143	  return map;
144	};
145	
146	const loadCurriculum = async (
147	  region: Region,
148	): Promise<CurriculumEntry[]> => {
149	  if (curriculumCache[region]) return curriculumCache[region];
150	  const chunks = await Promise.all(
151	    Object.values(loadersForRegion(region)).map((load) => load()),
152	  );
153	  const flat = chunks.flatMap((c) => c.default);
154	  curriculumCache[region] = flat;
155	  return flat;
156	};
157	
158	const draftToHit = (d: DraftEntry): WordHit => ({
159	  word: d.word,
160	  region: d.region,
161	  level: d.level,
162	  syllableCount: d.syllableCount,
163	  syllables: d.syllables,
164	  variants: d.variants,
165	  ipa: d.ipa || undefined,
166	  graphemes: d.graphemes,
167	  emoji: d.emoji,
168	  image: d.image,
169	  provenance: 'draft' satisfies Provenance,
170	  draftId: d.id,
171	});
172	
173	const joinHits = (
174	  curriculum: CurriculumEntry[],
175	  core: Map<string, WordCore>,
176	  region: Region,
177	): WordHit[] =>
178	  curriculum.flatMap((entry) => {
179	    const c = core.get(entry.word);
180	    if (!c) return [];
181	    return [
182	      {
183	        word: entry.word,
184	        region,
185	        level: entry.level,
186	        syllableCount: c.syllableCount,
187	        syllables: c.syllables,
188	        variants: c.variants,
189	        ipa: entry.ipa || undefined,
190	        graphemes: entry.graphemes,
191	        emoji: entry.emoji,
192	        image: entry.image,
193	        provenance: 'shipped',
194	      } as WordHit,
195	    ];
196	  });
197	
198	const sortByWord = (hits: WordHit[]): WordHit[] =>
199	  hits.toSorted((a, b) => a.word.localeCompare(b.word));
200	
201	export const loadShippedIndex = async (
202	  region: Region,
203	): Promise<Set<string>> => {
204	  const curriculum = await loadCurriculum(region);
205	  return new Set(curriculum.map((entry) => entry.word));
206	};
207	
208	export const loadShippedWordLevels = async (
209	  region: Region,
210	): Promise<Map<string, number>> => {
211	  const curriculum = await loadCurriculum(region);
212	  const map = new Map<string, number>();
213	  for (const entry of curriculum) {
214	    const existing = map.get(entry.word);
215	    if (existing === undefined || entry.level < existing) {
216	      map.set(entry.word, entry.level);
217	    }
218	  }
219	  return map;
220	};
221	
222	export const filterWords = async (
223	  filter: WordFilter,
224	): Promise<FilterResult> => {
225	  const core = await loadCore();
226	  const curriculum = await loadCurriculum(filter.region);
227	  const shipped = joinHits(curriculum, core, filter.region).filter(
228	    (h) => entryMatches(h, filter),
229	  );
230	
231	  // Lazy import to avoid Dexie singleton conflicts in environments
232	  // that also load RxDB (which bundles a different Dexie version).
233	  const { draftStore } = await import('./authoring/draftStore');
234	  const allDrafts = await draftStore.listDrafts({
235	    region: filter.region,
236	  });
237	  const drafts = allDrafts
238	    .map((d) => draftToHit(d))
239	    .filter((h) => entryMatches(h, filter));
240	
241	  if (
242	    shipped.length > 0 ||
243	    drafts.length > 0 ||
244	    filter.region === 'aus' ||
245	    filter.fallbackToAus === false
246	  ) {
247	    // Drafts shadow shipped entries for the same word — the draft is
248	    // treated as the authoritative working copy (this is how the
249	    // "edit shipped" flow persists corrections without mutating the
250	    // curriculum JSON at runtime).
251	    const draftWords = new Set(drafts.map((d) => d.word));
252	    const shippedOnly = shipped.filter((s) => !draftWords.has(s.word));
253	    return { hits: sortByWord([...shippedOnly, ...drafts]) };
254	  }
255	
256	  const ausCurriculum = await loadCurriculum('aus');
257	  const ausShipped = joinHits(ausCurriculum, core, 'aus').filter((h) =>
258	    entryMatches(h, { ...filter, region: 'aus' }),
259	  );
260	  const ausDrafts = await draftStore.listDrafts({ region: 'aus' });
261	  const ausFilteredDrafts = ausDrafts
262	    .map((d) => draftToHit(d))
263	    .filter((h) => entryMatches(h, { ...filter, region: 'aus' }));
264	  const ausDraftWords = new Set(ausFilteredDrafts.map((d) => d.word));
265	  const ausShippedOnly = ausShipped.filter(
266	    (s) => !ausDraftWords.has(s.word),
267	  );
268	  return {
269	    hits: sortByWord([...ausShippedOnly, ...ausFilteredDrafts]),
270	    usedFallback: { from: filter.region, to: 'aus' },
271	  };
272	};
273
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/routes/$locale/_app/home-screen-card-order.ts`

```text
1	export type HomeScreenCardSortRow =
2	  | { kind: 'default'; index: number; bookmarked: boolean }
3	  | { kind: 'custom'; index: number; bookmarked: boolean };
4	
5	export const compareHomeScreenCardRows = (
6	  a: HomeScreenCardSortRow,
7	  b: HomeScreenCardSortRow,
8	): number => {
9	  const byBookmark = Number(b.bookmarked) - Number(a.bookmarked);
10	  if (byBookmark !== 0) return byBookmark;
11	  if (a.kind !== b.kind) return a.kind === 'default' ? -1 : 1;
12	  return a.index - b.index;
13	};
14
```
</details></tool-use>

23	type ModalState =
24	  | { kind: 'closed' }
25	  | {
26	      kind: 'open';
27	      gameId: string;
28	      mode:
29	        | { kind: 'default' }
30	        | { kind: 'customGame'; configId: string };
31	      draft: Draft;
32	    };
33	
34	const HomeScreen = (): JSX.Element => {
35	  const { t } = useTranslation(['games', 'common']);
36	  const { locale } = useParams({ from: '/$locale' });
37	  const navigate = useNavigate({ from: '/$locale/' });
38	  const { customGames, save, update, remove, lastSessionConfigs } =
39	    useCustomGames();
40	  const { isBookmarked, toggle } = useBookmarks();
41	  const [modal, setModal] = useState<ModalState>({ kind: 'closed' });
42	
43	  const handlePlayDefault = (gameId: string) => {
44	    void navigate({
45	      to: '/$locale/game/$gameId',
46	      params: { locale, gameId },
47	      search: { configId: undefined },
48	    });
49	  };
50	
51	  const handlePlayCustomGame = (gameId: string, configId: string) => {
52	    void navigate({
53	      to: '/$locale/game/$gameId',
54	      params: { locale, gameId },
55	      search: { configId },
56	    });
57	  };
58	
59	  const openDefaultCog = async (gameId: string) => {
60	    const db = await getOrCreateDatabase();
61	    const lastDoc = await db.saved_game_configs
62	      .findOne(lastSessionConfigId(gameId))
63	      .exec();
64	    setModal({
65	      kind: 'open',
66	      gameId,
67	      mode: { kind: 'default' },
68	      draft: {
69	        config: lastDoc?.config ?? {},
70	        name: '',
71	        color: 'indigo',
72	        cover: undefined,
73	      },
74	    });
75	  };
76	
77	  const openCustomGameCog = (gameId: string, doc: CustomGameDoc) => {
78	    setModal({
79	      kind: 'open',
80	      gameId,
81	      mode: { kind: 'customGame', configId: doc.id },
82	      draft: {
83	        config: doc.config,
84	        name: doc.name,
85	        color: doc.color as GameColorKey,
86	        cover: doc.cover,
87	      },
88	    });
89	  };
90	
91	  const unsortedCards = [
92	    ...GAME_CATALOG.map((entry, index) => ({
93	      row: {
94	        kind: 'default' as const,
95	        index,
96	        bookmarked: isBookmarked({
97	          targetType: 'game',
98	          targetId: entry.id,
99	        }),
100	      },
101	      kind: 'default' as const,
102	      entry,
103	    })),
104	    ...customGames.flatMap((doc, listIndex) => {
105	      const entry = GAME_CATALOG.find((g) => g.id === doc.gameId);
106	      if (!entry) return [];
107	      return [
108	        {
109	          row: {
110	            kind: 'custom' as const,
111	            index: listIndex,
112	            bookmarked: isBookmarked({
113	              targetType: 'customGame',
114	              targetId: doc.id,
115	            }),
116	          },
117	          kind: 'custom' as const,
118	          doc,
119	          entry,
120	        },
121	      ];
122	    }),
123	  ].toSorted((a, b) => compareHomeScreenCardRows(a.row, b.row));
124	
125	  const cards = unsortedCards.map((item) => {
126	    if (item.kind === 'default') {
127	      const { entry } = item;
128	      return (
129	        <GameCard
130	          key={`default-${entry.id}`}
131	          variant="default"
132	          gameId={entry.id}
133	          title={t(entry.titleKey)}
134	          chips={configToChips(
135	            entry.id,
136	            lastSessionConfigs[entry.id] ?? {},
137	          )}
138	          onPlay={() => handlePlayDefault(entry.id)}
139	          onOpenCog={() => void openDefaultCog(entry.id)}
140	          isBookmarked={isBookmarked({
141	            targetType: 'game',
142	            targetId: entry.id,
143	          })}
144	          onToggleBookmark={() =>
145	            void toggle({ targetType: 'game', targetId: entry.id })
146	          }
147	        />
148	      );
149	    }
150	    const { doc, entry } = item;
151	    return (
152	      <GameCard
153	        key={`custom-${doc.id}`}
154	        variant="customGame"
155	        gameId={doc.gameId}
156	        title={t(entry.titleKey)}
157	        customGameName={doc.name}
158	        customGameColor={doc.color as GameColorKey}
159	        cover={doc.cover}
160	        chips={configToChips(doc.gameId, doc.config)}
161	        onPlay={() => handlePlayCustomGame(doc.gameId, doc.id)}
162	        onOpenCog={() => openCustomGameCog(doc.gameId, doc)}
163	        isBookmarked={isBookmarked({
164	          targetType: 'customGame',
165	          targetId: doc.id,
166	        })}
167	        onToggleBookmark={() =>
168	          void toggle({ targetType: 'customGame', targetId: doc.id })
169	        }
170	      />
171	    );
172	  });
173	
174	  return (
175	    <div className="px-4 py-4">
176	      <h1 className="sr-only">{t('common:home.title')}</h1>
177	      <GameGrid cards={cards} />
178	
179	      {modal.kind === 'open' && (
180	        <AdvancedConfigModal
181	          open
182	          onOpenChange={(next) => {
183	            if (!next) setModal({ kind: 'closed' });
184	          }}
185	          gameId={modal.gameId}
186	          mode={modal.mode}
187	          value={modal.draft}
188	          onChange={(patch) =>
189	            setModal((prev) =>
190	              prev.kind === 'open'
191	                ? { ...prev, draft: { ...prev.draft, ...patch } }
192	                : prev,
193	            )
194	          }
195	          existingCustomGameNames={customGames
196	            .filter((d) => d.gameId === modal.gameId)
197	            .map((d) => d.name)}
198	          onCancel={() => setModal({ kind: 'closed' })}
199	          onUpdate={async (payload) => {
200	            if (payload.configId) {
201	              await update(
202	                payload.configId,
203	                payload.config,
204	                payload.name,
205	                { cover: payload.cover, color: payload.color },
206	              );
207	            }
208	            setModal({ kind: 'closed' });
209	          }}
210	          onSaveNew={async (payload) => {
211	            await save({
212	              gameId: modal.gameId,
213	              name: payload.name,
214	              config: payload.config,
215	              color: payload.color,
216	              cover: payload.cover,
217	            });
218	            setModal({ kind: 'closed' });
219	          }}
220	          onDelete={async (configId) => {
221	            await remove(configId);
222	            setModal({ kind: 'closed' });
223	          }}
224	        />
225	      )}
226	    </div>
227	  );
228	};
229	
230	export const Route = createFileRoute('/$locale/_app/')({
231	  component: HomeScreen,
232	});
233
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/seen-words.ts`

```text
1	import { sampleHits } from './sample';
2	import type { GraphemePairFilter, WordFilter, WordHit } from './types';
3	
4	const sortedCsv = (xs: readonly string[] | undefined): string =>
5	  xs && xs.length > 0 ? xs.toSorted().join(',') : '';
6	
7	const sortedPairsCsv = (
8	  xs: readonly GraphemePairFilter[] | undefined,
9	): string =>
10	  xs && xs.length > 0
11	    ? xs
12	        .map((u) => `${u.g}|${u.p}`)
13	        .toSorted()
14	        .join(',')
15	    : '';
16	
17	/**
18	 * Stable key derived from a `WordFilter`. Two filters with the same
19	 * content produce the same signature regardless of property order or
20	 * array ordering (we sort `phonemesAllowed` etc.). Used as the key
21	 * under which "seen words" are tracked.
22	 *
23	 * Note: `fallbackToAus` is intentionally excluded from the signature
24	 * because it changes query strategy, not semantic word identity.
25	 */
26	export const filterSignature = (filter: WordFilter): string => {
27	  const parts: string[] = [
28	    `region=${filter.region}`,
29	    `level=${filter.level ?? ''}`,
30	    `levels=${sortedCsv(filter.levels?.map(String))}`,
31	    `levelRange=${filter.levelRange ? filter.levelRange.join('-') : ''}`,
32	    `syllableCountEq=${filter.syllableCountEq ?? ''}`,
33	    `syllableCountRange=${
34	      filter.syllableCountRange
35	        ? filter.syllableCountRange.join('-')
36	        : ''
37	    }`,
38	    `phonemesAllowed=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesAllowed)}`,
39	    `phonemesRequired=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesRequired)}`,
40	    `graphemesAllowed=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesAllowed)}`,
41	    `graphemesRequired=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesRequired)}`,
42	    `hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}`,
43	  ];
44	  return parts.join('|');
45	};
46	
47	export interface SeenWordsStore {
48	  get: (signature: string) => Promise<Set<string>>;
49	  addSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
50	  resetSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
51	}
52	
53	export const createInMemorySeenWordsStore = (): SeenWordsStore => {
54	  const sets = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
55	
56	  return {
57	    get: async (signature) => new Set(sets.get(signature)),
58	    addSeen: async (signature, words) => {
59	      const existing = sets.get(signature) ?? new Set<string>();
60	      for (const w of words) existing.add(w);
61	      sets.set(signature, existing);
62	    },
63	    resetSeen: async (signature, words) => {
64	      sets.set(signature, new Set(words));
65	    },
66	  };
67	};
68	
69	/**
70	 * Picks `limit` hits with the following rules:
71	 *   1. Never repeats a word within a single call (enforced by
72	 *      Fisher–Yates sampling without replacement).
73	 *   2. Prefers words not yet in `store` for this `signature`.
74	 *   3. When the unseen pile has fewer than `limit` words, takes all of
75	 *      them and tops up at random from already-seen words, then resets
76	 *      the signature's seen set to exactly the words just picked —
77	 *      starting the next cycle fresh.
78	 *   4. Not safe for concurrent callers on the same signature — the
79	 *      read-compute-write pattern can race if two awaited calls
80	 *      interleave. Callers are expected to serialize per signature.
81	 */
82	export const pickWithRecycling = async (
83	  hits: WordHit[],
84	  limit: number,
85	  signature: string,
86	  store: SeenWordsStore,
87	  seed?: string,
88	): Promise<WordHit[]> => {
89	  if (hits.length === 0 || limit <= 0) return [];
90	
91	  const seen = await store.get(signature);
92	  const unseen = hits.filter((h) => !seen.has(h.word));
93	
94	  if (unseen.length >= limit) {
95	    const picked = sampleHits(unseen, limit, seed);
96	    await store.addSeen(
97	      signature,
98	      picked.map((h) => h.word),
99	    );
100	    return picked;
101	  }
102	
103	  const seenHits = hits.filter((h) => seen.has(h.word));
104	  const recycled = sampleHits(seenHits, limit - unseen.length, seed);
105	  const combined = [...unseen, ...recycled];
106	  // Shuffle the combined pool so unseen and recycled words are interleaved,
107	  // not segregated (unseen-first would be visually obvious on exhausted plays).
108	  const picked = sampleHits(combined, limit, seed);
109	  await store.resetSeen(
110	    signature,
111	    picked.map((h) => h.word),
112	  );
113	  return picked;
114	};
115
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`

```text
1	import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
2	import type { WordSpellConfig, WordSpellRound } from './types';
3	import type { Region, SeenWordsStore } from '@/data/words';
4	import {
5	  filterSignature,
6	  filterWords,
7	  pickWithRecycling,
8	  toWordSpellRound,
9	} from '@/data/words';
10	
11	interface LibraryRoundsState {
12	  rounds: WordSpellRound[];
13	  isLoading: boolean;
14	  usedFallback?: { from: Region; to: 'aus' };
15	}
16	
17	const initialStateFor = (
18	  config: WordSpellConfig,
19	): LibraryRoundsState => {
20	  if (config.source) return { rounds: [], isLoading: true };
21	  return { rounds: config.rounds ?? [], isLoading: false };
22	};
23	
24	export const useLibraryRounds = (
25	  config: WordSpellConfig,
26	  seed: string | undefined,
27	  store: SeenWordsStore,
28	): LibraryRoundsState => {
29	  const [state, setState] = useState<LibraryRoundsState>(() =>
30	    initialStateFor(config),
31	  );
32	
33	  useEffect(() => {
34	    if (config.rounds && config.rounds.length > 0) {
35	      const explicit = config.rounds;
36	      // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/set-state-in-effect -- sync state when config transitions to explicit rounds; bail-out updater is a no-op when already in sync
37	      setState((prev) =>
38	        prev.rounds === explicit &&
39	        !prev.isLoading &&
40	        prev.usedFallback === undefined
41	          ? prev
42	          : { rounds: explicit, isLoading: false },
43	      );
44	      return;
45	    }
46	
47	    const source = config.source;
48	    if (!source) {
49	      setState((prev) =>
50	        prev.rounds.length === 0 &&
51	        !prev.isLoading &&
52	        prev.usedFallback === undefined
53	          ? prev
54	          : { rounds: [], isLoading: false },
55	      );
56	      return;
57	    }
58	
59	    const cancellation = { isCancelled: false as boolean };
60	
61	    setState((prev) =>
62	      prev.isLoading ? prev : { ...prev, isLoading: true },
63	    );
64	
65	    void (async () => {
66	      let activeFilter = source.filter;
67	      let result = await filterWords(activeFilter);
68	      if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
69	
70	      if (result.hits.length === 0 && source.filter.hasVisual) {
71	        const { hasVisual: _, ...fallbackFilter } = source.filter;
72	        activeFilter = fallbackFilter;
73	        result = await filterWords(activeFilter);
74	        // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition -- isCancelled may be set to true by cleanup between awaits
75	        if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
76	      }
77	
78	      const limit = source.limit ?? config.totalRounds;
79	      const picked = config.roundsInOrder
80	        ? result.hits.slice(0, limit)
81	        : await pickWithRecycling(
82	            result.hits,
83	            limit,
84	            filterSignature(activeFilter),
85	            store,
86	            seed,
87	          );
88	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition -- isCancelled may be set to true by cleanup between awaits
89	      if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
90	
91	      setState({
92	        rounds: picked.map((hit) => toWordSpellRound(hit)),
93	        isLoading: false,
94	        usedFallback: result.usedFallback,
95	      });
96	    })();
97	
98	    return () => {
99	      cancellation.isCancelled = true;
100	    };
101	  }, [
102	    config.rounds,
103	    config.source,
104	    config.totalRounds,
105	    config.roundsInOrder,
106	    seed,
107	    store,
108	  ]);
109	
110	  return state;
111	};
112
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import type { AnswerGameConfig } from '@/components/answer-game/types';
2	import type {
3	  LevelGraphemeUnit,
4	  Region,
5	  WordSpellSource,
6	} from '@/data/words';
7	import type { ConfigField } from '@/lib/config-fields';
8	
9	export type WordSpellSimpleConfig = {
10	  configMode: 'simple';
11	  selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[];
12	  region: Region;
13	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
14	  /**
15	   * Simple form supports two kid-friendly modes:
16	   *  - 'recall': pick one or more level grapheme units; word library filtered to that scope
17	   *  - 'picture': image-prompted rounds, no level selection required
18	   */
19	  mode?: 'picture' | 'recall';
20	};
21	
22	export interface GapDefinition {
23	  word: string;
24	  distractors?: string[];
25	}
26	
27	export interface WordSpellRound {
28	  word: string;
29	  /**
30	   * Native emoji (or ZWJ sequence) for picture mode. When set, this is shown instead of `image` / `sceneImage`.
31	   */
32	  emoji?: string;
33	  /** Fluent Emoji SVG path, URL, or `/public` asset path (picture/sentence-gap modes) */
34	  image?: string;
35	  /** Sentence with a blank for sentence-gap mode: "The ___ sat on the mat." */
36	  sentence?: string;
37	  /** Scene illustration for sentence-gap mode */
38	  sceneImage?: string;
39	  /** Optional custom audio override instead of TTS */
40	  audioOverride?: string;
41	  /** Gap definitions for sentence-gap mode. Each entry maps to a {N} placeholder. */
42	  gaps?: GapDefinition[];
43	}
44	
45	export interface WordSpellConfig extends AnswerGameConfig {
46	  component: 'WordSpell';
47	  configMode?: 'simple' | 'advanced';

+# Handoff: Consolidate Word and Emoji Data (#277)
+
+**Date:** 2026-05-01
+**Branch:** issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
+**Worktree:** worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
+**Worktree path:** /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
+**Git status:** clean
+**Last commit:** 49c8277e docs: require full worktree paths in agent output (#277)
+**PR:** #281 — open
+
+## Resume command
+
+```
+/resync
+cd worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
+# Execute the implementation plan — choose subagent-driven or inline
+# Plan: worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji.md
+```
+
+## Current state
+
+**Task:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
+**Phase:** ready to execute
+**Progress:** Requirements doc and implementation plan complete and reviewed. Ready for execution.
+
+## What we did
+
+Brainstormed and wrote a requirements document covering 9 requirements (R1-R9) for consolidating word and emoji data. Ran `ce-doc-review` on both the requirements doc and the implementation plan. Supersedes issue #266 with a comment linking it to #277. Implementation plan written with 9 TDD tasks, reviewed with 5 reviewer personas (5 findings resolved: 2 fixed, 2 skipped, 1 noted as known limitation). Also added "full worktree paths in output" rule to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and `.cursor/rules/git-workflow.mdc`.
+
+## Decisions made
+
+- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup** — single source of truth per word, no join at query time
+- **Both emoji and image fields** — `WordSpellRound` already supports both; curriculum should mirror that capability
+- **Auto-filter with `hasVisual: true`** — picture mode always filters to words with visuals, no teacher toggle
+- **No IndexedDB migration needed** — all new fields are optional, existing drafts/configs remain valid
+- **"mum" goes in level 2** — level 2 covers graphemes `m` and `u`; needs both `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` and `WordCore` in `core/level2.json`
+- **Zero-results fallback to recall mode** — if `hasVisual` filtering returns nothing, fall back to audio-only rather than blank screen
+- **Defer totalRounds silent truncation** — SRS for rounds is coming soon, making this moot
+
+## Spec / Plan
+
+- worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md
+- worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji.md
+
+## Key files
+
+- `src/data/words/types.ts` — `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`, `WordFilter` types need emoji/image fields (R1-R3, R7)
+- `src/data/words/filter.ts:173-189` — `joinHits` must propagate emoji/image from curriculum to `WordHit` (R2)
+- `src/data/words/filter.ts` — `draftToHit` must propagate emoji/image from `DraftEntry` (R2)
+- `src/data/words/adapters.ts` — `toWordSpellRound` must map emoji/image through (R4)
+- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level1.json` — add emoji to ant, pin (R5)
+- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json` — add emoji to can, cat, dog, sad, sun; add mum entry (R5, R6)
+- `src/data/words/core/level2.json` — add mum `WordCore` entry (R6)
+- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-92` — `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` to be removed (R9)
+- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:286-298` — picture mode invariant must be inverted to use `source` with `hasVisual: true` (R8)
+- `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` — already maps via `toWordSpellRound`, will pick up emoji/image automatically
+
+## Open questions / blockers
+
+- [x] "mum" IPA/graphemes: `/mʌm/` with graphemes `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `m/m` (resolved — follows pattern of "mug")
+- [x] Default picture-mode filter scope: Pull from all levels with visuals, `region: 'aus'` + `hasVisual: true` (resolved)
+
+## Next steps
+
+1. [ ] Review and merge PR #281 (requirements doc + plan)
+2. [ ] Execute the plan using TDD — choose subagent-driven or inline execution
+
+## Context to remember
+
+- `joinHits` joins curriculum entries against core entries by word — if a `WordCore` entry is missing, the curriculum entry is silently discarded. "mum" must be added to both `aus/level2.json` AND `core/level2.json`.
+- The mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` currently _deletes_ `source` for picture mode — it must be _inverted_ so picture mode becomes source-defined with `hasVisual: true`.
+- `WordSpell.tsx` already renders emoji/image from rounds correctly — no changes needed there.
+- Issue #266 was superseded by #277 — a comment was added to #266 linking to this work.
+- Doc review findings from the plan: sentence-gap hasVisual injection (skipped), totalRounds cap removal (skipped — deferred to SRS), fallback test fix (applied — use level 8), filterSignature mismatch on fallback (applied — `activeFilter` tracking), region hardcoded to aus (noted as known limitation).
diff --git a/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md b/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..5b319eef
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md
@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+---
+date: 2026-05-01
+topic: consolidate-word-emoji
+---
+
+# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data
+
+## Summary
+
+Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields directly into curriculum JSON entries so
+that any word in the library can carry a visual. Picture mode switches from a
+hardcoded 8-word pool to library-sourced rounds filtered by visual availability.
+
+---
+
+## Problem Frame
+
+WordSpell's picture mode shows an emoji or image alongside each word, but the
+visual data lives in a hardcoded array (`WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`) of 8 words in
+the route loader — completely disconnected from the curriculum word library. This
+means picture mode is locked to those 8 words regardless of what the teacher
+configures, and adding new picture-mode words requires a code change rather than
+a data edit.
+
+The word library already powers recall mode with rich filtering (region, level,
+graphemes, phonemes), but none of that infrastructure is available to picture
+mode because the visual data isn't part of the word data.
+
+---
+
+## Requirements
+
+**Data structure**
+
+- R1. `CurriculumEntry` gains optional `emoji?: string` and `image?: string`
+  fields. Both are optional — most words will have neither.
+- R2. `WordHit` gains matching optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields, populated
+  from the curriculum entry during filtering. Specifically, `joinHits` in
+  `filter.ts` must propagate `emoji` and `image` from `CurriculumEntry` into
+  the constructed `WordHit`, and `draftToHit` must propagate them from
+  `DraftEntry`.
+- R3. `DraftEntry` gains optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields for authoring
+  parity.
+- R4. The `toWordSpellRound` adapter maps `emoji` and `image` from `WordHit`
+  through to `WordSpellRound`.
+
+**Curriculum data**
+
+- R5. The 7 existing hardcoded emoji words that already have curriculum entries
+  (ant, can, cat, dog, pin, sad, sun) gain an `emoji` field in their respective
+  `aus/level1.json` or `aus/level2.json` curriculum entries.
+- R6. "mum" is added as a new `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` (level 2
+  covers graphemes `m` and `u`) with its emoji `🤱`. A corresponding `WordCore`
+  entry must also be added to `core/level2.json` (with `word` and
+  `syllableCount`) — without it, `joinHits` will silently discard the
+  curriculum entry.
+
+**Filtering**
+
+- R7. `WordFilter` gains a `hasVisual?: boolean` option. When `true`,
+  `filterWords` returns only words where `emoji` or `image` is set.
+
+**Picture mode behavior**
+
+- R8. When WordSpell is in picture mode, the config resolver uses
+  library-sourced rounds with `hasVisual: true` in the filter, instead of
+  falling back to the hardcoded pool. This requires inverting the existing mode
+  invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` (which currently deletes `source` for
+  picture mode) so that picture mode becomes `source` defined with
+  `hasVisual: true`. The existing test asserting source is dropped for picture
+  mode must be updated to assert the new behavior. If a saved config has
+  explicit `rounds` with emoji/image data, those are honored as-is (no library
+  fetch). If `hasVisual` filtering returns zero results, the game falls back to
+  recall mode (audio-only) rather than displaying a blank screen.
+- R9. The hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds` are
+  removed.
+
+---
+
+## Acceptance Examples
+
+- AE1. **Covers R4, R5, R8.** Given a WordSpell game in picture mode with
+  default config, when the game loads, it pulls rounds from `aus/level2.json`
+  (and `level1.json`) curriculum entries that have an `emoji` field set, and the
+  emoji is rendered via `EmojiQuestion`.
+- AE2. **Covers R7.** Given a `WordFilter` with `hasVisual: true` and
+  `region: 'aus'`, when `filterWords` runs, only entries with a non-empty
+  `emoji` or `image` field are returned.
+- AE3. **Covers R1, R2.** Given a curriculum entry without `emoji` or `image`,
+  when it appears in a `WordHit`, `emoji` and `image` are `undefined` and the
+  word works normally in recall mode.
+
+---
+
+## Success Criteria
+
+- Picture mode loads rounds from the word library instead of a hardcoded array.
+- Adding a new picture-mode word requires only a data edit (adding `emoji` or
+  `image` to a curriculum JSON entry), not a code change.
+- Existing recall mode and sentence-gap mode are unaffected.
+
+---
+
+## Scope Boundaries
+
+- Tagging additional words beyond the existing 8 with emojis
+- Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel
+- Emoji display in WordLibraryExplorer cards
+- Image upload or asset management
+- Multi-region emoji support (only `aus` curriculum entries are tagged in this
+  pass)
+
+---
+
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:38:31Z)**_

I see a potential issue. The mode detection at line 217 only checks for `'picture'` and defaults everything else to `'recall'`. But the comment at line 285 says `sentence-gap` is treated as picture for default fallback. Let me trace: if `saved.mode === 'sentence-gap'`, then `mode` at line 217 will be `'recall'`, and `base` will be `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG`. The merge at line 255 will override `mode` to `'sentence-gap'` from `saved`. Then line 277 checks `merged.mode === 'recall'` which is false. So it falls through to the picture/sentence-gap branch starting at line 287. But the `base` was `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (which has `source` but no `rounds`), not `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG`. Let me check what that means.

1	# Agent instructions (BaseSkill)
2	
3	This file is for **any** coding agent (Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, etc.). Prefer it as the first stop for **process**; implementation detail lives in [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md).
4	
5	## Git and worktrees (mandatory)
6	
7	- Do **not** edit the codebase from the main repo checkout unless the user explicitly asks to work in the root tree (e.g. “skip worktree”, “patch main directly”).
8	- **Before the first file change in a session**, confirm the workspace path is under `worktrees/<task-name>/`. If it is not, create a worktree from `origin/master` and continue there:
9	
10	  ```bash
11	  git fetch origin master
12	  git worktree add ./worktrees/<task-name> -b feat/<task-name> origin/master
13	  cd ./worktrees/<task-name>
14	  yarn install
15	  ```
16	
17	- Never commit to `master`. Branch → PR → merge.
18	- **Full paths in output:** When referencing files in messages, always print the **full path including the worktree directory** (e.g. `worktrees/my-branch/docs/plans/foo.md`), not just the repo-relative path. Relative paths are ambiguous across worktrees.
19	- Project rules also live in `.cursor/rules/` (see `git-workflow.mdc`); this file and those rules should agree.
20	
21	## Before writing a plan
22	
23	Plans are binding source of truth for executor agents. If a plan will create or modify any of the following, **invoke the corresponding project skill before prescribing code**, and let the skill override plan boilerplate:
24	
25	| Plan touches                                 | Required skill                                       |
26	| -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
27	| `*.stories.tsx`                              | `write-storybook`                                    |
28	| `tests-vr/**`, `tests-e2e/**`, `*.e2e.ts`    | `write-e2e-vr-tests`                                 |
29	| `*.md` authored by an agent                  | Markdown Authoring rules in [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) |
30	| Architecture docs (`*.mdx` under game state) | `update-architecture-docs`                           |
31	
32	If a plan's code sample conflicts with a skill, flag it as a **Spec Delta** at the top of the plan and justify the deviation — don't silently ship boilerplate that contradicts project convention. Executors must load the corresponding skill into context when the plan prescribes matching files; if the plan and skill disagree and there is no Spec Delta, treat the skill as authoritative and flag the conflict before coding.
33	
34	See [CLAUDE.md → Before writing a plan](./CLAUDE.md#before-writing-a-plan) for the canonical version.
35	
36	## Commits (baby steps)
37	
38	- After each **coherent** chunk of work (feature slice, bugfix + test, doc section, rule tweak), **commit** with a clear message. Do not wait until the end of a large task to make one giant commit.
39	- **Multiple commits in one PR are expected and preferred.** Reviewers can follow the story; you or the user can **cherry-pick** or **revert** a single commit without unpicking everything.
40	- If the user asks for “baby step” commits, treat that as the default for the rest of the branch.
41	
42	## Where to look next
43	
44	- [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) — React/ESLint expectations, TDD, markdown, CI buckets, VR tests.
45	- `.cursor/rules/` — Cursor-specific always-on rules.
46	- [.claude/skills/handoff/SKILL.md](./.claude/skills/handoff/SKILL.md) — session handoff workflow (also available as `/handoff` or `/continue` in Claude Code, mirrored as `.cursor/rules/handoff.mdc` for Cursor).
47
```
</details></tool-use>

+# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data Implementation Plan
+
+> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
+
+**Goal:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
+
+**Architecture:** Add `emoji?` and `image?` to the type chain (`CurriculumEntry` → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound`), add `hasVisual` filtering, tag 7 existing curriculum entries with emojis and add "mum" as the 8th entry, then invert the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` to use a library source with `hasVisual: true` instead of a hardcoded round pool. Remove the hardcoded pool entirely.
+
+**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Vitest, React (hook tests via `@testing-library/react`)
+
+**Spec:** `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`
+
+---
+
+## File Map
+
+| Action | File                                                          | Responsibility                                                                                         |
+| ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/types.ts`                                     | Add `emoji?`, `image?` to `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`; add `hasVisual?` to `WordFilter` |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:30-86`                              | Add `hasVisual` predicate to `entryMatches`                                                            |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:156-190`                            | Propagate `emoji`/`image` in `draftToHit` and `joinHits`                                               |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/adapters.ts:4-6`                              | Map `emoji`/`image` from `WordHit` through to `WordSpellRound`                                         |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/seen-words.ts:26-44`                          | Add `hasVisual` to `filterSignature`                                                                   |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level1.json`                   | Add `emoji` to `ant`, `pin` entries                                                                    |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json`                   | Add `emoji` to `can`, `cat`, `dog`, `sad`, `sun`; add `mum` entry                                      |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/core/level2.json`                             | Add `mum` `WordCore` entry                                                                             |
+| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-298`             | Invert picture-mode invariant, remove `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds`               |
+| Modify | `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts:65-87`              | Zero-results fallback when `hasVisual` returns nothing                                                 |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.test.ts`                               | Tests for `hasVisual` filtering and emoji propagation                                                  |
+| Modify | `src/data/words/adapters.test.ts`                             | Tests for emoji/image mapping                                                                          |
+| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts` | Update picture-mode tests                                                                              |
+| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/mode-default-invariant.test.ts` | Update picture-mode invariant assertions                                                               |
+| Modify | `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`              | Test hasVisual fallback and emoji propagation                                                          |
+
+---
+
+### Task 1: Add emoji/image fields to CurriculumEntry, WordHit, DraftEntry (R1, R2, R3)
+
+**Files:**
+
+- Modify: `src/data/words/types.ts:18-23` (CurriculumEntry)
+- Modify: `src/data/words/types.ts:29-42` (DraftEntry)
+- Modify: `src/data/words/types.ts:44-55` (WordHit)
+

283	  }
284	
285	  // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
286	  // picture with explicit rounds ⇒ honor them, drop source
287	  if (Array.isArray(merged.rounds) && merged.rounds.length > 0) {
288	    const { source: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
289	    return rest as WordSpellConfig;
290	  }
291	  // picture without explicit rounds ⇒ source with hasVisual, drop rounds
292	  const picture = { ...merged };
293	  if (picture.rounds) {
294	    delete picture.rounds;
295	  }
296	  picture.source = picture.source
297	    ? {
298	        ...picture.source,
299	        filter: { ...picture.source.filter, hasVisual: true },
300	      }
301	    : {
302	        type: 'word-library',
303	        filter: { region: 'aus', hasVisual: true },
304	      };
305	  return picture;
306	};

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/.claude/skills/handoff/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: handoff
3	description:
4	  Create a session handoff document so work can continue in a new session or
5	  with another agent. Use when just saved a plan or spec, just raised or
6	  merged a PR, ending a work session, context-switching, or explicitly told
7	  to hand off / continue later. Triggers on phrases like "hand this off",
8	  "continue in a new session", "pick this up later", "end of session",
9	  "wrap up".
10	---
11	
12	# Handoff
13	
14	Adapted from the [Cult of Claude handoff skill](https://cultofclaude.com/skills/handoff/) ([source](https://github.com/robertguss/claude-code-toolkit/blob/main/skills/handoff/SKILL.md)), tailored for BaseSkill's worktree + `/resync` + baby-step commit workflow.
15	
16	## When to use
17	
18	- Just saved a plan or spec (→ next session executes it)
19	- Just raised or merged a PR (→ next session starts follow-up work)
20	- Ending a work session or context-switching
21	- User says "hand this off", "continue in a new session", "pick this up later"
22	
23	Explicit invocation: `/handoff` or `/continue`.
24	
25	## Worktree rule
26	
27	Handoffs are written inside the **active worktree**, never on `master`. If the current working directory is not under `worktrees/<name>/`, stop and ask the user whether to create a worktree first (see [CLAUDE.md](../../../CLAUDE.md) worktree gate).
28	
29	## Process
30	
31	### Step 1 — Auto-detect context
32	
33	Run these commands and keep the output for the template:
34	
35	```bash
36	git branch --show-current
37	git worktree list | grep "$(pwd)"
38	git status --porcelain | wc -l
39	git rev-list --count @{u}..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0
40	git log -1 --pretty=format:"%h %s"
41	gh pr view --json number,state,url 2>/dev/null || true
42	```
43	
44	Also scan `docs/superpowers/specs/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/` for recently-modified files whose slug matches the current branch or task (optional — include only if clearly relevant).
45	
46	### Step 2 — Ask one question
47	
48	> "Anything specific you want captured? (key decisions, gotchas, things you'll forget)"
49	
50	Single prompt, no follow-up loop. The user can answer briefly or skip.
51	
52	### Step 3 — Generate the document
53	
54	Fill in this template. **Omit any section with no content** — keep the document scannable.
55	
56	````markdown
57	# Handoff: <brief description>
58	
59	**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
60	**Branch:** <branch-name>
61	**Worktree:** worktrees/<name>
62	**Worktree path:** <absolute path>
63	**Git status:** clean | N uncommitted | N unpushed commits ahead of origin
64	**Last commit:** <sha> <subject>
65	**PR:** #NNN — open|merged|closed (omit if none)
66	
67	## Resume command
68	
69	```
70	/resync
71	cd worktrees/<name>
72	<next action — e.g., execute plan docs/…md, open PR, resume debugging>
73	```
74	
75	## Current state
76	
77	**Task:** <what we're working on>
78	**Phase:** exploration | planning | implementation | debugging | review | shipped
79	**Progress:** <milestone or %>
80	
81	## What we did
82	
83	<2–3 sentences>
84	
85	## Decisions made
86	
87	- **<decision>** — <why>
88	
89	## Spec / Plan
90	
91	- docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md
92	- docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md
93	
94	## Key files
95	
96	- src/…/foo.ts:42 — <what and why>
97	
98	## Open questions / blockers
99	
100	- [ ] <question>
101	
102	## Next steps
103	
104	1. [ ] <first action>
105	2. [ ] <second>
106	
107	## Context to remember
108	
109	<constraints, user preferences, domain knowledge not obvious from the code>
110	````
111	
112	### Step 4 — Write the file
113	
114	Save to `.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<brief-slug>.md`. Slug is 2–5 lowercase words joined by hyphens, matching the branch topic.
115	
116	### Step 5 — Commit as a baby step
117	
118	```bash
119	git add .claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md
120	git commit -m "docs(handoff): <brief description>"
121	```
122	
123	### Step 6 — Print the resume hint
124	
125	Tell the user, using the **absolute path** (run `pwd` if needed and join with the handoff filename — never print the repo-relative form):
126	
127	> Saved handoff: `/absolute/path/to/.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` — paste that path in a new session to resume.
128	
129	## Quality check (before writing)
130	
131	Mental pass — no validation script:
132	
133	1. Could a fresh Claude (or Cursor) pick up from this?
134	2. Are decisions traceable (the "why", not just the "what")?
135	3. Are next steps actionable and specific?
136	4. Are file paths anchored (`foo.ts:42`, not "that function")?
137	
138	If any answer is "no", tighten the relevant section before saving.
139	
140	## What to capture
141	
142	### Always
143	
144	- Decisions with reasoning — the "why"
145	- File paths touched, with intent
146	- Current progress — where we stopped
147	- Actionable next steps
148	- User context — constraints, preferences, domain knowledge
149	
150	### When relevant
151	
152	- Errors hit and how they resolved (or didn't)
153	- Dead ends — approaches tried that didn't work (saves re-exploration)
154	
155	### Skip
156	
157	- Verbose tool output
158	- Intermediate reasoning that reached conclusions
159	- Information obvious from the code
160
```
</details></tool-use>

125	
126	/**
127	 * Deterministic default round values for NumberMatch. Using a fixed sequence
128	 * (rather than `Math.random`) keeps VR baselines stable across runs — nanoid
129	 * seeds in the route loader bypass any Math.random pin, so the only reliable
130	 * fix is to remove randomness from the default config entirely. Users still
131	 * customise via the settings panel.
132	 */
133	const DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES: readonly number[] = [5, 8, 3];
134	
135	const makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig = (): NumberMatchConfig => ({
136	  gameId: 'number-match',
137	  component: 'NumberMatch',
138	  inputMethod: 'drag',
139	  wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
140	  tileBankMode: 'distractors',
141	  distractorCount: 2,
142	  totalRounds: 3,
143	  // Deterministic order by default — keeps VR baselines stable (see note above).
144	  roundsInOrder: true,
145	  ttsEnabled: true,
146	  mode: 'numeral-to-group',
147	  tileStyle: 'dots',
148	  range: { min: 1, max: 12 },
149	  rounds: DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES.map((value) => ({ value })),
150	});
151	
152	const makeDefaultSpotAllConfig = (): SpotAllConfig =>
153	  resolveSpotAllSimpleConfig({
154	    configMode: 'simple',
155	    selectedConfusablePairs: [
156	      { pair: ['b', 'd'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
157	      { pair: ['p', 'q'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
158	    ],
159	    selectedReversibleChars: [],
160	  });
161	
162	export const resizeNumberMatchRounds = (
163	  prev: NumberMatchRound[],
164	  count: number,
165	  range: { min: number; max: number },
166	): NumberMatchRound[] => {
167	  const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, 50));
168	  const span = Math.max(1, range.max - range.min + 1);
169	  // Deterministic fill: cycle through the range starting at `range.min`. This
170	  // avoids `Math.random` so defaults stay stable (see note above) and is
171	  // simple enough for users to understand at a glance. They can override any
172	  // round value via the settings panel.
173	  const fillValueAt = (index: number): number =>
174	    range.min + (index % span);
175	
176	  // Detect a collapsed state: every round has the same value even though the
177	  // range allows variety. This happens when the user narrows the range down
178	  // to a single value (collapsing every round to that value) and then widens
179	  // it again — without this check the preserved values stay stuck at the
180	  // collapse point and the player sees the same number every round. Also
181	  // handles the empty-prev case from a legacy saved config.
182	  const firstValue = prev[0]?.value;
183	  const allCollapsed =
184	    prev.length >= 2 &&
185	    span > 1 &&
186	    prev.every((r) => r.value === firstValue);
187	  if (prev.length === 0 || allCollapsed) {
188	    return Array.from({ length: n }, (_, i) => ({
189	      value: fillValueAt(i),
190	    }));
191	  }
192	
193	  const next = prev.slice(0, n).map((r, i) => {
194	    if (r.value >= range.min && r.value <= range.max) return { ...r };
195	    return { value: fillValueAt(i) };
196	  });
197	  while (next.length < n) {
198	    next.push({ value: fillValueAt(next.length) });
199	  }
200	  return next;
201	};
202	
203	const makeDefaultSortNumbersConfig = (): SortNumbersConfig =>
204	  resolveSimpleConfig({
205	    configMode: 'simple',
206	    direction: 'ascending',
207	    start: 2,
208	    step: 2,
209	    quantity: 5,
210	    distractors: false,
211	  });
212	
213	export const resolveWordSpellConfig = (
214	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
215	): WordSpellConfig => {
216	  const mode =
217	    (saved as { mode?: unknown } | null)?.mode === 'picture'
218	      ? 'picture'
219	      : 'recall';
220	  const base =
221	    mode === 'picture' ? DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG : DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG;
222	  const baseClone: WordSpellConfig = {
223	    ...base,
224	    rounds: base.rounds
225	      ? base.rounds.map((r) => ({ ...r }))
226	      : undefined,
227	    source: base.source
228	      ? { ...base.source, filter: { ...base.source.filter } }
229	      : undefined,
230	  };
231	
232	  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'WordSpell') return baseClone;
233	
234	  // Simple-mode: delegate to word-spell's library-source resolver.
235	  // Any config with selectedUnits (but not explicitly advanced) was produced
236	  // by the picker and must be re-resolved so source.filter stays in sync.
237	  if (
238	    saved.configMode === 'simple' ||
239	    (saved.configMode !== 'advanced' &&
240	      Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits))
241	  ) {
242	    const savedInput = saved.inputMethod;
243	    return resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
244	      configMode: 'simple',
245	      ...saved,
246	      inputMethod:
247	        savedInput === 'type' || savedInput === 'both'
248	          ? savedInput
249	          : 'drag',
250	    } as Parameters<typeof resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig>[0]);
251	  }
252	
253	  // Advanced merge — start from mode-appropriate base, then layer saved
254	  // fields, then enforce the mode invariant.
255	  const merged: WordSpellConfig = {
256	    ...baseClone,
257	    ...(saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>),
258	    gameId: 'word-spell',
259	    component: 'WordSpell',
260	  };
261	
262	  // When the picker set selectedUnits, re-derive source.filter so
263	  // the filter stays in sync even in advanced mode.
264	  if (Array.isArray(merged.selectedUnits) && merged.source) {
265	    const derived = resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
266	      configMode: 'simple',
267	      selectedUnits: merged.selectedUnits,
268	      region: merged.source.filter.region,
269	      inputMethod: merged.inputMethod,
270	    });
271	    merged.source = derived.source;
272	  }
273	
274	  // Mode invariant:
275	  // recall  ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined
276	  // picture ⇒ explicit rounds ⇒ honor rounds (drop source); else source with hasVisual ∧ rounds undefined
277	  if (merged.mode === 'recall') {
278	    if (merged.rounds) {
279	      const { rounds: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
280	      return rest as WordSpellConfig;
281	    }
282	    return merged;
283	  }
284	
285	  // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
286	  // picture with explicit rounds ⇒ honor them, drop source
287	  if (Array.isArray(merged.rounds) && merged.rounds.length > 0) {
288	    const { source: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
289	    return rest as WordSpellConfig;
290	  }
291	  // picture without explicit rounds ⇒ source with hasVisual, drop rounds
292	  const picture = { ...merged };
293	  if (picture.rounds) {
294	    delete picture.rounds;
295	  }
296	  picture.source = picture.source
297	    ? {
298	        ...picture.source,
299	        filter: { ...picture.source.filter, hasVisual: true },
300	      }
301	    : {
302	        type: 'word-library',
303	        filter: { region: 'aus', hasVisual: true },
304	      };
305	  return picture;
306	};
307	
308	const resolveNumberMatchConfig = (
309	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
310	): NumberMatchConfig => {
311	  const base = makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig();
312	  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'NumberMatch') return base;
313	  const merged: NumberMatchConfig = {
314	    ...base,
315	    ...(saved as Partial<NumberMatchConfig>),
316	    gameId: 'number-match',
317	    component: 'NumberMatch',
318	  };
319	  const targetLen =
320	    merged.totalRounds > 0 ? merged.totalRounds : base.totalRounds;
321	  merged.rounds = resizeNumberMatchRounds(
322	    Array.isArray(merged.rounds) ? merged.rounds : [],
323	    targetLen,
324	    merged.range,
325	  );
326	  return merged;
327	};
328	
329	const resolveSpotAllConfig = (
330	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
331	): SpotAllConfig => {
332	  const fallback = makeDefaultSpotAllConfig();
333	  if (!saved || typeof saved !== 'object') return fallback;
334	
335	  const merged = { ...fallback, ...saved } as SpotAllConfig;
336	  if (
337	    (!Array.isArray(merged.selectedConfusablePairs) ||
338	      merged.selectedConfusablePairs.length === 0) &&
339	    (!Array.isArray(merged.selectedReversibleChars) ||
340	      merged.selectedReversibleChars.length === 0)
341	  ) {
342	    return fallback;
343	  }
344	  return merged;
345	};
346	
347	const resolveSortNumbersConfig = (
348	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
349	): SortNumbersConfig => {
350	  const base = makeDefaultSortNumbersConfig();
351	  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'SortNumbers') return base;
352	
353	  // Simple mode: resolve from 5 fields
354	  if (saved.configMode === 'simple') {
355	    const skip = saved.skip as
356	      | { step?: number; start?: number }
357	      | undefined;
358	    return resolveSimpleConfig({
359	      configMode: 'simple',
360	      direction:
361	        saved.direction === 'descending' ? 'descending' : 'ascending',
362	      start: typeof skip?.start === 'number' ? skip.start : 2,
363	      step: typeof skip?.step === 'number' ? skip.step : 2,
364	      quantity: typeof saved.quantity === 'number' ? saved.quantity : 5,
365	      distractors: saved.tileBankMode === 'distractors',
366	    });
367	  }
368	
369	  // Advanced mode (existing logic)
370	  // Migrate legacy allowSkips: boolean → skip: SkipConfig
371	  let migratedSaved = saved;
372	  if (!saved.skip && typeof saved.allowSkips === 'boolean') {
373	    migratedSaved = {
374	      ...saved,
375	      skip: saved.allowSkips
376	        ? { mode: 'random' }
377	        : { mode: 'consecutive' },
378	    };
379	  }
380	
381	  const merged: SortNumbersConfig = {
382	    ...base,
383	    ...(migratedSaved as Partial<SortNumbersConfig>),
384	    gameId: 'sort-numbers',
385	    component: 'SortNumbers',
386	  };
387	
388	  // Existing saved configs without configMode are treated as advanced
389	  if (saved.configMode !== 'simple') {
390	    merged.configMode = 'advanced';
391	  }
392	
393	  // Advanced mode always runs as level mode with a single seeded round; the
394	  // generator produces a fresh round per level using the configured rules.
395	  merged.totalRounds = 1;
396	
397	  // Normalize skip: mode 'by' requires step and start.
398	  if (merged.skip.mode === 'by') {
399	    const partial = merged.skip as {
400	      mode: 'by';
401	      step?: number;
402	      start?: 'range-min' | 'random' | number;
403	    };
404	    merged.skip = {
405	      mode: 'by',
406	      step: typeof partial.step === 'number' ? partial.step : 2,
407	      start: partial.start ?? 'range-min',
408	    };
409	  }
410	
411	  const roundsStale =
412	    Array.isArray(merged.rounds) &&
413	    isRoundsStale(merged.rounds, {
414	      quantity: merged.quantity,
415	      range: merged.range,
416	      skip: merged.skip,
417	    });
418	
419	  if (
420	    !Array.isArray(merged.rounds) ||
421	    merged.rounds.length !== 1 ||
422	    roundsStale
423	  ) {
424	    merged.rounds = generateSortRounds({
425	      range: merged.range,
426	      quantity: merged.quantity,
427	      skip: merged.skip,
428	      totalRounds: 1,
429	    });
430	  }
431	
432	  merged.levelMode = {
433	    generateNextLevel: createAdvancedLevelGenerator({
434	      range: merged.range,
435	      quantity: merged.quantity,
436	      skip: merged.skip,
437	      direction: merged.direction,
438	      tileBankMode: merged.tileBankMode,
439	      distractors: merged.distractors,
440	    }),
441	  };
442	
443	  return merged;
444	};
445	
446	const WordSpellGameBody = ({
447	  gameId,
448	  sessionId,
449	  seed,
450	  draftState,
451	  gameSpecificConfig,
452	  customGameId,
453	  customGameName,
454	  customGameColor,
455	  customGameCover,
456	  persistedContent,
457	  debug,
458	}: {
459	  gameId: string;
460	  sessionId: string;
461	  seed: string;
462	  draftState: AnswerGameDraftState | null;
463	  gameSpecificConfig: Record<string, unknown> | null;
464	  customGameId: string | null;
465	  customGameName: string | null;
466	  customGameColor: string | null;
467	  customGameCover: Cover | null;
468	  persistedContent: Record<string, unknown> | null;
469	  debug: boolean;
470	}): JSX.Element => {
471	  const { t } = useTranslation('games');
472	  const {
473	    save,
474	    update,
475	    remove,
476	    customGames,
477	    persistLastSessionConfig,
478	  } = useCustomGames();
479	  const { isBookmarked, toggle } = useBookmarks();
480	  const bookmarkTarget = customGameId
481	    ? ({ targetType: 'customGame', targetId: customGameId } as const)
482	    : ({ targetType: 'game', targetId: gameId } as const);
483	  const navigate = useNavigate({ from: '/$locale/game/$gameId' });
484	  const existingCustomGameNames = useMemo(
485	    () =>
486	      customGames.filter((d) => d.gameId === gameId).map((d) => d.name),
487	    [customGames, gameId],
488	  );
489	  const initial = useMemo(
490	    () => resolveWordSpellConfig(gameSpecificConfig),
491	    [gameSpecificConfig],
492	  );
493	  const [cfg, setCfg] = useState(initial);
494	  const [showInstructions, setShowInstructions] = useState(
495	    draftState === null,
496	  );
497	  useEffect(() => {
498	    setCfg(initial);
499	  }, [initial]);
500	
501	  const debugPanel = debug ? (
502	    <DebugPanel
503	      gameId={gameId}
504	      resolvedConfig={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
505	      rawSavedConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
506	      customGame={{
507	        id: customGameId,
508	        name: customGameName,
509	        color: customGameColor,
510	        cover: customGameCover,
511	      }}
512	      session={{
513	        sessionId,
514	        seed,
515	        draftState,
516	        persistedContent,
517	      }}
518	      rounds={cfg.rounds as unknown[]}
519	    />
520	  ) : null;
521	
522	  if (showInstructions) {
523	    return (
524	      <>
525	        <InstructionsOverlay
526	          text={t('instructions.word-spell')}
527	          onStart={() => setShowInstructions(false)}
528	          ttsEnabled={cfg.ttsEnabled}
529	          gameTitle={t('word-spell')}
530	          gameId={gameId}
531	          cover={customGameCover ?? undefined}
532	          customGameId={customGameId ?? undefined}
533	          customGameName={customGameName ?? undefined}
534	          customGameColor={
535	            (customGameColor ?? undefined) as GameColorKey | undefined
536	          }
537	          config={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
538	          onConfigChange={(c) => setCfg(resolveWordSpellConfig(c))}
539	          onSaveCustomGame={async ({ name, color, config, cover }) =>
540	            save({
541	              gameId,
542	              name,
543	              color,
544	              config,
545	              cover,
546	            })
547	          }
548	          onUpdateCustomGame={
549	            customGameId
550	              ? async (name, config, extras) => {
551	                  await update(customGameId, config, name, extras);
552	                }
553	              : undefined
554	          }
555	          onDeleteCustomGame={
556	            customGameId
557	              ? async (id) => {
558	                  await remove(id);
559	                  await navigate({
560	                    search: (prev) => ({
561	                      ...prev,
562	                      configId: undefined,
563	                    }),
564	                  });
565	                }
566	              : undefined
567	          }
568	          existingCustomGameNames={existingCustomGameNames}
569	          isBookmarked={isBookmarked(bookmarkTarget)}
570	          onToggleBookmark={() => void toggle(bookmarkTarget)}
571	          onPersistLastSession={(c) =>
572	            void persistLastSessionConfig(gameId, c)
573	          }
574	        />
575	        {debugPanel}
576	      </>
577	    );
578	  }
579	
580	  return (
581	    <>
582	      <WordSpell
583	        key={cfg.inputMethod}
584	        config={cfg}
585	        initialState={draftState ?? undefined}
586	        sessionId={sessionId}
587	        seed={seed}
588	        persistedContent={persistedContent}
589	      />
590	      {debugPanel}
591	    </>
592	  );
593	};

595	const NumberMatchGameBody = ({
596	  gameId,
597	  sessionId,
598	  seed,
599	  draftState,
600	  gameSpecificConfig,
601	  customGameId,
602	  customGameName,
603	  customGameColor,
604	  customGameCover,
605	  debug,
606	}: {
607	  gameId: string;
608	  sessionId: string;
609	  seed: string;
610	  draftState: AnswerGameDraftState | null;
611	  gameSpecificConfig: Record<string, unknown> | null;
612	  customGameId: string | null;
613	  customGameName: string | null;
614	  customGameColor: string | null;
615	  customGameCover: Cover | null;
616	  debug: boolean;

618	  const { t } = useTranslation('games');
619	  const {
620	    save,
621	    update,
622	    remove,
623	    customGames,
624	    persistLastSessionConfig,
625	  } = useCustomGames();
626	  const { isBookmarked, toggle } = useBookmarks();

628	    ? ({ targetType: 'customGame', targetId: customGameId } as const)

631	  const existingCustomGameNames = useMemo(
632	    () =>
633	      customGames.filter((d) => d.gameId === gameId).map((d) => d.name),
634	    [customGames, gameId],
635	  );
636	  const initial = useMemo(
637	    () => resolveNumberMatchConfig(gameSpecificConfig),
638	    [gameSpecificConfig],
639	  );
640	  const [cfg, setCfg] = useState(initial);
641	  const [showInstructions, setShowInstructions] = useState(
642	    draftState === null,
643	  );
644	  useEffect(() => {
645	    setCfg(initial);
646	  }, [initial]);
647	
648	  const debugPanel = debug ? (
649	    <DebugPanel
650	      gameId={gameId}
651	      resolvedConfig={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
652	      rawSavedConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
653	      customGame={{
654	        id: customGameId,
655	        name: customGameName,
656	        color: customGameColor,
657	        cover: customGameCover,
658	      }}
659	      session={{
660	        sessionId,
661	        seed,
662	        draftState,
663	        persistedContent: null,
664	      }}
665	      rounds={cfg.rounds as unknown[]}
666	    />
667	  ) : null;
668	
669	  if (showInstructions) {
670	    return (
671	      <>
672	        <InstructionsOverlay
673	          text={t('instructions.number-match')}
674	          onStart={() => setShowInstructions(false)}
675	          ttsEnabled={cfg.ttsEnabled}
676	          gameTitle={t('number-match')}
677	          gameId={gameId}
678	          cover={customGameCover ?? undefined}
679	          customGameId={customGameId ?? undefined}
680	          customGameName={customGameName ?? undefined}
681	          customGameColor={
682	            (customGameColor ?? undefined) as GameColorKey | undefined
683	          }
684	          config={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
685	          onConfigChange={(c) => setCfg(resolveNumberMatchConfig(c))}
686	          onSaveCustomGame={async ({ name, color, config, cover }) =>
687	            save({
688	              gameId,
689	              name,
690	              color,
691	              config,
692	              cover,
693	            })
694	          }
695	          onUpdateCustomGame={
696	            customGameId
697	              ? async (name, config, extras) => {
698	                  await update(customGameId, config, name, extras);
699	                }
700	              : undefined
701	          }
702	          onDeleteCustomGame={
703	            customGameId
704	              ? async (id) => {
705	                  await remove(id);
706	                  await navigate({
707	                    search: (prev) => ({
708	                      ...prev,
709	                      configId: undefined,
710	                    }),
711	                  });
712	                }
713	              : undefined
714	          }
715	          existingCustomGameNames={existingCustomGameNames}
716	          isBookmarked={isBookmarked(bookmarkTarget)}
717	          onToggleBookmark={() => void toggle(bookmarkTarget)}
718	          onPersistLastSession={(c) =>
719	            void persistLastSessionConfig(gameId, c)
720	          }
721	        />
722	        {debugPanel}
723	      </>
724	    );
725	  }
726	
727	  return (
728	    <>
729	      <NumberMatch
730	        key={cfg.inputMethod}
731	        config={cfg}
732	        initialState={draftState ?? undefined}
733	        sessionId={sessionId}
734	        seed={seed}
735	      />
736	      {debugPanel}
737	    </>
738	  );
739	};

825	          customGameId={customGameId ?? undefined}
826	          customGameName={customGameName ?? undefined}
827	          customGameColor={

829	          }
830	          config={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
831	          onConfigChange={(config) =>
832	            setCfg(resolveSpotAllConfig(config))
833	          }
834	          onSaveCustomGame={async ({ name, color, config, cover }) =>
835	            save({
836	              gameId,
837	              name,
838	              color,
839	              config,
840	              cover,
841	            })
842	          }

847	                }

854	                  await navigate({

856	                      ...prev,

858	                    }),
859	                  });
860	                }

870	        {debugPanel}
871	      </>
872	    );
873	  }
874	
875	  return (

879	    </>
880	  );

1029	const GameBody = ({
1030	  gameId,
1031	  sessionId,
1032	  seed,
1033	  draftState,
1034	  gameSpecificConfig,
1035	  customGameId,
1036	  customGameName,
1037	  customGameColor,
1038	  customGameCover,
1039	  persistedContent,
1040	  debug,
1041	}: {
1042	  gameId: string;
1043	  sessionId: string;
1044	  seed: string;
1045	  draftState: AnswerGameDraftState | null;
1046	  gameSpecificConfig: Record<string, unknown> | null;
1047	  customGameId: string | null;
1048	  customGameName: string | null;
1049	  customGameColor: string | null;
1050	  customGameCover: Cover | null;
1051	  persistedContent: Record<string, unknown> | null;
1052	  debug: boolean;
1053	}): JSX.Element => {
1054	  if (gameId === 'sort-numbers') {
1055	    return (
1056	      <SortNumbersGameBody
1057	        gameId={gameId}
1058	        sessionId={sessionId}
1059	        seed={seed}
1060	        draftState={draftState}
1061	        gameSpecificConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
1062	        customGameId={customGameId}
1063	        customGameName={customGameName}
1064	        customGameColor={customGameColor}
1065	        customGameCover={customGameCover}
1066	        debug={debug}
1067	      />
1068	    );
1069	  }
1070	
1071	  if (gameId === 'word-spell') {
1072	    return (
1073	      <WordSpellGameBody
1074	        gameId={gameId}
1075	        sessionId={sessionId}
1076	        seed={seed}
1077	        draftState={draftState}
1078	        gameSpecificConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
1079	        customGameId={customGameId}
1080	        customGameName={customGameName}
1081	        customGameColor={customGameColor}
1082	        customGameCover={customGameCover}
1083	        persistedContent={persistedContent}
1084	        debug={debug}
1085	      />
1086	    );
1087	  }
1088	
1089	  if (gameId === 'number-match') {
1090	    return (
1091	      <NumberMatchGameBody
1092	        gameId={gameId}
1093	        sessionId={sessionId}
1094	        seed={seed}
1095	        draftState={draftState}
1096	        gameSpecificConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
1097	        customGameId={customGameId}
1098	        customGameName={customGameName}
1099	        customGameColor={customGameColor}
1100	        customGameCover={customGameCover}
1101	        debug={debug}
1102	      />
1103	    );
1104	  }
1105	
1106	  if (gameId === 'spot-all') {
1107	    return (
1108	      <SpotAllGameBody
1109	        gameId={gameId}
1110	        sessionId={sessionId}
1111	        seed={seed}
1112	        draftState={draftState}
1113	        gameSpecificConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
1114	        customGameId={customGameId}
1115	        customGameName={customGameName}
1116	        customGameColor={customGameColor}
1117	        customGameCover={customGameCover}
1118	        debug={debug}
1119	      />
1120	    );
1121	  }
1122	
1123	  return (
1124	    <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-muted-foreground">
1125	      <p>Game not found</p>
1126	    </div>
1127	  );
1128	};
1129	
1130	export const GameRoute = ({
1131	  config,
1132	  initialLog,
1133	  draftState,
1134	  sessionId,
1135	  seed,
1136	  meta,
1137	  gameSpecificConfig,
1138	  customGameId,
1139	  customGameName,
1140	  customGameColor,
1141	  customGameCover,
1142	  persistedContent,
1143	  debug = false,
1144	}: GameRouteLoaderData & { debug?: boolean }): JSX.Element => (
1145	  <GameShell
1146	    config={config}
1147	    moves={{}}
1148	    initialState={meta.initialState}
1149	    sessionId={sessionId}
1150	    meta={meta}
1151	    initialLog={initialLog ?? undefined}
1152	  >
1153	    <GameBody
1154	      gameId={config.gameId}
1155	      sessionId={sessionId}
1156	      seed={seed}
1157	      draftState={draftState}
1158	      gameSpecificConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
1159	      customGameId={customGameId}
1160	      customGameName={customGameName}
1161	      customGameColor={customGameColor}
1162	      customGameCover={customGameCover}
1163	      persistedContent={persistedContent}
1164	      debug={debug}
1165	    />
1166	  </GameShell>
1167	);
1168	
1169	const RouteComponent = (): JSX.Element => {
1170	  const data = Route.useLoaderData();
1171	  const search = Route.useSearch();
1172	  const debug = search.debug === true || import.meta.env.DEV;
1173	  return <GameRoute {...data} debug={debug} />;
1174	};
1175	
1176	export const Route = createFileRoute('/$locale/_app/game/$gameId')({
1177	  validateSearch: (
1178	    search: Record<string, unknown>,
1179	  ): { configId: string | undefined; debug?: true } => {
1180	    const debug =
1181	      search.debug === '1' ||
1182	      search.debug === 'true' ||
1183	      search.debug === true;
1184	    return {
1185	      configId:
1186	        typeof search.configId === 'string'
1187	          ? search.configId
1188	          : undefined,
1189	      ...(debug ? { debug: true as const } : {}),
1190	    };
1191	  },
1192	  loaderDeps: ({ search }) => ({ configId: search.configId }),
1193	  loader: async ({ params, deps }): Promise<GameRouteLoaderData> => {
1194	    const { gameId } = params;
1195	    const profileId = 'default';
1196	    const defaultConfig = makeDefaultConfig(gameId);
1197	
1198	    // Route loaders run server-side (SSR) where IndexedDB is unavailable.
1199	    // Return minimal defaults so the client hydrates and re-runs on the client.
1200	    if (typeof indexedDB === 'undefined') {
1201	      const sessionId = nanoid();
1202	      const seed = nanoid();
1203	      const initialState: GameEngineState = {
1204	        phase: 'instructions',
1205	        roundIndex: 0,
1206	        score: 0,
1207	        streak: 0,
1208	        retryCount: 0,
1209	        content: STUB_CONTENT,
1210	        currentRound: {
1211	          roundId: STUB_CONTENT.rounds[0]?.id ?? '',
1212	          answer: null,
1213	          hintsUsed: 0,
1214	        },
1215	      };
1216	      return {
1217	        config: defaultConfig,
1218	        initialLog: null,
1219	        draftState: null,
1220	        sessionId,
1221	        seed,
1222	        meta: {
1223	          profileId,
1224	          gameId,
1225	          gradeBand: defaultConfig.gradeBand,
1226	          seed,
1227	          initialContent: STUB_CONTENT,
1228	          initialState,
1229	        },
1230	        gameSpecificConfig: null,
1231	        customGameId: null,
1232	        customGameName: null,
1233	        customGameColor: null,
1234	        customGameCover: null,
1235	        persistedContent: null,
1236	      };
1237	    }
1238	
1239	    const db = await getOrCreateDatabase();
1240	    const config = await loadGameConfig(
1241	      gameId,
1242	      profileId,
1243	      defaultConfig.gradeBand,
1244	      db,
1245	      defaultConfig,
1246	    );
1247

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:38:39Z)**_

So for `sentence-gap` mode: `base = DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG`, then merged gets `mode: 'sentence-gap'` from saved. The merge would have `source` from DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG (unless saved also has source). At line 287, if no explicit rounds, it falls through to lines 292-304 where it patches `picture.source` with `hasVisual: true`. This means sentence-gap gets `hasVisual: true` injected into its filter. Let me check if that was already the behavior before this PR.
