<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-phonics-word-library-design.md`

```text
1	# Phonics Word Library — Design
2	
3	> **Status:** Draft v2 · **Owner:** @leocaseiro · **Region focus:** AUS → UK → US (BR shape-ready) · **Target games:** WordSpell, future word games
4	> **Scope:** This spec covers **P1** only — the read-only static library, builder functions, ~650-word seed corpus, filter API, and WordSpell integration. P2 (dev authoring form) and P3 (runtime user word bags) are separate projects with their own specs.
5	
6	## 1. Goal
7	
8	Today WordSpell rounds are hand-authored per config. A teacher who wants "words the child can read using the graphemes they already know" has no way to express that — they'd have to curate the list themselves.
9	
10	This spec introduces a **phonics-aware word library** that lets a teacher (or future UI) filter the shared corpus by region, level, grapheme subset, phoneme subset, and syllable count, and have WordSpell generate rounds from the result. The library is seeded from an Australian 8-level phonics progression (~700 raw words, ~650 after removing proper nouns) and is the foundation other word games (Word Builder, Read Aloud) will share later.
11	
12	## 2. Project decomposition
13	
14	This feature breaks into three independent sub-projects. Each gets its own spec→plan→implementation cycle.
15	
16	| Project                               | Scope                                                                                                                | Depends on            |
17	| ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
18	| **P1 — Static library** _(this spec)_ | Types, chunk layout, builder functions, ~650-word seed corpus, filter API, WordSpell integration                     | Nothing               |
19	| **P2 — Dev authoring form**           | Storybook form + Vite middleware that reads/writes the P1 JSON chunk files; add/update/delete with confirmation      | P1's types + builders |
20	| **P3 — Runtime user word bags**       | IndexedDB-backed user-created word bags, CRUD UI, export/import, new `WordSpellConfig.source = { type: 'word-bag' }` | P1's types            |
21	
22	Build order is P1 → P2 → P3.
23	
24	## 3. Teaching model
25	
26	Words are the primary entity. Each region has its own teaching progression, stored as a list of numbered **levels**.
27	
28	"Level" is a neutral integer — the number only has meaning within a region's curriculum. UK pedagogy calls them "phases"; AUS calls them "levels" or "stages"; US calls them "units" within a grade. Storage uses the integer; display strings come from a per-region label table:
29	
30	```ts
31	// src/data/words/levels.ts
32	export const LEVEL_LABELS: Record<Region, (level: number) => string> = {
33	  aus: (n) => `Level ${n}`,
34	  uk: (n) => `Phase ${n}`,
35	  us: (n) => `K Unit ${n}`,
36	  br: (n) => `Unidade ${n}`,
37	};
38	```
39	
40	Four regions are modelled: `aus`, `uk`, `us`, `br`. AUS is seeded with all 8 levels from the source word list. UK/US start empty and get filled incrementally via P2. BR is shape-ready but unseeded.
41	
42	Proprietary programmes (Jolly Phonics, InitiaLit, Reading Wonders, Fountas & Pinnell) are not copied. The AUS levels are taken from a public synthetic-phonics progression; UK/US levels (when seeded) come from public references (UK Letters and Sounds DfE 2007, US CKLA / Core Knowledge) only.
43	
44	## 4. Data model — normalized split
45	
46	Two tables, joined on `word`. One captures invariant facts; the other captures per-region teaching facts.
47	
48	```ts
49	// src/data/words/types.ts
50	
51	export type Region = 'aus' | 'uk' | 'us' | 'br';
52	
53	/** Invariant per spelling. Lives in src/data/words/core/. */
54	export interface WordCore {
55	  word: string;
56	  syllableCount: number;
57	  /** When present: syllables.join('') === word. */
58	  syllables?: string[];
59	  /** Cross-spelling links (colour ↔ color, mum ↔ mom). Just the word strings. */
60	  variants?: string[];
61	}
62	
63	export interface Grapheme {
64	  /** 'sh', 'ch', 'a_e', 'igh'. */
65	  g: string;
66	  /** IPA phoneme. Silent letters store ''. */
67	  p: string;
68	  /** For split digraphs only. Letter positions in the word. */
69	  span?: [number, number];
70	}
71	
72	/** Per-region teaching facts. Lives in src/data/words/curriculum/<region>/. */
73	export interface CurriculumEntry {
74	  /** Foreign key into WordCore. */
75	  word: string;
76	  /** Ordered position within this region's progression. */
77	  level: number;
78	  /** Full-word IPA. Flat string — region is implicit from the file location. */
79	  ipa: string;
80	  /** graphemes.map(g => g.g.replace('_', '')).join('') === word. */
81	  graphemes: Grapheme[];
82	}
83	
84	/** The shape filterWords() returns — already joined. */
85	export interface WordHit {
86	  word: string;
87	  region: Region;
88	  level: number;
89	  syllableCount: number;
90	  syllables?: string[];
91	  variants?: string[];
92	  /** Present only for Tier 2-enriched entries. */
93	  ipa?: string;
94	  graphemes?: Grapheme[];
95	}
96	```
97	
98	### 4.1 Two tiers of enrichment
99	
100	| Tier       | Fields                                                               | Authoring cost                    | Filter support                                                                      |
101	| ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
102	| **Tier 1** | `WordCore` (word, syllableCount, variants) + `CurriculumEntry.level` | Codegen from source list          | `level`, `levels`, `syllableCount*`                                                 |
103	| **Tier 2** | `CurriculumEntry.ipa` + `graphemes[]`                                | Codegen best-effort + hand review | Adds `graphemesAllowed`, `graphemesRequired`, `phonemesAllowed`, `phonemesRequired` |
104	
105	Tier 1 is populated for **all** seeded words (all ~650). Tier 2 is populated by codegen for words whose grapheme split is unambiguous given the level's grapheme set (expected ~90% of levels 1–4, ~60% of levels 5–8) and hand-reviewed thereafter. Words missing Tier 2 fields are **excluded** from Tier 2 filters — never silently accepted.
106	
107	### 4.2 Region handling
108	
109	**Case A — same spelling, different sound** (`car`, `tomato`, `bath`):
110	One `WordCore`. Multiple `CurriculumEntry` rows, one per region, each with its own `ipa` and `graphemes[]`. No nested per-region map inside entries.
111	
112	**Case B — different spelling** (`colour`/`color`, `aluminium`/`aluminum`):
113	Two separate `WordCore` rows linked by bidirectional `variants[]`. Each appears only in the curriculum files of the regions that use that spelling. `colour` is in `curriculum/aus/` and `curriculum/uk/` but never in `curriculum/us/`; `color` is the opposite.
114	
115	### 4.3 Grapheme-phoneme authoring rules
116	
117	- Digraphs (`ch`, `sh`, `th`, `ng`, `qu`) are a **single** grapheme.
118	- Trigraphs (`igh`, `air`, `ear`) are a single grapheme.
119	- Split digraphs (`a_e` in `cake`, `i_e` in `kite`) use underscore notation and store `span: [startIndex, endIndex]`. The invariant `graphemes.map(g => g.g.replace('_', '')).join('') === word` accounts for the underscore.
120	- Silent letters get `p: ''` and still contribute to `word`.
121	- Digraphs must not cross syllable boundaries. Build-time test warns if they do.
122	- `ipa` is broad phonemic transcription (no narrow detail, no secondary stress unless disambiguating).
123	
124	### 4.4 Level grapheme pools are grapheme-phoneme units, not strings
125	
126	A single orthographic grapheme can teach different phonemes at different levels. In the AUS source, `c` appears at **level 2** (saying /k/, as in `cat`) and again at **level 4** (saying /s/, as in `cent`, `citrus`). Same for `g` (/g/ in `dog` at level 2, /dʒ/ in `gem` at level 4) and `th` (voiceless /θ/ and voiced /ð/).
127	
128	Modelling level pools as flat `string[]` would collide these variants — a single `'c'` entry can't say "this is the /k/ sound" vs "this is the /s/ sound". The library instead uses an explicit unit type:
129	
130	```ts
131	// src/data/words/levels.ts
132	export interface LevelGraphemeUnit {
133	  /** Orthographic form: 'c', 'sh', 'igh', 'a_e'. */
134	  g: string;
135	  /** IPA of the sound this unit teaches at this level: 'k', 's', 'ʃ', 'aɪ'. */
136	  p: string;
137	  /** Optional teacher label: 'soft c', 'th voiced'. */
138	  name?: string;
139	}
140	```
141	
142	`GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL: Record<number, readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[]>` stores **each variant** as its own entry. `cumulativeGraphemes(4)` returns both `{ g: 'c', p: 'k' }` and `{ g: 'c', p: 's' }`. Code that only needs the orthographic pool (e.g. the structural splitter in `makeGraphemes`) derives it via `units.map(u => u.g)` and dedupes.
143	
144	This matches the existing `Grapheme { g, p, span? }` instance type and makes the filter semantics in §7.1 work unchanged: a Tier-2 query like `{ graphemesRequired: ['c'], phonemesRequired: ['k'] }` correctly picks `cat` (whose Tier-2 entry has `{ g: 'c', p: 'k' }`) over `city` (whose Tier-2 entry has `{ g: 'c', p: 's' }`).
145	
146	### 4.5 Proper nouns are skipped
147	
148	The source word list contains proper nouns (`Nat`, `Sam`, `Kim`, `Meg`, `Phil`, `Troy`, `Mason`, `Pete`, `Duke`, `Friday`, `Vincent`). The codegen script filters them out by a simple first-letter-capitalized rule. They are not in the seed corpus. If we need them later (names-teaching mode), a dedicated chunk can be added — not in P1.
149	
150	## 5. File layout
151	
152	```text
153	src/data/words/
154	  types.ts                 # WordCore, CurriculumEntry, Grapheme, Region, WordFilter, WordHit
155	  levels.ts                # per-region label formatters + level → grapheme-set lookups
156	  phoneme-codes.ts         # teacher-code ↔ IPA alias table (sh, ch, th_voiceless…)
157	  builders.ts              # makeWordCore, makeGraphemes, makeCurriculumEntry, validateEntry
158	  writer.ts                # pure upsert/remove helpers — consumed by P2, lives here so invariants stay DRY
159	  filter.ts                # async filterWords() + chunk cache + two-file join
160	  adapters.ts              # toWordSpellRound(WordHit)
161	  words.test.ts            # invariant harness over every chunk file
162	  index.ts                 # public API re-exports
163	
164	  core/
165	    level1.json            # ~40 WordCore rows (SATPIN)
166	    level2.json            # ~55 WordCore rows (mdgockeur)
167	    level3.json            # ~60 WordCore rows (bhfljvwxyz)
168	    level4.json            # ~75 WordCore rows (digraphs + quirky graphemes)
169	    level5.json            # ~115 WordCore rows (long vowels ai/ee/oa/igh/oo)
170	    level6.json            # ~140 WordCore rows (oi/oy/oo/ou/ow/er/ir/ur/ar/or)
171	    level7.json            # ~120 WordCore rows (split digraphs + vowel-at-end y/e/o)
172	    level8.json            # ~90 WordCore rows (aw/ew/ou/air/are/ear/eer/ore/dge/tch)
173	
174	  curriculum/
175	    aus/
176	      level1.json          # CurriculumEntry rows — 1:1 with core/level1.json
177	      level2.json          # …
178	      level3.json
179	      level4.json
180	      level5.json
181	      level6.json
182	      level7.json
183	      level8.json
184	    uk/                    # empty directory — stub .gitkeep, seeded via P2 later
185	    us/                    # empty directory — stub .gitkeep
186	    br/                    # empty directory — shape-ready, no content
187	```
188	
189	Chunks are JSON (not TS). A build step generates a JSON schema from `types.ts` for optional IDE validation.
190	
191	The `core/levelN.json` chunking uses AUS levels because that's the source of truth. A UK-only chunking (e.g. `curriculum/uk/phase3.json` referencing words that span `core/level1..6.json`) is fine — the filter just loads the needed core chunks on demand.
192	
193	## 6. Storage strategy
194	
195	Library data ships as static JSON chunks, **lazy-loaded via Vite's `import.meta.glob`** and cached in module-level `Map`s. **Not persisted to RxDB/IndexedDB.**
196	
197	### 6.1 Why not IndexedDB for P1
198	
199	Per [`docs/adrs/0001-rxdb-without-tanstack-query.md`](../../adrs/0001-rxdb-without-tanstack-query.md), RxDB collections hold user-generated or frequently-mutated state. The P1 library is the opposite: read-only, ships with the app, replaced whole on every deploy, identical for every user.
200	
201	For ~650 words:
202	
203	| Concern           | JSON + in-memory filter | RxDB collection + indexes    |
204	| ----------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------- |
205	| Query performance | ~50µs over 650 entries  | ~1ms (IDB round-trip)        |
206	| Cold start        | One-time chunk fetch    | Fetch + seed + open IDB      |
207	| Schema evolution  | Redeploy JSON           | Migration on every data bump |
208	| Bundle/disk cost  | ~100 KB gzipped total   | Same + collection schema     |
209	| Test story        | Vitest reads JSON       | Integration test needs IDB   |
210	
211	`Array.filter` over 650 objects is tens of microseconds. IDB's strengths target problems the static library doesn't have.
212	
213	**P3 is different.** User word bags are mutable per-user state — they go in RxDB/IndexedDB. The P1 filter API is built to merge cleanly with P3's bag-sourced words later: the `WordSpellConfig.source` discriminated union adds a `'word-bag'` variant, and both paths produce the same `WordHit[]` shape downstream.
214	
215	### 6.2 Async signature protects future refactors
216	
217	`filterWords()` returns `Promise<WordHit[]>`. If the corpus grows past ~5,000 words and we eventually swap the in-memory implementation for a SQLite-wasm-backed one, the public API doesn't change — only `filter.ts` internals.
218	
219	### 6.3 Chunk loading
220	
221	```ts
222	const coreLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: WordCore[] }>(
223	  './core/level*.json',
224	);
225	const ausLoaders = import.meta.glob<{ default: CurriculumEntry[] }>(
226	  './curriculum/aus/level*.json',
227	);
228	// Same pattern for uk/, us/, br/ — resolved dynamically from the filter's region.
229	```
230	
231	Both sides are lazy-loaded on the first `filterWords()` call (or the first call that targets a given region) and cached. Workbox precaches them automatically in the service-worker asset manifest.
232	
233	## 7. Filter API
234	
235	```ts
236	// src/data/words/types.ts
237	export interface WordFilter {
238	  region: Region; // required
239	
240	  // Curriculum scope (Tier 1)
241	  level?: number;
242	  levels?: number[];
243	  levelRange?: [number, number];
244	
245	  // Structural (Tier 1)
246	  syllableCountEq?: number;
247	  syllableCountRange?: [number, number];
248	
249	  // Phonics (Tier 2 — require graphemes[] present)
250	  graphemesAllowed?: string[]; // every grapheme in the word ∈ this set
251	  graphemesRequired?: string[]; // word must contain ≥1 of these
252	  phonemesAllowed?: string[]; // every phoneme in the word ∈ this set
253	  phonemesRequired?: string[]; // word must contain ≥1 of these
254	
255	  /** Default true. Controls fallback-to-AUS behavior for unseeded regions. */
256	  fallbackToAus?: boolean;
257	}
258	
259	export interface FilterResult {
260	  hits: WordHit[];
261	  /** Set when the filter targeted a non-AUS region with no data and fell back. */
262	  usedFallback?: { from: Region; to: 'aus' };
263	}
264	```
265	
266	### 7.1 Matching semantics
267	
268	- `region` is required.
269	- `level` / `levels` / `levelRange` are OR-combined within the level dimension, AND-combined with everything else.
270	- `syllableCountRange: [min, max]` is inclusive.
271	- `graphemesAllowed`: a word passes only if **every** grapheme in the word's `graphemes[]` is in the allowed set. Words without `graphemes[]` (Tier 1 only) are **excluded** when this field is set.
272	- `graphemesRequired`: a word passes if **at least one** of the required graphemes appears in the word's `graphemes[]`. Excluded if `graphemes[]` absent.
273	- `phonemesAllowed` / `phonemesRequired`: same logic but on `graphemes[].p` values.
274	- All predicates AND together.
275	
276	### 7.2 AUS fallback
277	
278	When a filter targets `region: 'uk'` (or `'us'`, `'br'`) and no words match — typically because that region's curriculum is empty or doesn't cover the requested level — `filterWords()` re-runs the same filter against `region: 'aus'` and returns `usedFallback: { from: 'uk', to: 'aus' }` alongside the hits.
279	
280	The fallback is **per-query**: if even one word matches in the target region, no fallback kicks in. The caller (game config, UI) can inspect `usedFallback` and decide whether to show "showing Australian equivalents" notice.
281	
282	Callers can opt out with `fallbackToAus: false` to get a strict region-scoped query (returns empty hits instead of falling back).
283	
284	### 7.3 Example calls
285	
286	```ts
287	// Child is mid-level-2: knows SATPIN + m/d/g/o, not yet c/k/ck/e/u/r
288	await filterWords({
289	  region: 'aus',
290	  levels: [1, 2],
291	  graphemesAllowed: ['s', 'a', 't', 'p', 'i', 'n', 'm', 'd', 'g', 'o'],
292	  syllableCountEq: 1,
293	});
294	// → ['sit', 'sat', 'pin', 'pan', 'pad', 'dog', 'dot', 'mad', 'mat', ...]
295	// 'cat' excluded (has 'c'); 'red' excluded (has 'r' and 'e')
296	
297	// Teacher disambiguating 'c' making /k/ vs /s/
298	await filterWords({
299	  region: 'aus',
300	  graphemesRequired: ['c'],
301	  phonemesRequired: ['k'],
302	});
303	// → ['cat', 'cup', 'cap', 'car', 'cactus']
304	// 'city' excluded ('c' grapheme but no /k/ phoneme)
305	// 'pack' excluded (has /k/ but via 'ck' grapheme, not 'c')
306	
307	// Target /sh/ digraph practice
308	await filterWords({
309	  region: 'aus',
310	  graphemesRequired: ['sh'],
311	});
312	// → ['ship', 'shop', 'shed', 'fish', 'dish', 'wish', 'shin', ...]
313	
314	// UK filter with no UK data yet → falls back to AUS
315	const result = await filterWords({ region: 'uk', level: 3 });
316	// result.hits = AUS level 3 words
317	// result.usedFallback = { from: 'uk', to: 'aus' }
318	```
319	
320	## 8. Builder functions
321	
322	The builders are pure, deterministic, and used by **three** consumers:
323	
324	1. The **codegen script** that seeds the corpus from `words-list.md`
325	2. The **invariant test harness** (validation)
326	3. **P2's dev authoring form** (same validation + in-form preview)
327	
328	```ts
329	// src/data/words/builders.ts
330	
331	/** Heuristic: counts vowel groups, handles silent-e. Override with opts.syllables. */
332	export const makeWordCore = (
333	  word: string,
334	  opts?: { syllables?: string[]; variants?: string[] },
335	): WordCore;
336	
337	/**
338	 * Splits `word` into graphemes using `levelGraphemes` as the known set
339	 * (longest-match-first). Returns best-effort spans. Caller reviews ambiguous cases.
340	 * Returns `null` if the word can't be split cleanly (missing graphemes, ambiguity).
341	 */
342	export const makeGraphemes = (
343	  word: string,
344	  levelGraphemes: string[],
345	  phonemeByGrapheme?: Record<string, string>,
346	): Grapheme[] | null;
347	
348	/** Composes WordCore + CurriculumEntry with defaults; callers override specifics. */
349	export const makeCurriculumEntry = (
350	  word: string,
351	  level: number,
352	  opts: {
353	    levelGraphemes: string[];
354	    ipa?: string; // if omitted, derived from graphemes[].p
355	    graphemes?: Grapheme[]; // if omitted, derived via makeGraphemes
356	  },
357	): CurriculumEntry | null;
358	
359	/** Full invariant check. Returns structured errors so P2 can surface them. */
360	export const validateEntry = (
361	  core: WordCore,
362	  curriculum: CurriculumEntry,
363	): { ok: true } | { ok: false; errors: ValidationError[] };
364	
365	export interface ValidationError {
366	  field: 'word' | 'syllables' | 'graphemes' | 'ipa' | 'level';
367	  message: string;
368	}
369	```
370	
371	### 8.1 The codegen script
372	
373	A one-shot Node script at `scripts/seed-word-library.ts`:
374	
375	1. Parses `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-11-phonic-word-library_words-list.md` into `{ level, levelGraphemes, words[] }` blocks.
376	2. Drops proper nouns (`/^[A-Z]/`).
377	3. For each word, calls `makeWordCore` + `makeCurriculumEntry` with `levelGraphemes` from that level's grapheme set.
378	4. Splits results into two buckets:
379	   - **Success**: full Tier-2 entry — gets written to the appropriate `core/levelN.json` + `curriculum/aus/levelN.json`.
380	   - **Needs review**: Tier-1 only (builders returned `null` for graphemes/ipa) — gets written with `WordCore` only and **no** `CurriculumEntry.graphemes` / `ipa`. Listed in a `codegen-review.md` report for later enrichment via P2.
381	5. Dedups within a level (a word can appear in level 1 and level 2 but not twice in level 1).
382	6. Formats output with Prettier.
383	7. Writes files deterministically so re-running produces zero diff if inputs unchanged.
384	
385	The script runs once as part of the seed task. It's not part of the app build and doesn't need to be re-run per dev session — the JSON chunks are committed.
386	
387	## 9. Syllables
388	
389	### 9.1 Storage
390	
391	`WordCore.syllables` is an optional string array: `['sun', 'set']`. Length is the count; concatenation must equal `word`. Tier 1 words without `syllables[]` still have `syllableCount` for length filtering.
392	
393	When present, the array doubles as the chunks rendered in WordSpell's existing `tileUnit: 'syllable'` mode.
394	
395	Tricky cases:
396	
397	- Silent `e` does not get its own syllable. `cake` → `['cake']`, count 1.
398	- Digraphs never cross syllable boundaries. `chicken` → `['chick', 'en']`, not `['chic', 'ken']`.
399	- Regional syllable disagreement (e.g. US pronounces `fire` with two syllables, AUS/UK with one) uses the canonical spelling's dominant regional count. If it matters enough to diverge later, we escalate to per-region syllables — not in P1.
400	
401	### 9.2 Authoring
402	
403	Level 1–3 words (simple CVC, one syllable): codegen script sets `syllableCount: 1` and `syllables: [word]` automatically.
404	
405	Level 5+ multi-syllable words: codegen sets `syllableCount` via a vowel-group heuristic (`syllable` npm package, offline-installed) and leaves `syllables[]` undefined. Hand-review fills in the array via P2 when needed for syllable-mode games.
406	
407	## 10. Build-time validation
408	
409	`src/data/words/words.test.ts` loads every `core/*.json` and `curriculum/<region>/*.json` file and asserts:
410	
411	1. **`syllables.join('') === word`** when `syllables` is present.
412	2. **`graphemes.map(g => g.g.replace('_', '')).join('') === word`** when `graphemes` is present.
413	3. **Every `CurriculumEntry.word`** has a matching `WordCore` in the same level's core file.
414	4. **No orphan `WordCore`** — every core row must have at least one curriculum entry in at least one region (modulo P2 work-in-progress; warnings, not errors, for missing curricula).
415	5. **Variants backlink** — if `A.variants = ['B']` then `B.variants` must include `'A'`.
416	6. **No duplicate `word` within a single level's core file**. A word can appear in multiple levels.
417	7. **Phase/level consistency** — every `CurriculumEntry` lives in a file whose filename matches its `level` field.
418	8. **Proper nouns absent** — no entry starts with an uppercase letter (unless explicitly allowed later).
419	9. **Graphemes don't cross syllable boundaries** when both are present.
420	
421	Invariants are reused by P2's writer — the same validator runs in the dev form before a write is persisted.
422	
423	## 11. WordSpell integration
424	
425	Two non-breaking additions.
426	
427	### 11.1 `WordSpellConfig.source`
428	
429	```ts
430	// src/games/word-spell/types.ts
431	export type WordSpellSource = {
432	  type: 'word-library';
433	  filter: WordFilter;
434	  limit?: number;
435	};
436	// P3 later adds: | { type: 'word-bag'; bagId: string };
437	
438	export interface WordSpellConfig extends AnswerGameConfig {
439	  // ...existing fields
440	  rounds?: WordSpellRound[]; // explicit authoring — still wins when set
441	  source?: WordSpellSource;
442	}
443	```
444	
445	If `rounds` is set, it wins (explicit authoring beats filter). If `source` is set and `rounds` is absent, a new `useLibraryRounds` hook calls `filterWords(source.filter)`, picks `limit` entries (defaults to `totalRounds`), and feeds them to WordSpell via `toWordSpellRound`.
446	
447	### 11.2 Adapter
448	
449	```ts
450	// src/data/words/adapters.ts
451	import type { WordHit } from './types';
452	import type { WordSpellRound } from '@/games/word-spell/types';
453	
454	/** Joins syllables with '-' so WordSpell's existing segmentsForWord splits them. */
455	export const toWordSpellRound = (hit: WordHit): WordSpellRound => ({
456	  word: hit.syllables ? hit.syllables.join('-') : hit.word,
457	});
458	```
459	
460	### 11.3 Hook wiring
461	
462	`useLibraryRounds(config)` resolves either explicit `rounds` (sync) or library-driven `source` (async), returns `{ rounds, isLoading, usedFallback }`. WordSpell mounts it above its existing render and shows a brief loading state while async resolution runs. Legacy configs (with explicit `rounds`) short-circuit synchronously — no user-visible change.
463	
464	### 11.4 Ordering / sampling
465	
466	`filterWords()` returns entries in a deterministic order — alphabetical by region+level filename, then source-file order within each chunk. `.slice(0, limit)` gives the same first-N for the same filter. The existing `roundsInOrder: false` config controls shuffling downstream in `buildRoundOrder`; it applies after the library filter produces the round list, so no new shuffling logic at this layer.
467	
468	## 12. Seed corpus (~650 words)
469	
470	Distribution is driven by the source file at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-11-phonic-word-library_words-list.md`:
471	
472	| Level | Grapheme set                           | Raw words | After dedup + proper-noun filter |
473	| ----- | -------------------------------------- | --------- | -------------------------------- |
474	| 1     | `s a t p i n`                          | ~44       | ~40                              |
475	| 2     | `m d g o c k ck e u r`                 | ~62       | ~55                              |
476	| 3     | `b h f l j v w x y z`                  | ~66       | ~60                              |
477	| 4     | `sh ch th (both) qu ng wh ph g c`      | ~80       | ~75                              |
478	| 5     | `ai ay ea ee ie igh oa ow ew ue`       | ~120      | ~115                             |
479	| 6     | `oi oy oo (both) ou ow er ir ur ar or` | ~150      | ~140                             |
480	| 7     | `a_e e_e i_e o_e u_e + vowel-at-end`   | ~130      | ~120                             |
481	| 8     | `aw ew ou air are ear eer ore dge tch` | ~95       | ~90                              |
482	
483	Counts are approximate — the codegen script's dedup pass produces the exact numbers. All ~650 ship with **Tier 1 data**. Tier 2 enrichment coverage is expected to be ~90% for levels 1–4 (straightforward one-letter-one-phoneme splits) and ~60% for levels 5–8 (split digraphs, vowel ambiguity, multi-syllable edge cases). The ~100-word remainder lands in `codegen-review.md` for hand-review via P2.
484	
485	No regional showcase chunk in P1. Region variants (`colour`/`color`, `mum`/`mom`) are introduced via P2's dev form after the base corpus lands — they're specific hand-authored cases, not part of the source word list.
486	
487	## 13. Out of scope for P1
488	
489	These get their own specs.
490	
491	- **P2 — dev authoring form.** Storybook UI + Vite middleware for reading/writing the JSON chunks with add/update/delete (confirmation dialog). Dev-only, never ships to prod.
492	- **P3 — runtime user word bags.** IndexedDB-backed user-created word bags, CRUD UI in-app, export/import, new `WordSpellConfig.source = { type: 'word-bag' }`. Tier 1 only (no IPA authoring UI for parents).
493	- **Parent filter UI.** Controls in the WordSpell config form that let a parent compose a `WordFilter`. Waits on P2 maturity.
494	- **UK/US seed corpora.** Shape-ready from day one but unseeded. Added incrementally via P2. AUS fallback covers the gap until then.
495	- **pt-BR seed corpus.** Shape-ready, no content.
496	- **Bulk import pipeline** (CMU Pronouncing Dictionary, Wiktionary) for scaling past ~2,000 words. Not needed for K–Y2.
497	- **Algorithmic syllable splitting** (beyond counts). Codegen uses counts-only; the array form is hand-authored via P2 when needed.
498	- **RxDB-backed P1 library.** Blocked on corpus size and measured performance cost, neither of which applies now.
499	- **Second word-game adapters** (Word Builder, Read Aloud). Adapters land in `src/data/words/adapters.ts` when those games ship.
500	
501	## 14. Open questions
502	
503	None at design time. All region / scale / shape / filter / fallback decisions are committed above.
504	
505	## 15. Affected files — new
506	
507	| File                                             | Purpose                                                                                      |
508	| ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
509	| `src/data/words/types.ts`                        | `WordCore`, `CurriculumEntry`, `Grapheme`, `Region`, `WordFilter`, `WordHit`, `FilterResult` |
510	| `src/data/words/levels.ts`                       | Per-region label formatters + level → grapheme-set lookups                                   |
511	| `src/data/words/phoneme-codes.ts`                | Teacher-code ↔ IPA alias table                                                               |
512	| `src/data/words/builders.ts`                     | `makeWordCore`, `makeGraphemes`, `makeCurriculumEntry`, `validateEntry`                      |
513	| `src/data/words/writer.ts`                       | Pure upsert/remove helpers (consumed by P2)                                                  |
514	| `src/data/words/filter.ts`                       | Async `filterWords()` + chunk cache + two-file join + AUS fallback                           |
515	| `src/data/words/adapters.ts`                     | `toWordSpellRound()`                                                                         |
516	| `src/data/words/words.test.ts`                   | Invariants over every chunk file                                                             |
517	| `src/data/words/index.ts`                        | Public API re-exports                                                                        |
518	| `src/data/words/core/level{1..8}.json`           | `WordCore[]` per level                                                                       |
519	| `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level{1..8}.json` | `CurriculumEntry[]` per AUS level                                                            |
520	| `src/data/words/curriculum/{uk,us,br}/.gitkeep`  | Empty stubs (shape-ready)                                                                    |
521	| `scripts/seed-word-library.ts`                   | One-shot codegen script                                                                      |
522	| `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`       | Hook resolving `source` → rounds                                                             |
523	| `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx` | Hook tests                                                                                   |
524	
525	## 16. Affected files — edited
526	
527	| File                                                   | Change                                                                                                                                 |
528	| ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
529	| `src/games/word-spell/types.ts`                        | Make `rounds` optional, add `source?: WordSpellSource`                                                                                 |
530	| `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.tsx`         | Mount `useLibraryRounds`, render loading state, handle `usedFallback` banner hook-up (banner UI itself deferred to the filter-UI spec) |
531	| `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.stories.tsx` | Add `LibrarySourced` story                                                                                                             |
532
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md`

```text
1	# WordSpell — Cumulative Tile Pool, Y Phoneme Filter, and Mode-Driven Round Source
2	
3	> Status: **Draft (design)** — supersedes
4	> `2026-04-28-wordspell-multi-level-units-design.md` for the UI selection
5	> model and filter contract. Migration design from the prior spec is
6	> retained.
7	> Date: 2026-04-29
8	> Owner: leocaseiro
9	> Related: PR #219 (issue #216)
10	
11	## Background
12	
13	PR #219 shipped a free-form multi-level chip picker for WordSpell. While
14	testing the picker against the
15	`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-11-phonic-word-library_words-list.md`
16	curriculum, three problems surfaced:
17	
18	1. **The disjoint chip selection produces empty pools.** Picking only the
19	   Level-4 `sh` chip yields zero playable words because `i` and `p` are
20	   not in `graphemesAllowed` — the kid would not have the tiles to spell
21	   `ship`. The current contract requires the user to manually tick every
22	   prior-level chip too, which is a footgun.
23	2. **The mode-default leaks emoji words into recall mode.**
24	   `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG` ships `mode: 'recall'` (correct) **and**
25	   `rounds: sliceWordSpellRounds(8)` (the `cat 🐱 / dog 🐶 / sun ☀️` pool).
26	   `useLibraryRounds` short-circuits to explicit rounds whenever
27	   `config.rounds.length > 0`, so the library is never queried in the
28	   default-saved-null path.
29	3. **No live preview of the selection.** The teacher can pick chips and
30	   levels but cannot see what words the player will actually receive
31	   without launching the game.
32	
33	This spec keeps PR #219's `selectedUnits` data shape and the IndexedDB
34	migration from `2026-04-28-wordspell-multi-level-units-design.md`. It
35	replaces the filter contract, the chip-row interaction, the variant split
36	between Simple and Advanced, the mode default, and adds a preview bar.
37	
38	## Goals
39	
40	1. **Tiles always available.** A word never appears in a round unless the
41	   player has every grapheme tile needed to spell it. Selecting one chip
42	   anywhere should not strand the player without vowels.
43	2. **Y phoneme filter.** A word plays iff it contains **at least one**
44	   phoneme the user has explicitly ticked. This drops "easy" pure-prior-
45	   level words automatically when the user is focused on a higher-level
46	   sound.
47	3. **Group-click chip rows in both Simple and Advanced.** Clicking the
48	   level header bulk-toggles every chip in that row. Clicking an
49	   individual chip toggles one phoneme. No native checkboxes.
50	4. **One UI rendering.** Drop the `chips`/`checkbox-tree` variant split.
51	   Same component, same look, in Simple form and Advanced modal slot.
52	5. **Mode-driven default.** Recall default uses the word library; picture
53	   default uses the curated emoji pool. Neither default config carries
54	   the other mode's data.
55	6. **Live word preview.** A muted-background bar below the picker lists
56	   the words that would be in the round pool given the current selection.
57	   Mirrors the SortNumbers Simple form pattern.
58	
59	## Non-goals
60	
61	- **Curriculum data changes.** `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` and the per-level
62	  word JSON files are unchanged. The plan doc word lists in
63	  `2026-04-11-phonic-word-library_words-list.md` remain reference
64	  material; word selection is computed, not authored.
65	- **New round sources.** Picture mode keeps the existing
66	  `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`. Adding image-paired library words is out of
67	  scope.
68	- **Per-region tuning.** This change is region-agnostic — the same filter
69	  rule applies for `aus`, `uk`, `us`, `br`. Any region whose curriculum
70	  data is sparse simply yields a smaller pool.
71	- **Storybook visual baselines.** Stories whose snapshots include the
72	  affected forms are re-recorded as part of implementation, not designed
73	  here.
74	
75	## What changes vs. PR #219
76	
77	| PR #219 piece                                                                        | Status                                                                            |
78	| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
79	| `WordSpellLibrarySource` shell + registry slot                                       | Keep                                                                              |
80	| `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` registry hook                                            | Keep                                                                              |
81	| `selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[]` config shape                                    | Keep                                                                              |
82	| `level-unit-selection.ts` pure helpers                                               | Keep `toggleLevel`, `toggleUnit`, `defaultSelection`; **drop** `triStateForLevel` |
83	| `migrateWordSpellConfig` + schema bumps (`saved_game_configs` v3, `custom_games` v1) | Keep unchanged                                                                    |
84	| `chips` vs `checkbox-tree` variant prop                                              | **Drop** — single rendering                                                       |
85	| Native `<input type="checkbox">` for level rows                                      | **Drop** — replaced by clickable header                                           |
86	| `cumulativeGraphemes` helper in `levels.ts`                                          | Keep — now used by `resolveSimpleConfig` for `graphemesAllowed`                   |
87	| `phonemesAllowed` field on `source.filter`                                           | **Drop** in derived filter — replaced by `phonemesRequired`                       |
88	| `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG` carrying both `mode: 'recall'` and emoji `rounds`        | **Split** into `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` and `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG`               |
89	| `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`                                                              | Keep — only consulted for picture mode now                                        |
90	| `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` (per-level playability)                               | Keep, **extend** to assert the new cumulative + Y rule                            |
91	| `source-emits-playable.test.tsx`                                                     | **Rewrite** to drive the group-click UI and assert the new filter shape           |
92	
93	## Selection model — unchanged data, new derivation
94	
95	The authoritative selection state stays:
96	
97	```ts
98	type LevelGraphemeUnit = { g: string; p: string };
99	
100	type WordSpellSimpleConfig = {
101	  configMode: 'simple';
102	  selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[];
103	  region: Region;
104	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
105	};
106	```
107	
108	The migration helper `migrateWordSpellConfig` from the prior spec
109	populates `selectedUnits` from legacy `{level, phonemesAllowed}` docs
110	unchanged.
111	
112	### What's new: how `selectedUnits` becomes a filter
113	
114	```ts
115	// resolve-simple-config.ts (rewritten)
116	export const resolveSimpleConfig = (
117	  simple: WordSpellSimpleConfig,
118	): WordSpellConfig => {
119	  const units = readSelectedUnits(simple); // tolerant reader, falls back to defaults
120	  const maxLevel =
121	    units.length > 0 ? Math.max(...units.map(unitLevel)) : 1;
122	  const graphemesAllowed = cumulativeGraphemes(maxLevel).map(
123	    (u) => u.g,
124	  );
125	  const phonemesRequired = [...new Set(units.map((u) => u.p))];
126	
127	  return {
128	    gameId: 'word-spell',
129	    component: 'WordSpell',
130	    configMode: 'simple',
131	    mode: 'recall',
132	    tileUnit: 'letter',
133	    inputMethod: simple.inputMethod,
134	    wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
135	    ttsEnabled: true,
136	    roundsInOrder: false,
137	    totalRounds: 4,
138	    tileBankMode: 'exact',
139	    source: {
140	      type: 'word-library',
141	      filter: {
142	        region: simple.region ?? 'aus',
143	        graphemesAllowed: [...new Set(graphemesAllowed)],
144	        phonemesRequired,
145	      },
146	    },
147	  };
148	};
149	```
150	
151	`unitLevel(u)` is a small helper that returns the introduction level of a
152	`(g, p)` unit (looked up against `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL`). It is
153	deterministic — `(g, p)` uniquely identifies a unit within the AUS
154	curriculum.
155	
156	## Filter contract — `graphemesAllowed` ∧ `phonemesRequired`
157	
158	`filterWords` and `entryMatches` are unchanged — they already implement
159	both fields with the right semantics:
160	
161	- `graphemesAllowed`: every grapheme of a candidate word must be in the
162	  set. Guarantees the kid has every tile they need to spell the word.
163	- `phonemesRequired`: at least one of a candidate word's phonemes must
164	  be in the set. Implements the Y filter — drops easy words.
165	
166	These are AND'd. A word plays iff:
167	
168	```text
169	∀ g ∈ word.graphemes: g.g ∈ graphemesAllowed
170	∧ ∃ g ∈ word.graphemes: g.p ∈ phonemesRequired
171	```
172	
173	`phonemesAllowed` is intentionally **not** set by the simple form. It
174	would over-constrain — e.g., picking only `sh` would block `ship`
175	because `/ɪ/` is not in `phonemesAllowed`. Players still get the
176	educational benefit because every word's phonemes are inherently
177	teachable (every grapheme they use was learned in some prior or current
178	level).
179	
180	The `level` field on `WordFilter` is also intentionally not set — the
181	curriculum's `level` tag is informational only. A word tagged at L4 can
182	play for a player whose `selectedUnits` peak at L4, regardless of which
183	level the word was originally introduced at.
184	
185	## UI — single rendering for both Simple and Advanced
186	
187	Stacked rows, one per level (L1 through L8). Each row contains a
188	clickable header label and the level's `(g, p)` chips.
189	
190	```text
191	┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
192	│ ◐ Level 1     · 6 / 6 sounds                              │
193	│ [s /s/] [a /æ/] [t /t/] [p /p/] [i /ɪ/] [n /n/]            │
194	├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
195	│ ○ Level 2     · 0 / 8 sounds                              │
196	│ [m /m/] [d /d/] [g /g/] [o /ɒ/] [c, k, ck /k/]            │
197	│ [e /ɛ/] [u /ʌ/] [r /r/]                                    │
198	├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
199	│ ◯ Level 3     · partial 4 / 10 sounds                     │
200	│ [b /b/] [h /h/]·on  [f /f/]·on  [l /l/]·on  …              │
201	└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
202	
203	Live preview: ship, shop, fish, dish, wish, lash, …       (47 words)
204	```
205	
206	### Chip and header states
207	
208	| Selection                | Header glyph | Header text                      | Header bg          | Chip variant          |
209	| ------------------------ | ------------ | -------------------------------- | ------------------ | --------------------- |
210	| All chips on             | ●            | "Level N · N / N sounds"         | filled accent      | green                 |
211	| Some chips on            | ◐            | "Level N · partial M / N sounds" | half-filled accent | mix of green and grey |
212	| No chips on, level ≤ max | ◯            | "Level N · tiles only"           | neutral grey       | grey-outlined         |
213	| No chips on, level > max | ○            | "Level N · not in scope"         | dimmed             | faded                 |
214	
215	The "tiles only" state visualises the cumulative-grapheme cascade: the
216	row contributes its graphemes to the tile pool but no phonemes to the
217	Y filter. The "not in scope" state is identical visually but
218	semantically different — those graphemes are excluded from the tile pool
219	because no chip at or above that level is ticked.
220	
221	### Interaction
222	
223	- **Click level header** → bulk-toggle every chip in that row. Computed
224	  by `toggleLevel(level, selected, target)` where `target = 'unchecked'`
225	  if the row is currently `all-on`, else `'checked'`. Indeterminate rows
226	  go to all-on.
227	- **Click an individual chip** → toggle just that `(g, p)` unit.
228	  `toggleUnit(unit, selected)` (already exists).
229	- **Multi-grapheme chip** (e.g., L2 `c, k, ck /k/` — three graphemes,
230	  one phoneme) → toggling the chip toggles all three units atomically.
231	  The chip is "on" iff all three units are in `selectedUnits`.
232	- **Empty selection** (`selectedUnits.length === 0`) → form surfaces
233	  the existing "Pick at least one sound to play." validation message.
234	- **Headers in "not in scope" state remain clickable.** Clicking a
235	  header above the current `maxLevel` bulk-adds its chips and extends
236	  the cumulative tile pool — the "not in scope" tag is a passive cue,
237	  not a disabled state.
238	
239	### Accessibility
240	
241	The clickable header is rendered as a `<button>` with `aria-pressed`
242	reflecting the all-on / partial / off state. The header text includes
243	the count ("Level 4 · 1 / 10 sounds") so screen readers announce both
244	the level and selection state in one phrase.
245	
246	Chips remain `<button>` elements with `aria-pressed` reflecting their
247	own toggled state. Keyboard navigation: tab through header, then
248	through chips in the row, with `Space` / `Enter` to toggle.
249	
250	## Default state
251	
252	- **First launch (no saved config):** `selectedUnits = all units in L1`
253	  (six units). Equivalent to "L1 row all-on, every other row off".
254	  Tile pool = L1 graphemes; Y filter = L1 phonemes; word pool = the
255	  ~45 words in `curriculum/aus/level1.json`.
256	- **Subsequent launches:** restore last-persisted selection from
257	  `last-session-game-config` (RxDB-backed; existing infrastructure).
258	- **Deep-link route loads** without a saved config still default to L1
259	  via `resolveWordSpellConfig`.
260	
261	## Mode-driven round source (Fix B)
262	
263	`DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG` is split into two non-overlapping defaults:
264	
265	```ts
266	const DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG: WordSpellConfig = {
267	  // ...shared fields
268	  mode: 'recall',
269	  source: {
270	    type: 'word-library',
271	    filter: {
272	      region: 'aus',
273	      graphemesAllowed: cumulativeGraphemes(1).map((u) => u.g),
274	      phonemesRequired: defaultSelection().map((u) => u.p),
275	    },
276	  },
277	  // No `rounds` field — `useLibraryRounds` queries `source.filter`
278	};
279	
280	const DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG: WordSpellConfig = {
281	  // ...shared fields
282	  mode: 'picture',
283	  rounds: sliceWordSpellRounds(8),
284	  // No `source` — picture rounds are explicit emoji+word pairs
285	};
286	```
287	
288	`resolveWordSpellConfig` picks the appropriate default by looking at the
289	saved config's mode (defaulting to `recall`):
290	
291	```ts
292	export const resolveWordSpellConfig = (
293	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
294	): WordSpellConfig => {
295	  const mode = saved?.mode === 'picture' ? 'picture' : 'recall';
296	  const base = mode === 'picture' ? DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG : DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG;
297	
298	  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'WordSpell') return base;
299	
300	  if (saved.configMode === 'simple') {
301	    return resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig(...);
302	  }
303	
304	  // Advanced merge — ensure the result obeys the mode invariant
305	  const merged: WordSpellConfig = { ...base, ...saved, gameId, component };
306	
307	  if (merged.mode === 'recall' && merged.rounds) {
308	    delete merged.rounds; // recall must come from source
309	  }
310	  if (merged.mode === 'picture' && merged.source) {
311	    delete merged.source; // picture must come from rounds
312	    if (!merged.rounds) merged.rounds = sliceWordSpellRounds(8);
313	  }
314	
315	  return merged;
316	};
317	```
318	
319	This guarantees the shape invariant **mode = recall ⇒ source defined ∧
320	rounds undefined; mode = picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined**.
321	`useLibraryRounds`'s short-circuit on `config.rounds.length > 0` becomes
322	unreachable in recall mode.
323	
324	A new `mode-default-invariant.test.ts` asserts the invariant for every
325	mode flip path: simple → advanced, advanced toggle in modal, route
326	loader fallback.
327	
328	## Live word preview (Fix C)
329	
330	A new component `WordPreviewBar` lives below the chip rows in
331	`WordSpellLibrarySource`. It uses the same async filter pipeline that
332	the game itself uses:
333	
334	```ts
335	const WordPreviewBar = ({ filter }: { filter: WordFilter }) => {
336	  const sig = filterSignature(filter);
337	  const result = useFilteredWords(filter); // useEffect + cancellation
338	  if (result.isLoading) return <Bar muted>Loading…</Bar>;
339	  if (result.hits.length === 0) {
340	    return <Bar danger>Pick at least one sound to play.</Bar>;
341	  }
342	  const preview = result.hits.slice(0, 24).map((h) => h.word).join(', ');
343	  const more = result.hits.length > 24 ? `, … (${result.hits.length} total)` : '';
344	  return <Bar muted>{preview}{more}</Bar>;
345	};
346	```
347	
348	Visual: matches SortNumbers' preview row —
349	`rounded-lg bg-muted px-3 py-2 text-center font-mono text-sm`.
350	Truncation at 24 words keeps the bar to one line on most viewports;
351	total count is shown when truncated.
352	
353	The `useFilteredWords` hook is a thin wrapper around `filterWords`. No
354	debounce — chip toggles are user-paced (≤ 1 call per click). The hook
355	cancels in-flight queries on unmount or signature change and caches
356	by `filterSignature` so repeated identical selections are free.
357	
358	## Component API
359	
360	`WordSpellLibrarySource` (existing file, internals rewritten):
361	
362	```ts
363	type Props = {
364	  config: Record<string, unknown>;
365	  onChange: (config: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
366	};
367	```
368	
369	The `variant` prop from PR #219 is removed. The Advanced modal hosts the
370	same component as the Simple form via the existing
371	`getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` registry slot — no styling difference.
372	
373	The component:
374	
375	- Reads `config.selectedUnits` (or applies default L1 selection if absent).
376	- Reads `config.region` (defaults to `'aus'`).
377	- Renders one row per level 1–8.
378	- Each row's chips are computed from `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[level]`,
379	  collapsing same-phoneme-same-level units into one chip
380	  (`c, k, ck /k/`).
381	- Header state is derived from how many of the level's chips are on.
382	- Renders `WordPreviewBar` below the rows.
383	- Dispatches `onChange` with the updated `selectedUnits` array on
384	  every toggle.
385	
386	### Level-unit-selection helpers (small change)
387	
388	`level-unit-selection.ts` keeps `toggleLevel`, `toggleUnit`,
389	`defaultSelection`. `triStateForLevel` is replaced by a richer state
390	descriptor used by the new header:
391	
392	```ts
393	type LevelHeaderState =
394	  | { kind: 'all-on'; count: number; total: number }
395	  | { kind: 'partial'; count: number; total: number }
396	  | { kind: 'tiles-only'; total: number }   // no chips ticked, level ≤ max
397	  | { kind: 'not-in-scope'; total: number }; // no chips ticked, level > max
398	
399	export const headerStateForLevel = (
400	  level: number,
401	  selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[],
402	  maxLevel: number,
403	): LevelHeaderState;
404	```
405	
406	Pure function. Trivially unit-testable.
407	
408	## Edge cases
409	
410	| Case                                                    | Behaviour                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
411	| ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
412	| User clears every chip                                  | `selectedUnits = []`, validation message shown                                                                                                                                                                           |
413	| User picks only one L8 chip                             | Tile pool = L1-8 cumulative, Y filter = 1 phoneme; preview may show < 4 words but pool is non-empty                                                                                                                      |
414	| User picks chips at L3 + L7 only                        | `maxLevel = 7`, tile pool = L1-7 cumulative, Y filter = chosen L3 + L7 phonemes                                                                                                                                          |
415	| Targeted struggle drill, e.g. `d, g, b, v` across L2/L3 | Each chip is independently toggleable. `maxLevel = 3`, tile pool = L1-3 cumulative, Y filter = `{/d/, /g/, /b/, /v/}` → words like `bad, big, dog, van, vet, bug, dad` play; pure-vowel-only words drop out              |
416	| Filter yields zero hits                                 | Preview bar shows "Pick at least one sound to play." (same copy as empty selection — rare in practice; would only happen if Y phonemes never co-occur with cumulative graphemes, which doesn't happen in AUS curriculum) |
417	| Picture mode toggled in Advanced                        | `source` is dropped, `rounds = WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` is restored, picker is hidden                                                                                                                                      |
418	| Recall mode re-toggled                                  | `rounds` is dropped, last-known `selectedUnits` is restored, picker is shown                                                                                                                                             |
419	
420	## Testing strategy
421	
422	Three layers, mirroring PR #219's discipline:
423	
424	### 1. Pure-logic unit tests
425	
426	`src/games/word-spell/level-unit-selection.test.ts` (extend existing):
427	
428	- `headerStateForLevel` returns `all-on` / `partial` / `tiles-only` /
429	  `not-in-scope` correctly across selection × maxLevel combinations.
430	- `toggleLevel` and `toggleUnit` continue to behave as in PR #219.
431	
432	`src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.test.ts` (extend existing):
433	
434	- Empty `selectedUnits` → `maxLevel = 1`, default L1 graphemes, no
435	  `phonemesRequired` (or `phonemesRequired = []`).
436	- Single L4 unit (`{g:'sh', p:'ʃ'}`) → `graphemesAllowed = L1-4
437	cumulative graphemes`, `phonemesRequired = ['ʃ']`.
438	- Mixed L3 + L7 → `maxLevel = 7`, cumulative L1-7 graphemes, both
439	  phoneme sets in `phonemesRequired`.
440	- Multi-grapheme phoneme (`/k/` from L2) → `phonemesRequired` contains
441	  `'k'` once even though three units contributed it.
442	
443	### 2. Component tests
444	
445	`src/games/word-spell/WordSpellLibrarySource/WordSpellLibrarySource.test.tsx`
446	(rewrite):
447	
448	- Renders 8 level rows.
449	- Default render shows L1 row in `all-on`, others in `not-in-scope`.
450	- Click level header → all chips in that row toggle on; subsequent
451	  click toggles them off.
452	- Click individual chip → toggles one phoneme; row state transitions
453	  correctly through `partial`.
454	- Multi-grapheme chip (`c, k, ck /k/` at L2) toggles all three units
455	  atomically.
456	- L1 `s /s/` and L4 `c /s/` are independent chips (do not co-toggle).
457	- `WordPreviewBar` renders the words for the current selection.
458	- Empty selection surfaces "Pick at least one sound to play."
459	- No `<input type="checkbox">` in the DOM (regression guard).
460	
461	### 3. Curriculum invariant
462	
463	`src/data/words/curriculum-invariant.test.ts` (extend):
464	
465	- For every `(region, level)`, build `selectedUnits` from that level's
466	  units alone and assert `filterWords` returns ≥ `MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS`
467	  hits. Same as PR #219, but the filter now uses cumulative
468	  `graphemesAllowed` + Y `phonemesRequired`.
469	- New cross-level invariant: for every `(region, level N)`, picking
470	  the single highest-level chip at level N yields a non-empty pool
471	  (proves the cumulative-grapheme cascade keeps single-chip selections
472	  playable).
473	
474	### 4. Form-emits-playable
475	
476	`src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm/source-emits-playable.test.tsx`
477	(rewrite):
478	
479	- Render the new chip UI.
480	- For each level 1–8, click the level header (bulk-on) and assert the
481	  emitted `source.filter` is well-formed.
482	- Click individual chips and assert `phonemesRequired` updates.
483	- Verify the L1-only default produces non-empty `filterWords` results.
484	- Specifically test "only L4 sh" (the regression case from PR #219):
485	  emits `graphemesAllowed = L1-4 cumulative`, `phonemesRequired =
486	['ʃ']`, `filterWords` returns ≥ 4 hits.
487	
488	### 5. Mode-default invariant
489	
490	`src/games/word-spell/mode-default-invariant.test.ts` (new):
491	
492	- `resolveWordSpellConfig(null)` returns recall + library source + no
493	  emoji `rounds`.
494	- `resolveWordSpellConfig({mode: 'picture'})` returns picture + emoji
495	  `rounds` + no `source`.
496	- Mode flip recall → picture → drops `source`, restores `rounds`.
497	- Mode flip picture → recall → drops `rounds`, restores `source`.
498	- Saved config with `configMode: 'simple'` always resolves to recall +
499	  library (Simple form has no picture mode).
500	
501	## Risk & mitigation
502	
503	**Saved Custom Games under the old shape break on read.** Addressed
504	already by the `migrateWordSpellConfig` migration from the prior spec —
505	unchanged. Belt-and-suspenders: `resolve-simple-config.ts` reads
506	`selectedUnits` with a fallback to deriving from legacy
507	`{level, phonemesAllowed}` if the migration somehow didn't run.
508	
509	**`useFilteredWords` for the preview adds another query path.** The
510	filter signature is the same as the game's, so the existing
511	`pickWithRecycling` cache is reused. Worst case a no-op cache miss on
512	first render of the form.
513	
514	**Visual regression in Storybook.** Plan re-records the affected stories
515	(`WordSpellSimpleConfigForm`, `AdvancedConfigModal` with WordSpell
516	header) and includes a manual diff review step. The chip UI looks
517	similar to PR #219 but with a different header — expect baseline drift.
518	
519	**Performance — `filterWords` runs without a level prefilter.** The
520	phoneme test is a small Set lookup; the chunked indexes already load
521	lazily. PR #219 measured well under 50 ms for typical selections; this
522	spec adds at most one additional query (the preview), bounded the same
523	way.
524	
525	**Drift between selection state and filter derivation.** Both Simple
526	form and resolveSimpleConfig derive their state purely from
527	`selectedUnits` — there is no cached intermediate. Test assertions
528	exercise the full round-trip.
529	
530	## Implementation order (preview — full plan separate)
531	
532	1. Replace `triStateForLevel` with `headerStateForLevel` in
533	   `level-unit-selection.ts`; tests.
534	2. Rewrite `WordSpellLibrarySource` component: drop variant prop,
535	   replace native checkbox with clickable header, render
536	   `WordPreviewBar`; component tests.
537	3. Drop the `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` variant wrapper if any (the
538	   registry hook itself stays); ensure Advanced modal renders the
539	   same component.
540	4. Rewrite `resolve-simple-config.ts`: cumulative `graphemesAllowed` +
541	   `phonemesRequired`; no `level` field; tests.
542	5. Split `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG` into recall and picture defaults;
543	   rewrite `resolveWordSpellConfig` for mode-driven default and
544	   invariant enforcement; new `mode-default-invariant.test.ts`.
545	6. Add `useFilteredWords` hook and `WordPreviewBar`; component test.
546	7. Extend `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` and rewrite
547	   `source-emits-playable.test.tsx` for the new contract.
548	8. Re-record Storybook baselines for affected stories; run VR.
549	9. Manual smoke test in dev server: pick "L4 + sh only" and confirm
550	   `ship, shop, fish, dish` play; switch to picture mode and confirm
551	   the emoji pool returns; switch back to recall and confirm library
552	   words return without an app reload.
553	
554	## Open questions
555	
556	None at this point. The selection model, filter contract, UI, defaults,
557	preview, and migration are all settled.
558
```
