<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-word-spell-library-levels-recall-design.md`

```text
1	# WordSpell — Library Levels (Recall Mode) Design Spec
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-15
4	Branch: `feat/word-spell-library-levels-recall`
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Let WordSpell generate its rounds from the phonics word library
9	(`src/data/words/`). The Simple Config form — owned by the
10	`kid-friendly-homepage` PR — writes a `WordSpellSource` onto
11	`config.source`. This spec covers the **data layer beneath**: sampling
12	`totalRounds` words randomly from the filtered pool, guaranteeing no
13	repeats within a session, preferring unseen words across sessions, and
14	recycling when the unseen pile runs out. Recall mode only.
15	
16	## Goals
17	
18	- Random sampling of `N = totalRounds` words from the filtered library
19	  pool when `roundsInOrder` is `false` (the new default for WordSpell).
20	- No repeats within a single session.
21	- Fresh set on each replay.
22	- Different users / visits get different sequences for the same config.
23	- When the unseen pile is smaller than `N`, take all of it and top up
24	  from already-seen words so the session still has `N` rounds.
25	- "Seen words" state persists across reloads, scoped per profile (RxDB).
26	- Respect `roundsInOrder: true`: skip sampling and recycling entirely;
27	  play the first `N` hits in the order `filterWords` returns them.
28	
29	## Non-goals
30	
31	- Simple Config UI for level + sound chips — owned by
32	  `kid-friendly-homepage`.
33	- Scramble / picture / sentence-gap modes — recall only in this spec.
34	- `levelMode` auto-progression (Level 2 → Level 3 after success) — future.
35	- Smart re-queue of mistakenly-answered words — future.
36	
37	## Pool-exhaustion behaviour
38	
39	The rule, by example: 11-word pool, 5 rounds per session, 3 sessions.
40	
41	| Play | Unseen before | Sample taken (5 distinct)       | Unseen after    |
42	| ---- | ------------- | ------------------------------- | --------------- |
43	| 1    | 11            | 5 fresh                         | 6               |
44	| 2    | 6             | 5 fresh                         | 1               |
45	| 3    | 1             | 1 unseen + 4 recycled from seen | reset to 4 seen |
46	
47	The 4 recycled words in play 3 are picked at random from the "seen"
48	bucket. After play 3 the seen set is reset to just the words used in
49	play 3, so the next session starts with 7 fresh unseen words.
50	
51	## Boundary with `kid-friendly-homepage`
52	
53	That PR owns the Simple Config form and writes:
54	
55	```ts
56	config.source = {
57	  type: 'word-library',
58	  filter: {
59	    region: 'aus',
60	    level: N,              // or levelRange / levels
61	    phonemesAllowed: [...],
62	  },
63	};
64	```
65	
66	This spec consumes `config.source` in `useLibraryRounds` and samples
67	from the result. No type changes to `WordSpellConfig`, `WordSpellSource`,
68	or `WordFilter`. No shared files edited beyond a single-line
69	`seededRandom` import swap in `build-round-order.ts`.
70	
71	## Data layer
72	
73	### New: `src/lib/seeded-random.ts`
74	
75	Move the existing `seededRandom(seedStr): () => number` helper out of
76	`src/games/build-round-order.ts` (lines 7-21) into a shared module.
77	`build-round-order.ts` switches to
78	`import { seededRandom } from '@/lib/seeded-random'`. No behaviour
79	change; covered by the existing `build-round-order` tests.
80	
81	### New: `src/data/words/sample.ts`
82	
83	Fisher–Yates shuffle on a copy of the hits array, then slice.
84	
85	```ts
86	export const sampleHits = (
87	  hits: WordHit[],
88	  limit: number,
89	  seed?: string,
90	): WordHit[] => {
91	  if (hits.length === 0 || limit <= 0) return [];
92	  const random = seed === undefined ? Math.random : seededRandom(seed);
93	  const copy = [...hits];
94	  for (let i = copy.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
95	    const j = Math.floor(random() * (i + 1));
96	    [copy[i], copy[j]] = [copy[j]!, copy[i]!];
97	  }
98	  return copy.slice(0, Math.min(limit, copy.length));
99	};
100	```
101	
102	- Pool smaller than `limit` → returns the whole shuffled pool (silent cap).
103	- `seed === undefined` → `Math.random` path; two calls give different
104	  sequences (matches how `buildRoundOrder` behaves without a seed).
105	- Same seed → same sequence (tests get determinism by passing a seed).
106	
107	### New: `src/data/words/seen-words.ts`
108	
109	Pure logic + a `SeenWordsStore` interface. No storage implementation
110	here — that's injected by the caller.
111	
112	```ts
113	export interface SeenWordsStore {
114	  get: (signature: string) => Promise<Set<string>>;
115	  addSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
116	  resetSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
117	}
118	
119	export const filterSignature = (filter: WordFilter): string => {
120	  // region | level|levels|levelRange | sorted(phonemesAllowed) |
121	  // sorted(phonemesRequired) | sorted(graphemesAllowed) |
122	  // sorted(graphemesRequired) | syllableCountEq | syllableCountRange
123	  //
124	  // Sorting the array fields makes the signature stable regardless of
125	  // the order the Simple Config form emits chips.
126	};
127	
128	export const pickWithRecycling = async (
129	  hits: WordHit[],
130	  limit: number,
131	  signature: string,
132	  store: SeenWordsStore,
133	  seed?: string,
134	): Promise<WordHit[]> => {
135	  const seen = await store.get(signature);
136	  const unseen = hits.filter((h) => !seen.has(h.word));
137	
138	  if (unseen.length >= limit) {
139	    const picked = sampleHits(unseen, limit, seed);
140	    await store.addSeen(
141	      signature,
142	      picked.map((h) => h.word),
143	    );
144	    return picked;
145	  }
146	
147	  const recycled = sampleHits(
148	    hits.filter((h) => seen.has(h.word)),
149	    limit - unseen.length,
150	    seed,
151	  );
152	  const picked = [...unseen, ...recycled];
153	  await store.resetSeen(
154	    signature,
155	    picked.map((h) => h.word),
156	  );
157	  return picked;
158	};
159	
160	export const createInMemorySeenWordsStore = (): SeenWordsStore => {
161	  // Used by tests and as the fallback when RxDB is unavailable.
162	};
163	```
164	
165	- `filterSignature` is stable across filter objects with the same
166	  content. Array fields are sorted; `undefined` fields are skipped.
167	- `resetSeen(sig, picked)` starts the next cycle with the most-recently-
168	  used words marked seen — matches the "reset to 4 seen" row in the
169	  table above.
170	- The in-memory store keeps unit tests isolated and also acts as the
171	  fallback when the database isn't ready yet (first paint before RxDB
172	  hydrates).
173	
174	### Persistence: RxDB collection
175	
176	Seen-words state persists per profile so a kid picking up a tablet
177	tomorrow still sees fresh words.
178	
179	**New schema: `src/db/schemas/word_spell_seen_words.ts`**
180	
181	```ts
182	export type WordSpellSeenWordsDoc = {
183	  id: string; // `${profileId}__${signature}`
184	  profileId: string;
185	  signature: string;
186	  words: string[];
187	  updatedAt: string;
188	};
189	
190	export const wordSpellSeenWordsSchema: RxJsonSchema<WordSpellSeenWordsDoc> =
191	  {
192	    version: 0,
193	    primaryKey: 'id',
194	    type: 'object',
195	    properties: {
196	      id: { type: 'string', maxLength: 256 },
197	      profileId: { type: 'string', maxLength: 36 },
198	      signature: { type: 'string', maxLength: 512 },
199	      words: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
200	      updatedAt: { type: 'string', format: 'date-time' },
201	    },
202	    required: ['id', 'profileId', 'signature', 'words', 'updatedAt'],
203	    additionalProperties: false,
204	  };
205	```
206	
207	- Primary key `${profileId}__${signature}` keeps per-profile isolation
208	  and lets upserts hit a single row.
209	- `words` is the whole seen set. Updates always rewrite the array.
210	  Array size is bounded by level-pool size (hundreds at most).
211	- Registered in `src/db/schemas/index.ts`, added to `MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION`,
212	  and wired into `src/db/create-database.ts` alongside the other
213	  collections.
214	
215	**New hook: `src/db/hooks/useSeenWordsStore.ts`**
216	
217	```ts
218	export const useSeenWordsStore = (): SeenWordsStore => {
219	  const { db } = useRxDB();
220	  const profileId = ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID; // TODO: active profile hook
221	  const fallback = useMemo(() => createInMemorySeenWordsStore(), []);
222	
223	  return useMemo<SeenWordsStore>(() => {
224	    if (!db) return fallback;
225	    const docId = (signature: string) => `${profileId}__${signature}`;
226	
227	    return {
228	      get: async (signature) => {
229	        const doc = await db.word_spell_seen_words
230	          .findOne(docId(signature))
231	          .exec();
232	        return new Set(doc?.words ?? []);
233	      },
234	      addSeen: async (signature, words) => {
235	        const id = docId(signature);
236	        const existing = await db.word_spell_seen_words
237	          .findOne(id)
238	          .exec();
239	        const merged = Array.from(
240	          new Set([...(existing?.words ?? []), ...words]),
241	        );
242	        await (existing
243	          ? existing.incrementalPatch({
244	              words: merged,
245	              updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
246	            })
247	          : db.word_spell_seen_words.insert({
248	              id,
249	              profileId,
250	              signature,
251	              words: merged,
252	              updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
253	            }));
254	      },
255	      resetSeen: async (signature, words) => {
256	        const id = docId(signature);
257	        const existing = await db.word_spell_seen_words
258	          .findOne(id)
259	          .exec();
260	        const now = new Date().toISOString();
261	        await (existing
262	          ? existing.incrementalPatch({ words, updatedAt: now })
263	          : db.word_spell_seen_words.insert({
264	              id,
265	              profileId,
266	              signature,
267	              words,
268	              updatedAt: now,
269	            }));
270	      },
271	    };
272	  }, [db, fallback, profileId]);
273	};
274	```
275	
276	- Uses `ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID` today; the hook can later switch to the
277	  real active profile without callers changing.
278	- When `db` is still bootstrapping, falls back to the in-memory store
279	  so the first session still plays correctly — the next session picks
280	  up the persisted state.
281	- No explicit migration needed beyond the schema-version bump that
282	  `MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION` already enforces.
283	
284	### No changes to
285	
286	- `src/data/words/types.ts`
287	- `src/data/words/filter.ts`
288	- `src/data/words/adapters.ts`
289	
290	### Config default: `roundsInOrder`
291	
292	`WordSpellConfig.roundsInOrder` defaults to **`false`** going forward.
293	Confirm and update the default where `WordSpellConfig` is instantiated
294	by the catalog / Simple Config form. When `true`, the game skips the
295	sampler and recycling entirely — see the branching below.
296	
297	**Scope note:** `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG` in
298	`src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx` stays `roundsInOrder: true`
299	— its inline comment documents why (VR determinism with hand-authored
300	emoji rounds). The "default to false" directive applies to the
301	library-sourced configs produced by the kid-friendly-homepage Simple
302	Config form. Coordinate with that PR so its form emits
303	`roundsInOrder: false` whenever it sets `source`.
304	
305	## Hook integration
306	
307	### `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`
308	
309	Signature:
310	
311	```ts
312	export const useLibraryRounds = (
313	  config: WordSpellConfig,
314	  seed: string | undefined,
315	  store: SeenWordsStore,
316	): LibraryRoundsState;
317	```
318	
319	In the async effect block, branch on `roundsInOrder`:
320	
321	```ts
322	const source = config.source;
323	const result = await filterWords(source.filter);
324	const limit = source.limit ?? config.totalRounds;
325	
326	const picked =
327	  config.roundsInOrder === true
328	    ? result.hits.slice(0, limit)
329	    : await pickWithRecycling(
330	        result.hits,
331	        limit,
332	        filterSignature(source.filter),
333	        store,
334	        seed,
335	      );
336	
337	const rounds = picked.map(toWordSpellRound);
338	```
339	
340	- `roundsInOrder: true` → deterministic, no RxDB writes, matches the
341	  current pre-spec behaviour exactly. Useful for teacher demos and
342	  deterministic screenshots.
343	- `roundsInOrder: false` (the new default) → full
344	  random-sample-with-recycling path.
345	- Effect deps gain `seed` and the `store` identity. When the seed
346	  changes (new session), the effect re-runs and samples fresh. The
347	  existing `config.rounds` fast path (hand-authored rounds) is
348	  untouched — sampling only applies when `config.source` is set.
349	
350	### `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.tsx`
351	
352	Two additions: pull the seen-words store, and generate a fresh seed
353	per session.
354	
355	```ts
356	const seenWordsStore = useSeenWordsStore();
357	const sampleSeed = useMemo(() => nanoid(), [sessionEpoch]);
358	const { rounds: resolvedRounds, isLoading } = useLibraryRounds(
359	  config,
360	  sampleSeed,
361	  seenWordsStore,
362	);
363	```
364	
365	- First mount → `nanoid()` → random 5, recycling-aware.
366	- Play Again bumps `sessionEpoch` → new `nanoid()` → different random 5;
367	  seen-words state (now in RxDB) updates across plays so the next
368	  session prefers fresh words.
369	- Two users / two visits → different `nanoid()` → different sequences.
370	- When `config.roundsInOrder === true`, the hook short-circuits and the
371	  store is never touched.
372	
373	No other WordSpell changes. Recall mode already works end-to-end:
374	`WordSpell.tsx` lines 204-208 suppress emoji/image when
375	`config.mode === 'recall'`, and line 234 keeps the `AudioButton` so the
376	kid hears the word. `buildRoundOrder` continues to handle the play
377	order — shuffled when `roundsInOrder` is false, sequential when true.
378	
379	## End-to-end flow (recall mode, library source, `roundsInOrder: false`)
380	
381	1. `kid-friendly-homepage` Simple Config writes
382	   `config.source = { type: 'word-library', filter: { region: 'aus',
383	level: 2, phonemesAllowed: [...] } }`, `totalRounds: 5`, and
384	   `roundsInOrder: false` (the new default).
385	2. `WordSpell` mounts → `seenWordsStore = useSeenWordsStore()` →
386	   `sampleSeed = nanoid()` → `useLibraryRounds(config, sampleSeed,
387	seenWordsStore)`.
388	3. Hook calls `filterWords(filter)` → e.g., 11 hits.
389	4. Hook calls `pickWithRecycling(hits, 5, sig, store, seed)` → 5
390	   distinct `WordHit`s; the RxDB-backed store writes the seen words for
391	   `(profileId, signature)`.
392	5. Hook maps to `WordSpellRound[]` via the existing `toWordSpellRound`.
393	6. `buildRoundOrder(5, false, seed)` shuffles play order.
394	7. Each round plays in recall mode: TTS speaks the word, kid drags or
395	   types the letters.
396	
397	When `roundsInOrder: true`, step 4 becomes `result.hits.slice(0, 5)`
398	and the store is bypassed; step 6 produces sequential play order.
399	
400	## Testing
401	
402	Per `CLAUDE.md`, tests come first (red → green).
403	
404	### Unit
405	
406	- `src/lib/seeded-random.test.ts` (new) — same seed → same sequence;
407	  different seeds → different sequences. Guards the extraction.
408	- `src/data/words/sample.test.ts` (new)
409	  - Returns `min(limit, hits.length)` items.
410	  - All items distinct.
411	  - Same seed → same output; different seed → different output.
412	  - `limit = 0` → `[]`. Empty pool → `[]`.
413	- `src/data/words/seen-words.test.ts` (new) — covers `pickWithRecycling`
414	  with the in-memory store.
415	  - First call samples `limit` fresh, marks them seen.
416	  - Second call excludes previously seen when the pool is large enough.
417	  - **Pool-exhaustion scenario (11 words, 5 rounds, 3 plays)** — asserts
418	    the table above: 5+5+5 distinct per session, unseen runs out on play
419	    3, seen set resets to the words just played.
420	  - `filterSignature` is stable across filter objects with the same
421	    content regardless of property order or array ordering of
422	    `phonemesAllowed` / `graphemesAllowed`.
423	  - Different signatures don't share seen sets.
424	- `src/db/hooks/useSeenWordsStore.test.tsx` (new)
425	  - RxDB roundtrip: `addSeen` then `get` returns the merged set.
426	  - `resetSeen` replaces the set entirely.
427	  - Different profiles don't share rows (keyed by `${profileId}__${sig}`).
428	  - When `db` is not yet ready, falls back to in-memory behaviour.
429	
430	### Component / hook
431	
432	- `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx` — extend:
433	  - When `seed` changes between renders, rounds re-sample.
434	  - Small-pool case returns the whole pool (silent cap).
435	  - `config.roundsInOrder === true` → takes `hits.slice(0, limit)` and
436	    does not call `store.get` / `store.addSeen`.
437	  - Hand-authored `config.rounds` path is unaffected.
438	- `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.test.tsx` — extend:
439	  - With `config.source` set, renders `totalRounds` rounds from the
440	    library.
441	  - "Play Again" triggers a new sample (assert different words appear
442	    when the pool is large enough; or assert that the seen-store was
443	    called with an updated signature).
444	
445	### VR / E2E
446	
447	- No new VR baselines — the rendered UI for a single recall round is
448	  unchanged.
449	- No new E2E — the Simple Config → Play flow belongs to
450	  `kid-friendly-homepage`. This PR is pure data-layer.
451	
452	### TDD order
453	
454	1. `seeded-random` extraction + its test — green after the import-site
455	   swap.
456	2. `sample.ts` + test — red → green.
457	3. `seen-words.ts` (interface + in-memory store + `pickWithRecycling`)
458	   - test including pool-exhaustion table — red → green.
459	4. New RxDB schema `word_spell_seen_words` registered + schema tests
460	   updated; `useSeenWordsStore` hook + its test — red → green.
461	5. `useLibraryRounds` — extend its existing test first (including the
462	   `roundsInOrder: true` short-circuit), then change the hook.
463	6. `WordSpell.tsx` — extend the component test first, then pass
464	   `sampleSeed` + `seenWordsStore`.
465	7. Update the `roundsInOrder` default to `false` wherever WordSpell
466	   configs are constructed.
467	
468	## Risks
469	
470	- **Shared tabs / devices**: two tabs write to the same RxDB collection
471	  on the same profile, so concurrent `Play Again` presses could race.
472	  Acceptable for the kid-first audience (one tab at a time in
473	  practice); the store uses `incrementalPatch` for additive writes.
474	- **Seen-set growth**: bounded by level-pool size (each level has at
475	  most a few hundred words); `resetSeen` naturally caps growth when the
476	  cycle rolls over.
477	- **Schema migration**: adding a new collection is version 0 in its
478	  own schema — no data migration of existing collections. The bump to
479	  `MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION` covers the `app_meta` tracking.
480	- **Test determinism**: `WordSpell` already threads a `seed` prop; the
481	  new `sampleSeed` layer uses the same pattern. Tests pass explicit
482	  seeds or mock `nanoid`; the hook test injects a fresh in-memory
483	  `SeenWordsStore` per case.
484	- **Signature drift with `kid-friendly-homepage`**: if that PR ships a
485	  `WordFilter` shape that differs from today's (`region` + `level` +
486	  `phonemesAllowed`), only `filterSignature` needs to learn the new
487	  fields. The sampler itself is shape-agnostic.
488	- **Profile wiring**: today the hook uses `ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID`. When
489	  the real active-profile hook lands, `useSeenWordsStore` updates in
490	  one place; no call-site changes needed.
491	
492	## Rollout
493	
494	Single PR to master, gated by existing CI.
495	
496	Files touched:
497	
498	- `src/lib/seeded-random.ts` (new)
499	- `src/games/build-round-order.ts` (import swap, no behaviour change)
500	- `src/data/words/sample.ts` (new)
501	- `src/data/words/seen-words.ts` (new — interface, pure logic,
502	  in-memory store)
503	- `src/data/words/index.ts` (exports, if needed by consumers)
504	- `src/db/schemas/word_spell_seen_words.ts` (new)
505	- `src/db/schemas/index.ts` (register + include in `MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION`)
506	- `src/db/create-database.ts` (add the new collection)
507	- `src/db/types.ts` (typed DB handle — if applicable)
508	- `src/db/hooks/useSeenWordsStore.ts` (new)
509	- `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` (add `seed` + `store`,
510	  branch on `roundsInOrder`)
511	- `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.tsx` (wire `sampleSeed` +
512	  `seenWordsStore`)
513	- Anywhere a WordSpell config default is constructed — set
514	  `roundsInOrder: false`
515	- Test files per the Testing section.
516	
517	No architecture docs update required — no game-state logic changes
518	(reducer, dispatch, behaviours untouched). The CLAUDE.md architecture-
519	docs rule doesn't trigger for `src/data/words/`, `src/lib/`, or
520	`src/db/`.
521	
522	## Open questions (deferred to future specs)
523	
524	- Extending recycling to scramble / picture / sentence-gap modes.
525	- Wiring `levelMode` auto-progression into WordSpell (Level 2 → Level 3
526	  after a successful session).
527	- Smart re-queue for mistakenly-answered words.
528
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-kid-friendly-homepage-design.md`

```text
1	# Kid-Friendly Homepage & Simple Config — Design Spec
2	
3	> _Renamed 2026-04-16: "bookmark" → "custom game". See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-16-custom-games-and-bookmarks-design.md`._
4	
5	Date: 2026-04-15
6	Branch: `design/kid-friendly-homepage`
7	
8	## Summary
9	
10	Redesign the homepage, game cards, and instructions screen so that kids (PK–K
11	roughly, with parent assist) can find and launch games without reading
12	developer-style labels. The changes land in four areas:
13	
14	1. **Game covers** — every game gets visual cover art (big emoji on a themed
15	   gradient for v1) so the homepage reads visually, not textually.
16	2. **Playful game titles** and kid-readable labels that still teach the
17	   academic term (e.g. "Going Up! _ascending_").
18	3. **Simple config forms** as the default for every game, made of chunky
19	   buttons, native-`<select>` dropdown cells, and +/− steppers. The existing
20	   dev-style "advanced" form moves behind an `Advanced ⚙️` link and stays
21	   unchanged.
22	4. **Saved custom games become first-class homepage cards** with a config chip
23	   strip, colored ribbon, and custom game icon — so parents set up presets once
24	   and kids just tap a card. Every custom game can also carry its **own cover**
25	   (emoji or image URL), falling back to the game's default when unset.
26	5. **Per-card cog opens a modal with the full Advanced form.** Saving from
27	   the modal updates the current custom game (if any) or creates a new one —
28	   it never overwrites a game default.
29	
30	Audience pattern: **kid-first, parent-assist** (chosen in brainstorming). Kids
31	drive, parents tweak without leaving the page.
32	
33	## Non-goals
34	
35	- Game defaults stay emoji-based for v1. The cover type supports images
36	  (URL or asset path) so custom games can opt in today, but no game default
37	  ships with an image in this spec.
38	- No device file uploads — image covers accept a URL string only. A future
39	  upload flow can reuse the same `cover.src` field.
40	- No sectioned category layout (Favorites / Math / Reading). With only three
41	  games today, a flat grid is honest. Revisit when catalog grows past ~6
42	  games.
43	- No changes to the underlying game engines, move logs, or scoring.
44	- No changes to the existing advanced config form's content or field set.
45	- No new locales — all copy additions are `en` and `pt-BR`.
46	- No changes to the Parent route (`/$locale/_app/parent/*`) in this spec.
47	
48	## Game renames
49	
50	Source of truth: `src/lib/i18n/locales/{en,pt-BR}/games.json`.
51	
52	| Current title | New title (en)        | Rationale                                       |
53	| ------------- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
54	| Word Spell    | **Spell It!**         | Verb-forward, playful, suggests an action.      |
55	| Number Match  | **Match the Number!** | Adds a verb; "Number Match" reads like a label. |
56	| Sort Numbers  | **Count in Order**    | Teaches the underlying action (count + order).  |
57	
58	### Count in Order — direction labels
59	
60	The `sort-numbers-ui.ascending-label` / `descending-label` keys become
61	playful-first, school-vocabulary-second:
62	
63	| Direction  | Big label          | Subtitle (smaller, italic) |
64	| ---------- | ------------------ | -------------------------- |
65	| ascending  | 🚀 **Going Up!**   | `ascending`                |
66	| descending | 🛝 **Going Down!** | `descending`               |
67	
68	The big label is what the kid sees. The subtitle reinforces the academic term
69	so parents can tie the UI back to schoolwork.
70	
71	## Covers (v1)
72	
73	Covers are **per-config**, not just per-game. A saved custom game can carry its
74	own cover (e.g. 🦁 for "Zoo Words"); when none is set, it falls back to the
75	game's default cover. The underlying render supports a **hybrid** of emoji or
76	image so custom games can opt into real artwork without forcing the whole catalog
77	onto an image pipeline.
78	
79	### Cover type
80	
81	```ts
82	// src/games/cover.ts (new)
83	export type Cover =
84	  | { kind: 'emoji'; emoji: string; gradient?: [string, string] }
85	  | { kind: 'image'; src: string; alt?: string; background?: string };
86	```
87	
88	- `emoji` covers keep the zero-art v1 story: a single large emoji on a themed
89	  two-stop gradient.
90	- `image` covers accept a URL or imported asset (PNG / WebP / SVG). `src` is
91	  stored as-is; `background` is an optional solid CSS colour used as a
92	  fallback while the image loads.
93	
94	### GameCover component
95	
96	`<GameCover cover={...} size="card" | "hero" />` renders either variant. The
97	component lives in `src/components/GameCover.tsx` and is the single consumer
98	of the `Cover` type. All callers (homepage cards, instructions hero, save-
99	config preview) go through it.
100	
101	If `cover` is undefined, the component falls back to
102	`resolveDefaultCover(gameId)`.
103	
104	### Per-game defaults
105	
106	Each `GAME_CATALOG` entry carries a `defaultCover: Cover` (emoji for v1):
107	
108	| Game              | Default emoji | Gradient                                 |
109	| ----------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
110	| Spell It!         | 🔤            | `#fde68a → #fb923c` (warm yellow→orange) |
111	| Match the Number! | 🔢            | `#bae6fd → #6366f1` (sky blue→indigo)    |
112	| Count in Order    | 📊            | `#bbf7d0 → #10b981` (mint→emerald)       |
113	
114	Any game default can later be swapped to an `image` cover by changing only
115	the registry entry.
116	
117	### Per-custom game cover
118	
119	`SavedGameConfigDoc` (see `src/db/schemas/saved_game_configs.ts`) gains an
120	optional `cover?: Cover` field. Unset means "inherit the game default".
121	
122	Resolution order at render time:
123	
124	1. If the custom game has `cover`, use it.
125	2. Otherwise, use the game's `defaultCover`.
126	3. The custom game's colored ribbon + icon + subtitle still differentiate it
127	   even when it inherits the game cover.
128	
129	### Picking a cover (save / update custom game flow)
130	
131	The custom game save dialog and the Instructions screen's "Update" flow both
132	show a small cover picker:
133	
134	- **Emoji picker** — a curated palette of ~24 emojis grouped by theme
135	  (animals, food, nature, numbers, shapes). Tap an emoji + pick a gradient
136	  preset (same palette as the custom game color picker) → stored as
137	  `{ kind: 'emoji', emoji, gradient }`.
138	- **Image input** — a single text input labeled "Image URL (optional)". If
139	  filled, it overrides the emoji and stores `{ kind: 'image', src }`. Paste
140	  a public URL or a relative path to a file in `public/`.
141	- **"Use game default"** — clears the cover field, falling back to the
142	  game's `defaultCover`.
143	
144	File upload from the device is **out of scope for v1** — we only accept a
145	URL string today. The data model supports a future upload flow without
146	schema changes (the `src` field is just a string).
147	
148	## Homepage
149	
150	Current: `src/routes/$locale/_app/index.tsx` renders a `LevelRow` + `GameGrid`
151	of text-forward `GameCard`s. Saved configs appear as inline chips under each
152	card.
153	
154	New structure:
155	
156	```text
157	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
158	│  BaseSkill logo                           │  ← compact topbar
159	├───────────────────────────────────────────┤
160	│  [cover]    [cover]    [cover]    [cover] │
161	│  Title  ⚙️  Title  ⚙️  Title  ⚙️  Title ⚙️│
162	│  chips      chips      chips      chips   │
163	│  [Play ]    [Play ]    [Play ]    [Play ] │
164	│                                            │
165	│  [cover ribbon ⚙️]  [cover ribbon ⚙️]  …
166	│  Title                                     │
167	│  "Custom game name"                           │
168	│  chips                                     │
169	│  [Play ]                                   │
170	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
171	```
172	
173	Each card carries its **own cog** (⚙️) in the top-right. Tapping the cog
174	opens the **advanced config modal** (see "Cog + advanced modal" below). No
175	global parent gear in the topbar — the per-card cog is the only advanced
176	surface on the homepage.
177	
178	### Responsive grid
179	
180	- Mobile (`< 640px`): 1 column
181	- `sm` (≥ 640px): 2 columns
182	- `lg` (≥ 1024px): 3 columns
183	- `xl` (≥ 1280px): 4 columns
184	- `2xl` (≥ 1536px): 5 columns
185	
186	### Card shapes
187	
188	Two card variants, both built on a single `GameCard` component:
189	
190	**Default card** (one per `GAME_CATALOG` entry):
191	
192	- Cover (emoji + gradient)
193	- Game title (e.g. "Spell It!")
194	- 2–3 **config chips** summarizing the game's current default simple-config
195	  values (see "Config chip rules" below)
196	- Big "Play" button
197	
198	**Custom game card** (one per `SavedGameConfigDoc`):
199	
200	- Cover: the custom game's own `cover` if set, otherwise the game default.
201	- **Colored top ribbon** matching the custom game color.
202	- **Custom game icon** in the top-right corner of the cover.
203	- Game title.
204	- **Custom game name** as a subtitle under the title (e.g. "My Words").
205	- 2–3 config chips reflecting **the custom game's config**, not the game default.
206	- Play button styled in the custom game's color.
207	
208	Clicking a default card navigates to the game with no `configId`. Clicking a
209	custom game card navigates with `configId` (same behavior as today's
210	`handlePlayWithConfig`).
211	
212	### Config chip rules
213	
214	Chips surface the most salient values from the **simple config** (see
215	"Simple config forms" below). Verbose over tight — parents need to pick
216	custom games apart at a glance.
217	
218	| Game              | Chips (in order, `·` separated)                                            |
219	| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
220	| Spell It!         | `Level {n}` · sounds csv (truncated `…` after ~3) · input (Drag/Type/Both) |
221	| Match the Number! | `{from} → {to}` pair · `{min}–{max}` range · input (Drag/Type/Both)        |
222	| Count in Order    | direction (Up/Down) · `{n} numbers` · `{step}s` · input (Drag/Type/Both)   |
223	
224	Chips come from a pure `configToChips(gameId, config)` function so the card
225	and the instructions-screen summary stay in sync.
226	
227	### Sort order
228	
229	The card list is sorted:
230	
231	1. **Last-played first** (game or custom game). Requires reading `session_history`
232	   for the current profile and taking the most-recent entry per
233	   `(gameId, configId)`.
234	2. Then **game defaults** in registry order.
235	3. Then **other custom games**, grouped by `gameId`.
236	
237	### Level / subject filters
238	
239	Removed from the homepage. Kids don't use them. Level is already a
240	per-game simple-config control (Spell It!), and subject is implicit in
241	the three-game catalog today. Parents can still access the full
242	`/$locale/_app/parent/*` route via the existing nav for bulk oversight.
243	Out of scope for this spec: redesigning the Parent route itself.
244	
245	## Cog + advanced modal
246	
247	Every card — default or custom game — shows a small **⚙️ cog** in its top-right
248	corner. Tapping it opens a modal that renders the **full advanced config
249	form** for that game (the existing `ConfigFormFields` output, unchanged).
250	
251	The modal replaces what used to be an inline "Advanced ⚙️" link inside the
252	simple form. The simple form stays the kid-friendly default; the advanced
253	form lives entirely behind the cog.
254	
255	### Modal entry points
256	
257	- **Homepage card cog** — opens the modal pre-filled with that card's
258	  config (the game default for a default card; the custom game's config for a
259	  custom game card).
260	- **Instructions screen cog** — same modal, same behavior. Replaces the
261	  inline "Advanced ⚙️" link previously shown under the simple form.
262	
263	### Modal contents
264	
265	- Cover picker (emoji palette + image URL input + "Use game default").
266	- Custom game name field (required when saving as new; pre-filled for existing
267	  custom games).
268	- Custom game color picker (for "Save as new").
269	- **Full advanced form** (existing `ConfigFormFields` + `renderConfigForm`
270	  per game, no visual changes).
271	- Save bar:
272	  - If opened from a **custom game card**: `Update "{custom game name}"`
273	    (primary) + `Save as new custom game` (secondary) + `Cancel`.
274	  - If opened from a **default card**: `Save as new custom game` (primary
275	    only) + `Cancel`. There is no "Update default" button — **defaults are
276	    immutable**.
277	
278	### Never overwrites defaults
279	
280	Saving from the modal always either updates an existing custom game or
281	creates a new one. The `GAME_CATALOG` default config is never persisted
282	to; it is the immutable starting point. If a parent wants a different
283	"default-feeling" experience, they save a custom game — that custom game becomes
284	the card they and the kid tap.
285	
286	## Simple config forms
287	
288	Every game gets a `<SimpleConfigForm gameId>` component. This is the **default**
289	view for:
290	
291	1. The Instructions screen's settings panel.
292	2. The custom game save/update dialog's config preview (inside the cog modal
293	   — see "Cog + advanced modal" above).
294	
295	The simple form never exposes an inline "Advanced" link. The advanced form
296	is reachable only via the per-card cog. The persisted data model stays the
297	same — `SortNumbersSimpleConfig` uses the existing `configMode:
298	'simple' | 'advanced'` discriminator; Match the Number! and Spell It! gain
299	analogous simple-mode types.
300	
301	### Shared UI primitives (new)
302	
303	These primitives live in `src/components/config/` and are reused across all
304	simple forms:
305	
306	- **`<ChunkGroup>`** — a row of chunky toggle buttons (big emoji + label +
307	  optional italic subtitle). Single-select or multi-select. Used for
308	  direction, input method, level selection.
309	- **`<CellSelect>`** — a styled cell wrapping a native `<select>`. Shows
310	  `preview` text and a small `hint` line. OS picker on mobile, accessible by
311	  default.
312	- **`<Stepper>`** — a `−`/value/`+` numeric stepper with `min` and `max`.
313	- **`<ChipStrip>`** — wrap-flow of small chip tags. Supports read-only and
314	  toggleable modes (for Spell It! sound filtering).
315	
316	These are intentionally simple — no custom dropdowns or popovers. Native
317	`<select>` is the heavy lifter.
318	
319	### Spell It! simple config
320	
321	```
322	┌ Level ──────────────────────────────────┐
323	│ [CellSelect: "Level 2" / AUS progression]│
324	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
325	┌ Sounds at this level (tap to toggle) ───┐
326	│ [ChipStrip: m /m/] [d /d/] [g /g/] [o /ɒ/]│
327	│ [c /k/] [k /k/] [ck /k/] [e /e/] ...      │
328	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
329	┌ How do you answer? ──────────────────────┐
330	│ [ChunkGroup: ✋ Drag | ⌨️ Type | ✨ Both] │
331	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
332	```
333	
334	Notes:
335	
336	- Region defaults to AUS; dropdown deferred until another region's word
337	  library is populated (UK/US/BR are `.gitkeep` today).
338	- `Sounds at this level` chips are **toggleable** — tap a chip to
339	  include/exclude. The selected set feeds `WordFilter.phonemesAllowed` (or
340	  `graphemesAllowed`) so the word pool is filtered to only words that use the
341	  selected sounds.
342	  - Default: all sounds at the current level selected.
343	  - If the user deselects all, the form shows an inline warning and the
344	    `Play` CTA is disabled until at least one sound is selected.
345	- Picture mode is removed from simple (and from the default). Library words
346	  have no guaranteed image, so the default mode is `scramble`.
347	- `WordSpellSource` usage: the form produces a `WordSpellSource = { type:
348	'word-library', filter: { region: 'aus', level: N, phonemesAllowed: [...] } }`
349	  and writes it to the config's `source` field.
350	
351	### Match the Number! simple config
352	
353	```
354	┌ What you see → What you match ──────────┐
355	│ [CellSelect: "5 / numeral"] → [CellSelect: "●●●●● / group"] │
356	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
357	┌ Range (min/max) ─────────────────────────┐
358	│ Min [Stepper: 1]    Max [Stepper: 10]    │
359	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
360	┌ How do you answer? ──────────────────────┐
361	│ [ChunkGroup: ✋ Drag | ⌨️ Type | ✨ Both] │
362	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
363	┌ Extra wrong tiles ───────────────────────┐
364	│ [Stepper: 3]                             │
365	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
366	```
367	
368	Notes:
369	
370	- Tile style is forced to `dots` (only supported style today). The other
371	  options in `tileStyle` (`objects`, `fingers`) remain reachable from the
372	  advanced form once those are supported.
373	- The two "from / to" cells expose the four primitives: `numeral`, `group`,
374	  `word`, `ordinal`. The pair is resolved to the existing
375	  `NumberMatchMode` union by a pure `pairToMode(from, to)` function. Invalid
376	  pairs (e.g. `numeral → numeral`) are prevented by:
377	  1. Filtering the second `<select>`'s options based on the first value.
378	  2. If the current "to" becomes invalid after a "from" change, it auto-
379	     updates to the first valid option.
380	- Range `min`/`max`: steppers with `min = 1` and **no upper cap** (clamping
381	  only to reasonable tile-render sane values via `max = 999` at the input
382	  level, but the UI offers no visible ceiling). Constraint: `max >= min`
383	  (adjusting `min` pushes `max` up; adjusting `max` below `min` pushes `min`
384	  down).
385	- `Extra wrong tiles` stepper is visible only when the pair mode supports
386	  distractors (all current modes do). 0–10. Writes to `distractorCount` and
387	  implicitly sets `tileBankMode` to `distractors` when > 0, `exact` when 0.
388	
389	### Count in Order simple config
390	
391	```
392	┌ Which way? ──────────────────────────────┐
393	│ [ChunkGroup: 🚀 Going Up! | 🛝 Going Down!]│
394	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
395	┌ How many? ─── Start at ─── Skip by ──────┐
396	│ [Stepper 5]    [Stepper 2]   [Stepper 2] │
397	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
398	┌ Preview ─────────────────────────────────┐
399	│ 2, 4, 6, 8, 10                            │
400	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
401	┌ How do you answer? ──────────────────────┐
402	│ [ChunkGroup: ✋ Drag | ⌨️ Type | ✨ Both] │
403	└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
404	```
405	
406	Notes:
407	
408	- Stepper ranges: `quantity` 2–8, `start` 1–99, `step` 1–10 (larger values
409	  remain available via advanced).
410	- Preview line updates live: `sequence = Array.from({length: quantity}, (_, i)
411	=> start + i * step)` (reverse when descending). Uses the existing
412	  `resolveSimpleConfig` helper.
413	- Direction ChunkGroup stores `ascending` / `descending` as it does today.
414	- Distractors default OFF in simple (not shown). Parent enables via the
415	  advanced cog modal (see "Cog + advanced modal").
416	
417	## Instructions screen
418	
419	Current: `src/components/answer-game/InstructionsOverlay/InstructionsOverlay.tsx`
420	already has the right structure (collapsible settings, save bar). Changes are
421	mostly content:
422	
423	**Visible by default:**
424	
425	- `← Back` affordance (new).
426	- **Cover** (new, replacing the text-only `GameNameChip` at the top).
427	- Game title + custom game badge (if launched from a custom game).
428	- **Cog ⚙️** in the header (matches the homepage card cog) — opens the
429	  advanced config modal.
430	- Instructions text + TTS speaker (unchanged).
431	- **Big "Let's go!" CTA** (styled bigger/more playful than today).
432	- Settings chevron (collapsed).
433	
434	**Settings expanded:**
435	
436	- `<SimpleConfigForm gameId>` — same component used by the cog modal's
437	  preview area (single source of truth for simple-mode rendering).
438	- **No inline Advanced link.** The advanced form lives exclusively behind
439	  the cog modal. The simple form stays focused on the kid-friendly
440	  controls.
441	- Save bar beneath:
442	  - If launched from a custom game: `Update "{custom game name}"` (primary) +
443	    `Save as new custom game` (secondary). Same behavior as today.
444	  - If launched from a default: `Save as new custom game` only (defaults are
445	    immutable — see "Cog + advanced modal").
446	
447	## Data and typing changes
448	
449	- `Cover` union type in `src/games/cover.ts` (new).
450	- `GAME_CATALOG` gains a `defaultCover: Cover` field per entry.
451	- `SavedGameConfigDoc` schema gains an optional `cover?: Cover`. RxDB
452	  migration bumps the `saved_game_configs` schema version and adds the new
453	  optional field — existing docs remain valid with `cover: undefined`.
454	- `resolveCover(doc, gameId): Cover` helper in `src/games/cover.ts`
455	  implements the fallback chain (custom game cover → game default).
456	- `WordSpellConfig` gains a `configMode?: 'simple' | 'advanced'` and a
457	  `WordSpellSimpleConfig` type mirroring `SortNumbersSimpleConfig`.
458	- `NumberMatchConfig` gains the same `configMode` + `NumberMatchSimpleConfig`
459	  type.
460	- `configToChips(gameId, config): string[]` utility in
461	  `src/games/config-chips.ts` (new), replacing ad-hoc chip logic in the card.
462	- Direction / input-method formatters live in
463	  `src/games/simple-labels.ts` so the homepage chips and the Instructions
464	  screen label render identically.
465	- `sortByLastPlayed` helper in `src/games/catalog-utils.ts` (extension),
466	  reading from `session_history`.
467	
468	No breaking changes to existing saved configs: the advanced form still edits
469	the same underlying shape, and simple-mode configs are a superset (all fields
470	that simple doesn't touch keep their default values from the full shape).
471	
472	## Localization
473	
474	- All new UI copy lands in `src/lib/i18n/locales/en/*.json` and
475	  `src/lib/i18n/locales/pt-BR/*.json`.
476	- Game titles, direction labels, instruction settings labels, chunk labels
477	  ("Drag", "Type", "Both", "Going Up!", "Going Down!"), row labels, and the
478	  `Advanced ⚙️` / `Update` / `Save as new…` strings all get translation
479	  keys.
480	- Sound-chip phonetic transcriptions (`/m/`, `/d/`, etc.) are not
481	  translated; they come from the existing IPA metadata.
482	
483	## Accessibility
484	
485	- All ChunkGroup, CellSelect, and Stepper primitives ship with correct ARIA:
486	  `aria-pressed`, labeled groups, visible focus rings.
487	- Native `<select>` inside CellSelect is keyboard-operable without custom
488	  event plumbing.
489	- ChunkGroup uses radio semantics when single-select.
490	- Color-only differentiation of custom game ribbons is supplemented by the
491	  custom game icon and the subtitle name so colorblind users still disambiguate.
492	- Instructions screen keeps the existing `role="dialog"` behavior.
493	
494	## Testing
495	
496	- Unit tests for `configToChips`, `pairToMode`, `sortByLastPlayed`, and the
497	  "no sounds selected disables Play" edge case in Spell It! simple.
498	- Component tests for each `SimpleConfigForm` variant confirming:
499	  - Changing a control updates config correctly.
500	  - Advanced toggle reveals the existing form inline.
501	  - Preview / chips reflect current simple config.
502	- VR tests (new snapshots):
503	  - Homepage with: (a) defaults only, (b) defaults + custom games, (c)
504	    custom games only (edge case). Mobile and desktop widths.
505	  - Instructions screen for each game, settings collapsed and expanded.
506	- E2E: kid flow — open homepage → tap custom game card → see instructions →
507	  press "Let's go!" → game starts with custom game config applied.
508	- No changes to existing game-engine tests.
509	
510	## Rollout
511	
512	Single PR to master via the existing CI gating. Files likely touched:
513	
514	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/index.tsx`
515	- `src/components/GameCard.tsx`, `src/components/GameGrid.tsx` (or split
516	  into `GameCoverCard` + `Custom gameCoverCard` if the size grows).
517	- `src/components/GameCover.tsx` (new)
518	- `src/components/CoverPicker.tsx` (new — emoji palette + image URL input)
519	- `src/games/cover.ts` (new — `Cover` type + `resolveCover` helper)
520	- `src/db/schemas/saved_game_configs.ts` (add optional `cover` field +
521	  migration bump)
522	- `src/components/config/{ChunkGroup,CellSelect,Stepper,ChipStrip}.tsx`
523	  (new)
524	- `src/games/{word-spell,number-match,sort-numbers}/*SimpleConfigForm.tsx`
525	  (new / replacing today's `SortNumbersConfigForm`)
526	- `src/games/config-chips.ts`, `src/games/simple-labels.ts` (new)
527	- `src/games/registry.ts` (cover field)
528	- `src/games/catalog-utils.ts` (sortByLastPlayed)
529	- `src/components/answer-game/InstructionsOverlay/InstructionsOverlay.tsx`
530	  (content replacement)
531	- Locale updates in `src/lib/i18n/locales/{en,pt-BR}/*.json`
532	- Architecture doc updates (per CLAUDE.md, if game-state logic changes — it
533	  doesn't in this spec, but touched files under `answer-game/` warrant a
534	  review).
535	
536	Update `docs/architecture/*.mdx` co-located docs only if logic in
537	`answer-game/` changes materially during implementation.
538	
539	## Open questions (to resolve in implementation plan)
540	
541	- **Resolved:** editing a game default. Game defaults are **immutable**.
542	  Tapping a default card's cog opens the advanced modal with the default
543	  config pre-filled; the only save action is `Save as new custom game`. There
544	  is no in-place edit of defaults. The "personalized default" feel comes
545	  from a custom game the parent saves once and the kid taps thereafter.
546	- Cog affordance on small screens: whether the cog is always visible in
547	  the card's top-right corner or revealed by long-press. Default:
548	  always visible (a 28×28 tap target is fine alongside the Play button).
549	- Modal presentation on mobile: full-screen sheet vs. centered modal with
550	  backdrop. Default: full-screen sheet on `< sm`, centered dialog above.
551
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:16Z)**_

Now I have a complete picture. Let me check the `as` casts in the resolver more carefully, then look at the output directory and write the findings.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:16Z)**_

Good -- `resolveWordSpellConfig` is called inside `useMemo` at line 490, and the `config.source` object ref is stable until `initial` changes. However, at line 538, `resolveWordSpellConfig(c)` is called on config change and creates a new source object, which is set via `setCfg`. That's fine -- it only triggers when the user actively changes config.

Now, there's the pre-existing issue with `config.source` being an object reference in the useEffect deps. If the config is the same object across renders, no re-trigger. If it's a new object with same values (referential inequality), re-trigger. But this pre-dates this PR (the dep array is unchanged), so it's not a new bug introduced here.

Let me check the `filterSignature` backward compatibility concern more carefully.

The old signature format was:
`region=aus|level=1|levels=|levelRange=|syllableCountEq=|syllableCountRange=|phonemesAllowed=|phonemesRequired=|graphemesAllowed=|graphemesRequired=`

The new signature format adds `|hasVisual=` at the end:
`region=aus|level=1|levels=|levelRange=|syllableCountEq=|syllableCountRange=|phonemesAllowed=|phonemesRequired=|graphemesAllowed=|graphemesRequired=|hasVisual=`

This means all existing seen-words entries stored under the old key format will no longer match the new key format. Any in-memory or persisted seen-words state will be orphaned. Let me check if the store is persisted.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji.md`

```text
1	# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
6	
7	**Architecture:** Add `emoji?` and `image?` to the type chain (`CurriculumEntry` → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound`), add `hasVisual` filtering, tag 7 existing curriculum entries with emojis and add "mum" as the 8th entry, then invert the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` to use a library source with `hasVisual: true` instead of a hardcoded round pool. Remove the hardcoded pool entirely.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Vitest, React (hook tests via `@testing-library/react`)
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`
12	
13	---
14	
15	## File Map
16	
17	| Action | File                                                          | Responsibility                                                                                         |
18	| ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
19	| Modify | `src/data/words/types.ts`                                     | Add `emoji?`, `image?` to `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`; add `hasVisual?` to `WordFilter` |
20	| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:30-86`                              | Add `hasVisual` predicate to `entryMatches`                                                            |
21	| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:156-190`                            | Propagate `emoji`/`image` in `draftToHit` and `joinHits`                                               |
22	| Modify | `src/data/words/adapters.ts:4-6`                              | Map `emoji`/`image` from `WordHit` through to `WordSpellRound`                                         |
23	| Modify | `src/data/words/seen-words.ts:26-44`                          | Add `hasVisual` to `filterSignature`                                                                   |

25	| Modify | `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json`                   | Add `emoji` to `can`, `cat`, `dog`, `sad`, `sun`; add `mum` entry                                      |

149	- [ ] **Step 2: Add `hasVisual` to `filterSignature`**
150	
151	In `src/data/words/seen-words.ts:26-44`, add a `hasVisual` part to the signature array:
152	
153	```typescript
154	export const filterSignature = (filter: WordFilter): string => {
155	  const parts: string[] = [
156	    `region=${filter.region}`,
157	    `level=${filter.level ?? ''}`,
158	    `levels=${sortedCsv(filter.levels?.map(String))}`,
159	    `levelRange=${filter.levelRange ? filter.levelRange.join('-') : ''}`,
160	    `syllableCountEq=${filter.syllableCountEq ?? ''}`,
161	    `syllableCountRange=${
162	      filter.syllableCountRange
163	        ? filter.syllableCountRange.join('-')
164	        : ''
165	    }`,
166	    `phonemesAllowed=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesAllowed)}`,
167	    `phonemesRequired=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesRequired)}`,
168	    `graphemesAllowed=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesAllowed)}`,
169	    `graphemesRequired=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesRequired)}`,
170	    `hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}`,
171	  ];
172	  return parts.join('|');
173	};
174	```

307	- [ ] **Step 3: Update `joinHits` to propagate emoji/image**
308	
309	In `src/data/words/filter.ts:169-190`, add `emoji` and `image` to the returned `WordHit`:
310	
311	```typescript
312	const joinHits = (
313	  curriculum: CurriculumEntry[],
314	  core: Map<string, WordCore>,
315	  region: Region,
316	): WordHit[] =>
317	  curriculum.flatMap((entry) => {
318	    const c = core.get(entry.word);
319	    if (!c) return [];
320	    return [
321	      {
322	        word: entry.word,
323	        region,
324	        level: entry.level,
325	        syllableCount: c.syllableCount,
326	        syllables: c.syllables,
327	        variants: c.variants,
328	        ipa: entry.ipa || undefined,
329	        graphemes: entry.graphemes,
330	        emoji: entry.emoji,
331	        image: entry.image,
332	        provenance: 'shipped',
333	      } as WordHit,
334	    ];
335	  });
336	```

629

633	- Test: `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts`

636	This is the largest task. The picture-mode invariant currently says "picture → rounds defined, source undefined". It must change to "picture default → source with hasVisual:true, rounds undefined" while still honoring saved configs that have explicit rounds.

639

641	
642	```typescript
643	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
644	import { resolveWordSpellConfig } from './$gameId';
645	import type { WordSpellConfig } from '@/games/word-spell/types';
646	
647	const assertRecallInvariant = (cfg: WordSpellConfig) => {
648	  expect(cfg.mode).toBe('recall');
649	  expect(cfg.source).toBeDefined();
650	  expect(cfg.source?.type).toBe('word-library');
651	  expect(cfg.rounds).toBeUndefined();
652	};
653	
654	const assertPictureInvariant = (cfg: WordSpellConfig) => {
655	  expect(cfg.mode).toBe('picture');
656	  expect(cfg.source).toBeDefined();
657	  expect(cfg.source?.type).toBe('word-library');
658	  expect(cfg.source?.filter.hasVisual).toBe(true);
659	  expect(cfg.rounds).toBeUndefined();
660	};
661	
662	describe('mode-default invariant', () => {
663	  it('null saved config → recall + library, no emoji', () => {

665	  });
666	
667	  it('saved with only mode=recall → recall + library', () => {
668	    assertRecallInvariant(
669	      resolveWordSpellConfig({
670	        component: 'WordSpell',
671	        configMode: 'advanced',
672	        mode: 'recall',
673	      }),
674	    );
675	  });
676	
677	  it('saved with only mode=picture → picture + source with hasVisual', () => {
678	    assertPictureInvariant(
679	      resolveWordSpellConfig({
680	        component: 'WordSpell',
681	        configMode: 'advanced',
682	        mode: 'picture',
683	      }),
684	    );
685	  });
686	
687	  it('saved with mode=recall + leaked rounds → rounds dropped', () => {
688	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({
689	      component: 'WordSpell',
690	      configMode: 'advanced',
691	      mode: 'recall',
692	      rounds: [{ word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' }],
693	      source: {
694	        type: 'word-library',
695	        filter: {
696	          region: 'aus',
697	          graphemesAllowed: [{ g: 's', p: 's' }],
698	          phonemesRequired: ['s'],
699	        },
700	      },
701	    });
702	    assertRecallInvariant(cfg);
703	  });
704	
705	  it('saved with mode=picture + explicit rounds → honors saved rounds', () => {
706	    const rounds = [{ word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' }];
707	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({

712	    });

714	    expect(cfg.rounds).toEqual(rounds);
715	    expect(cfg.source).toBeUndefined();
716	  });
717	
718	  it('saved with mode=picture + rounds + leaked source → honors rounds, drops source', () => {

720	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({

730	        },
731	      },
732	    });
733	    expect(cfg.mode).toBe('picture');
734	    expect(cfg.rounds).toEqual(rounds);
735	    expect(cfg.source).toBeUndefined();
736	  });
737	
738	  it('simple-mode saved config always resolves to recall + library', () => {

740	      component: 'WordSpell',
741	      configMode: 'simple',
742	      selectedUnits: [{ g: 's', p: 's' }],
743	      region: 'aus',
744	      inputMethod: 'drag',
745	    });
746	    assertRecallInvariant(cfg);
747	  });
748	});
749	```

835	- [ ] **Step 6: Rewrite the picture-mode branch of `resolveWordSpellConfig`**
836	
837	Replace the picture-mode branch (lines 286-298) with:
838	
839	```typescript
840	// mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
841	// picture with explicit rounds ⇒ honor them, drop source
842	if (Array.isArray(merged.rounds) && merged.rounds.length > 0) {
843	  const { source: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
844	  return rest as WordSpellConfig;
845	}
846	// picture without explicit rounds ⇒ source with hasVisual, drop rounds
847	const picture = { ...merged };
848	if (picture.rounds) {
849	  delete picture.rounds;
850	}
851	if (!picture.source) {
852	  picture.source = {
853	    type: 'word-library',
854	    filter: { region: 'aus', hasVisual: true },
855	  };
856	} else {
857	  picture.source = {
858	    ...picture.source,
859	    filter: { ...picture.source.filter, hasVisual: true },
860	  };
861	}
862	return picture;
863	```

886	### Task 8: Zero-results fallback for hasVisual filtering (R8 fallback)
887	
888	**Files:**
889	
890	- Modify: `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts:65-87`
891	- Test: `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`
892	
893	When `hasVisual: true` in the filter returns zero hits, the game must fall back to audio-only (recall-like) rounds by retrying without `hasVisual`.
894	
895	- [ ] **Step 1: Write a failing test for hasVisual zero-results fallback**
896	
897	In `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`, add this test inside the `useLibraryRounds` describe block:
898	
899	```typescript
900	it('falls back to non-visual rounds when hasVisual filter returns zero hits', async () => {
901	  const config: WordSpellConfig = {
902	    ...baseConfig,
903	    mode: 'picture',
904	    totalRounds: 3,
905	    source: {
906	      type: 'word-library',
907	      // Level 8 has no emoji-tagged words → zero visual hits → fallback triggers
908	      filter: { region: 'aus', level: 8, hasVisual: true },
909	    },
910	  };
911	  const { result } = renderHook(() =>
912	    useLibraryRounds(config, undefined, store),
913	  );
914	  await waitFor(() => {
915	    expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
916	  });
917	  // Fallback retried without hasVisual, so we get level 8 words without emoji
918	  expect(result.current.rounds.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
919	  expect(result.current.rounds.some((r) => !r.emoji && !r.image)).toBe(
920	    true,
921	  );
922	});
923	
924	it('returns visual rounds when hasVisual filter matches enough words', async () => {
925	  const config: WordSpellConfig = {
926	    ...baseConfig,
927	    mode: 'picture',
928	    totalRounds: 2,
929	    source: {
930	      type: 'word-library',
931	      filter: { region: 'aus', hasVisual: true },
932	    },
933	  };
934	  const { result } = renderHook(() =>
935	    useLibraryRounds(config, undefined, store),
936	  );
937	  await waitFor(() => {
938	    expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
939	  });
940	  expect(result.current.rounds).toHaveLength(2);
941	  for (const r of result.current.rounds) {
942	    expect(r.emoji ?? r.image).toBeDefined();
943	  }
944	});
945	```

953	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement fallback in `useLibraryRounds`**
954	
955	In `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`, update the async function inside the `useEffect` (around lines 65-87). After getting initial results, if they're empty and the filter had `hasVisual`, retry without it:
956	
957	```typescript
958	void (async () => {
959	  let activeFilter = source.filter;
960	  let result = await filterWords(activeFilter);
961	  if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
962	
963	  if (result.hits.length === 0 && source.filter.hasVisual) {
964	    const { hasVisual: _, ...fallbackFilter } = source.filter;
965	    activeFilter = fallbackFilter;
966	    result = await filterWords(activeFilter);
967	    if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
968	  }
969	
970	  const limit = source.limit ?? config.totalRounds;
971	  const picked = config.roundsInOrder
972	    ? result.hits.slice(0, limit)
973	    : await pickWithRecycling(
974	        result.hits,
975	        limit,
976	        filterSignature(activeFilter),
977	        store,
978	        seed,
979	      );
980	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition -- isCancelled may be set to true by cleanup between awaits
981	  if (cancellation.isCancelled) return;
982	
983	  setState({
984	    rounds: picked.map((hit) => toWordSpellRound(hit)),
985	    isLoading: false,
986	    usedFallback: result.usedFallback,
987	  });
988	})();
989	```

1035	
1036	## Spec Coverage Matrix
1037	
1038	| Requirement                            | Task(s)                    | Acceptance Example |
1039	| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------ |
1040	| R1 — CurriculumEntry emoji/image       | Task 1                     | AE3                |
1041	| R2 — WordHit emoji/image propagation   | Task 3                     | AE3                |
1042	| R3 — DraftEntry emoji/image            | Task 1                     | —                  |
1043	| R4 — toWordSpellRound maps emoji/image | Task 4                     | AE1                |
1044	| R5 — Existing 7 words get emoji        | Task 5                     | AE1                |
1045	| R6 — "mum" added to curriculum + core  | Task 6                     | —                  |
1046	| R7 — hasVisual filter                  | Task 2                     | AE2                |
1047	| R8 — Picture mode uses library source  | Task 7                     | AE1                |
1048	| R8 — Saved explicit rounds honored     | Task 7 (Step 1, test case) | —                  |
1049	| R8 — Zero-results fallback             | Task 8                     | —                  |
1050	| R9 — Remove hardcoded pool             | Task 7 (Step 4)            | —                  |
1051	
1052	## Outstanding Questions Resolved
1053	
1054	- **"mum" IPA/graphemes:** `/mʌm/` with graphemes `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `m/m`. This follows the same pattern as existing level 2 words with the `ʌ` vowel (e.g. "mug" = `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `g/g`).
1055	- **Default picture-mode filter scope:** Pull from all levels with visuals (no level/grapheme constraint). The default `WordFilter` uses `region: 'aus'` and `hasVisual: true` with no level restriction, so any curriculum word tagged with emoji or image across any level is included.
1056	
1057	## Known Limitations
1058	
1059	- **Region hardcoded to `aus`:** The default picture-mode filter and `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` hardcode `region: 'aus'`. This mirrors the existing behavior (the hardcoded pool was also aus-only), but means adding picture-mode words for other regions requires a code change to the default filter, not just a data edit. Multi-region picture-mode support is out of scope for this pass — when other regions are introduced, the default should derive region from the user's locale or a config setting.
1060
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
---
date: 2026-05-01
topic: consolidate-word-emoji
---

# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data

## Summary

Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields directly into curriculum JSON entries so
that any word in the library can carry a visual. Picture mode switches from a
hardcoded 8-word pool to library-sourced rounds filtered by visual availability.

---

## Problem Frame

WordSpell's picture mode shows an emoji or image alongside each word, but the
visual data lives in a hardcoded array (`WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`) of 8 words in
the route loader — completely disconnected from the curriculum word library. This
means picture mode is locked to those 8 words regardless of what the teacher
configures, and adding new picture-mode words requires a code change rather than
a data edit.

The word library already powers recall mode with rich filtering (region, level,
graphemes, phonemes), but none of that infrastructure is available to picture
mode because the visual data isn't part of the word data.

---

## Requirements

**Data structure**

- R1. `CurriculumEntry` gains optional `emoji?: string` and `image?: string`
  fields. Both are optional — most words will have neither.
- R2. `WordHit` gains matching optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields, populated
  from the curriculum entry during filtering. Specifically, `joinHits` in
  `filter.ts` must propagate `emoji` and `image` from `CurriculumEntry` into
  the constructed `WordHit`, and `draftToHit` must propagate them from
  `DraftEntry`.
- R3. `DraftEntry` gains optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields for authoring
  parity.
- R4. The `toWordSpellRound` adapter maps `emoji` and `image` from `WordHit`
  through to `WordSpellRound`.

**Curriculum data**

- R5. The 7 existing hardcoded emoji words that already have curriculum entries
  (ant, can, cat, dog, pin, sad, sun) gain an `emoji` field in their respective
  `aus/level1.json` or `aus/level2.json` curriculum entries.
- R6. "mum" is added as a new `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` (level 2
  covers graphemes `m` and `u`) with its emoji `🤱`. A corresponding `WordCore`
  entry must also be added to `core/level2.json` (with `word` and
  `syllableCount`) — without it, `joinHits` will silently discard the
  curriculum entry.

**Filtering**

- R7. `WordFilter` gains a `hasVisual?: boolean` option. When `true`,
  `filterWords` returns only words where `emoji` or `image` is set.

**Picture mode behavior**

- R8. When WordSpell is in picture mode, the config resolver uses
  library-sourced rounds with `hasVisual: true` in the filter, instead of
  falling back to the hardcoded pool. This requires inverting the existing mode
  invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` (which currently deletes `source` for
  picture mode) so that picture mode becomes `source` defined with
  `hasVisual: true`. The existing test asserting source is dropped for picture
  mode must be updated to assert the new behavior. If a saved config has
  explicit `rounds` with emoji/image data, those are honored as-is (no library
  fetch). If `hasVisual` filtering returns zero results, the game falls back to
  recall mode (audio-only) rather than displaying a blank screen.
- R9. The hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds` are
  removed.

---

## Acceptance Examples

- AE1. **Covers R4, R5, R8.** Given a WordSpell game in picture mode with
  default config, when the game loads, it pulls rounds from `aus/level2.json`
  (and `level1.json`) curriculum entries that have an `emoji` field set, and the
  emoji is rendered via `EmojiQuestion`.
- AE2. **Covers R7.** Given a `WordFilter` with `hasVisual: true` and
  `region: 'aus'`, when `filterWords` runs, only entries with a non-empty
  `emoji` or `image` field are returned.
- AE3. **Covers R1, R2.** Given a curriculum entry without `emoji` or `image`,
  when it appears in a `WordHit`, `emoji` and `image` are `undefined` and the
  word works normally in recall mode.

---

## Success Criteria

- Picture mode loads rounds from the word library instead of a hardcoded array.
- Adding a new picture-mode word requires only a data edit (adding `emoji` or
  `image` to a curriculum JSON entry), not a code change.
- Existing recall mode and sentence-gap mode are unaffected.

---

## Scope Boundaries

- Tagging additional words beyond the existing 8 with emojis
- Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel
- Emoji display in WordLibraryExplorer cards
- Image upload or asset management
- Multi-region emoji support (only `aus` curriculum entries are tagged in this
  pass)

---

## Key Decisions

- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup:** single source of truth per
  word, no join at query time. Trades separation of concerns for simplicity.
- **Both emoji and image fields:** `WordSpellRound` already supports both; the
  curriculum entry should mirror that capability even though only emoji is used
  today.
- **Auto-filter, not configurable:** picture mode always filters to words with
  visuals. No teacher toggle for text-only fallback in picture mode.
- **No IndexedDB migration:** all new fields are optional. Existing drafts and
  saved configs remain valid without a schema version bump.

---

## Outstanding Questions

### Deferred to Planning

- Affects R6 · Needs research — What are the correct IPA transcription and
  grapheme mappings for "mum" in `aus` region?
- Affects R8 · Technical — Should the default picture-mode `WordFilter` constrain
  to specific levels/graphemes, or pull from all levels that have visuals?
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
