 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-28-wordspell-levels-defaults-design.md`

```text
1	# WordSpell — Levels in Advanced + Defaults Overhaul
2	
3	> Status: **Approved (design)** — implementation plan to follow.
4	> Date: 2026-04-28
5	> Owner: leocaseiro
6	
7	## Background
8	
9	WordSpell exposes two configuration surfaces:
10	
11	- A **Simple** form (`WordSpellSimpleConfigForm`) with Level + Phonemes + Input Method
12	- An **Advanced** modal (`AdvancedConfigModal` driven by `wordSpellConfigFields`)
13	  with eight other fields
14	
15	The two forms don't share fields, so Advanced lacks the most fundamental
16	content controls (which Level, which sounds). Several defaults also fight the
17	synthetic-phonics flow the curriculum is built around. And — discovered while
18	writing this spec — Levels 3+ are effectively broken: a filter-cumulativity
19	mismatch silently drops most words from the playable pool, so the player sees
20	"no rounds" or a tiny pool.
21	
22	This spec covers the user-facing defaults overhaul, the Advanced-form parity
23	fix, the Level 3+ bug fix, and the regression coverage that prevents the bug
24	class from returning.
25	
26	## Goals
27	
28	1. **Fix Level 3+** — cumulative phoneme handling so library queries return
29	   playable word counts at every level.
30	2. **Advanced ⊇ Simple** — every option in the Simple form is also reachable
31	   from the Advanced form. Concretely, Advanced gains:
32	   - **Level** (currently Simple-only)
33	   - **Phonemes / sounds at this level** (currently Simple-only)
34	   - **`distractorCount`** — never exposed in WordSpell's Advanced even though
35	     the game uses distractor mode. NumberMatch already exposes this; WordSpell
36	     forgot to.
37	3. **`recall` becomes the default mode** — picture mode is a scaffold; recall
38	   is the synthetic-phonics target. Library-sourced rounds also have no
39	   image/emoji today, so picture mode silently degrades.
40	4. **Random rounds by default** (`roundsInOrder: false`).
41	5. **Remove the unused `scramble` mode.**
42	6. **Lock the bug class out of the codebase** with parameterized regression
43	   tests that fail loudly if any (region, level) ever drops below the playable
44	   minimum.
45	
46	## Non-Goals
47	
48	- No data migration for existing saved configs that use `mode: 'scramble'` —
49	  pre-launch / dev-only data is treated as throwaway.
50	- No new game modes; `picture`, `recall`, and `sentence-gap` stay.
51	- No curriculum content changes.
52	- No changes to `skin`, `hud`, `timing` exposure (consistent with other games).
53	
54	## Findings (the why)
55	
56	### Level 3+ is silently broken
57	
58	`filterWords` requires that **every** grapheme of a candidate word's phoneme
59	mapping be present in `filter.phonemesAllowed` (`src/data/words/filter.ts`,
60	lines 70–73):
61	
62	```ts
63	if (filter.phonemesAllowed) {
64	  const allowed = new Set(filter.phonemesAllowed);
65	  if (!hit.graphemes.every((g) => allowed.has(g.p))) return false;
66	}
67	```
68	
69	`WordSpellSimpleConfigForm.setLevel(N)` populates `phonemesAllowed` with the
70	phonemes from level `N` only — not levels 1..N (`WordSpellSimpleConfigForm.tsx`
71	lines 39–52, 60–75):
72	
73	```ts
74	const unitsAtLevel = GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[level] ?? [];
75	// ...
76	const setLevel = (n: number) => {
77	  const available = [
78	    ...new Set((GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[n] ?? []).map((u) => u.p)),
79	  ];
80	  // -> phonemesAllowed = level-N phonemes only
81	};
82	```
83	
84	Consequence: a Level 3 word like `bat` uses three phonemes: `/b/` (level 3),
85	`/æ/` (level 1), and `/t/` (level 1). With `phonemesAllowed` containing only
86	level-3 phonemes, `/æ/` and `/t/` are not in the allowed set and the word is
87	rejected. Levels 1 and 2 worked by coincidence (level 2's phoneme set
88	contains enough overlap with level-1 sounds for some words to slip through).
89	Level 3+ effectively yields zero or near-zero hits.
90	
91	The fix is to use `cumulativeGraphemes(level)` (already defined in
92	`src/data/words/levels.ts:130-144`) for both the chip pool and the default
93	`phonemesAllowed` set.
94	
95	### Library rounds have no image / emoji
96	
97	`toWordSpellRound` in `src/data/words/adapters.ts:4-6` only sets `word`. No
98	`emoji`, no `image`. So library-sourced rounds in `picture` mode show nothing
99	visual and silently behave like `recall` — except in `recall` we also disable
100	the placeholder, so the user gets a coherent UX. This is a second reason
101	`recall` is the right default for library-driven gameplay.
102	
103	### Defaults bias toward "training wheels" instead of synthetic-phonics flow
104	
105	| Default                                   | Today                       | Should be        |
106	| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ---------------- |
107	| `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG.mode`          | `picture`                   | `recall`         |
108	| `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG.roundsInOrder` | `true` (deliberate, for VR) | `false` (random) |
109	| `resolveSimpleConfig.mode`                | `scramble`                  | `recall`         |
110	
111	Random rounds were avoided in defaults because the shuffler uses a
112	`crypto.getRandomValues` seed VR tests can't pin. Solution: deterministic
113	seed injection in test mode (see "VR stability" below) — keep the user-facing
114	default truly random.
115	
116	### Advanced form gap
117	
118	| Field                                     | In Advanced today | Action                               |
119	| ----------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------ |
120	| `inputMethod`                             | yes               | keep                                 |
121	| `wrongTileBehavior`                       | yes               | keep                                 |
122	| `tileBankMode`                            | yes               | keep                                 |
123	| `distractorCount`                         | no                | **add** (NumberMatch already has it) |
124	| `totalRounds`                             | yes               | keep                                 |
125	| `roundsInOrder`                           | yes               | keep                                 |
126	| `ttsEnabled`                              | yes               | keep                                 |
127	| `mode`                                    | yes               | keep (drop `scramble`)               |
128	| `tileUnit`                                | yes               | keep                                 |
129	| `source.filter.level` + `phonemesAllowed` | no                | **add** via shared sub-component     |
130	| `skin`                                    | no                | skip — managed by `SkinHarness`      |
131	| `hud` fields                              | no                | skip — not exposed by any game       |
132	| `timing` fields                           | no                | skip — not exposed by any game       |
133	| `slotInteraction`                         | no                | skip — derived from `mode`           |
134	| `touchKeyboardInputMode`                  | no                | skip — derived from `inputMethod`    |
135	
136	### `scramble` mode is unused
137	
138	Reference search shows `scramble` only in defaults, stories, and tests — not
139	in any user-visible flow the team uses. Removing it simplifies the slot-
140	interaction logic in `WordSpell.tsx:505-509` to a single `mode === 'sentence-gap'`
141	check.
142	
143	## Approach
144	
145	### A. Cumulative phonemes
146	
147	In `WordSpellSimpleConfigForm.tsx`, replace `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[level] ?? []`
148	with `cumulativeGraphemes(level)`:
149	
150	- Used to build the chip pool so users can toggle phonemes from levels 1..N.
151	- Used inside `setLevel(N)` to seed `phonemesAllowed` to all phonemes at
152	  levels 1..N (the natural "all sounds learned so far" default).
153	
154	The chip-strip rendering (one chip per unique phoneme, listing all graphemes
155	that teach it) stays the same — it already collapses graphemes-per-phoneme.
156	
157	### B. Shared `WordSpellLibrarySource` sub-component
158	
159	Extract the Level select + Phonemes chip strip from `WordSpellSimpleConfigForm`
160	into a new component:
161	
162	```text
163	src/games/word-spell/WordSpellLibrarySource/
164	├── WordSpellLibrarySource.tsx
165	└── WordSpellLibrarySource.test.tsx
166	```
167	
168	The component owns `config.source` (`type: 'word-library'`, `filter.level`,
169	`filter.phonemesAllowed`, `filter.region`). It exposes the same
170	`{ config, onChange }` props every config form takes.
171	
172	Both forms render it:
173	
174	- `WordSpellSimpleConfigForm` renders `<WordSpellLibrarySource />` then the
175	  Input Method picker.
176	- `AdvancedConfigModal` renders it via a new registry hook
177	  (`getAdvancedHeaderRenderer(gameId)`) above the standard `ConfigFormFields`.
178	
179	### C. Registry hook: `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer`
180	
181	Add a third lookup to `src/games/config-fields-registry.ts`:
182	
183	```ts
184	export const getAdvancedHeaderRenderer = (
185	  gameId: string,
186	): ConfigFormRenderer | undefined => {
187	  switch (gameId) {
188	    case 'word-spell':
189	      return WordSpellLibrarySource;
190	    default:
191	      return undefined;
192	  }
193	};
194	```
195	
196	`AdvancedConfigModal.tsx` consults the hook; when a renderer exists it appears
197	above the existing `<ConfigFormFields>`. Other games are unaffected.
198	
199	### D. Default mode = `recall`
200	
201	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:96-112` →
202	  `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG.mode = 'recall'`.
203	- `src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts:9` → `mode: 'recall'`.
204	- Stories / tests that hardcode `mode: 'picture'` or `mode: 'scramble'`
205	  updated where they describe the default; story scenarios that explicitly
206	  cover `picture` mode keep that mode.
207	
208	### E. Random rounds by default + VR stability
209	
210	- `DEFAULT_WORD_SPELL_CONFIG.roundsInOrder = false`.
211	- The engine's `buildRoundOrder` (or its underlying shuffler) gains a way to
212	  accept a seed. When `import.meta.env.MODE === 'test'` (or, more precisely,
213	  in the Storybook/VR setup file), inject a fixed seed so VR baselines stay
214	  pinned. Production runtime stays truly random.
215	- Plan note: confirm the exact seed-injection point during plan-writing
216	  (likely the nanoid-based shuffle inside the engine; might mean exposing a
217	  `roundOrderSeed` config option used only by tests).
218	- VR baselines re-recorded after the seed mechanism lands.
219	
220	### F. Remove `scramble`
221	
222	- Drop `'scramble'` from `WordSpellConfig['mode']` (`types.ts:38`).
223	- Drop the `scramble` option from `wordSpellConfigFields` mode select
224	  (`types.ts:88-94`).
225	- Simplify `WordSpell.tsx:505-509`:
226	
227	  ```ts
228	  slotInteraction:
229	    resolvedConfig.mode === 'sentence-gap' ? 'free-swap' : 'ordered',
230	  ```
231	
232	- Update or delete stories/tests referencing `scramble`.
233	- No migration: any saved config with `mode: 'scramble'` is throwaway dev data.
234	
235	### G. Add `distractorCount` to Advanced
236	
237	Add a new field with `visibleWhen` so it only appears when the user picks
238	`tileBankMode: 'distractors'`:
239	
240	```ts
241	{
242	  type: 'number',
243	  key: 'distractorCount',
244	  label: 'Distractor count',
245	  min: 1,
246	  max: 10,
247	  visibleWhen: { key: 'tileBankMode', value: 'distractors' },
248	},
249	```
250	
251	## Files Touched
252	
253	| Path                                                                                   | Change                                               |
254	| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
255	| `src/games/word-spell/types.ts`                                                        | Drop `scramble`; add `distractorCount` field         |
256	| `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm.tsx`         | Use cumulative; delegate to `WordSpellLibrarySource` |
257	| **NEW** `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellLibrarySource/WordSpellLibrarySource.tsx`       | Shared Level + Phonemes UI                           |
258	| **NEW** `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellLibrarySource/WordSpellLibrarySource.test.tsx`  | Sub-component tests                                  |
259	| `src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts`                                        | `mode: 'recall'` default                             |
260	| `src/games/config-fields-registry.ts`                                                  | Add `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer`                      |
261	| `src/components/AdvancedConfigModal.tsx`                                               | Render header when registered                        |
262	| `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx`                                             | Defaults: `recall`, `roundsInOrder: false`           |
263	| `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/WordSpell.tsx`                                         | Drop `scramble` branch                               |
264	| Engine round-order shuffler (TBD in plan)                                              | Seed-injection for test mode                         |
265	| Stories / tests referencing `scramble` or old defaults                                 | Update or delete                                     |
266	| **NEW** `src/data/words/curriculum-invariant.test.ts`                                  | Parameterized (region, level) playability test       |
267	| **NEW** `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm/source-emits-playable.test.ts` | Parameterized form-emits-playable test               |
268	| `src/games/word-spell/WordSpell/word-spell-initial-content.test.ts`                    | Update mode references                               |
269	| VR baselines                                                                           | Re-record after seed-injection                       |
270	
271	## Testing Strategy
272	
273	### Layer 1 — Curriculum invariant (the test that would have caught this)
274	
275	`src/data/words/curriculum-invariant.test.ts`. Parameterized over every
276	`(region, level)` in `ALL_REGIONS × {1..8}`:
277	
278	```ts
279	const MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS = 4; // one full round at the default totalRounds
280	
281	describe('curriculum invariant: every level has playable defaults', () => {
282	  for (const region of ALL_REGIONS) {
283	    for (const level of [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]) {
284	      it(`${region} L${level}: cumulative phonemes yield ≥ ${MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS} words`, async () => {
285	        const phonemesAllowed = [
286	          ...new Set(cumulativeGraphemes(level).map((u) => u.p)),
287	        ];
288	        const result = await filterWords({
289	          region,
290	          level,
291	          phonemesAllowed,
292	        });
293	        expect(result.hits.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(
294	          MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS,
295	        );
296	      });
297	    }
298	  }
299	});
300	```
301	
302	This fails loudly if any future change (curriculum content, filter logic,
303	cumulativity helper) breaks the playable contract.
304	
305	### Layer 2 — Form emits playable source
306	
307	`src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm/source-emits-playable.test.ts`.
308	Render the form, simulate `setLevel(N)` for each level, capture the emitted
309	`source.filter`, and assert `filterWords` returns ≥ 1 hit. Catches form-side
310	regressions even when the curriculum is fine.
311	
312	### Layer 3 — Cumulative helper unit test
313	
314	If `cumulativeGraphemes` doesn't already have direct test coverage, add a
315	short test asserting `cumulativeGraphemes(3)` includes phonemes from levels
316	1, 2, and 3 with no duplicates. Cheap, documents intent.
317	
318	### Layer 4 — Existing-test updates
319	
320	- `resolve-simple-config.test.ts`: default mode is `recall`.
321	- `useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`, `WordSpell.test.tsx`,
322	  `word-spell-initial-content.test.ts`: update hardcoded `mode: 'picture'`
323	  / `mode: 'scramble'` references that describe the default. Targeted-mode
324	  scenarios stay unchanged.
325	- New AdvancedConfigModal test: opening the modal for `gameId === 'word-spell'`
326	  shows Level + Phonemes controls.
327	- Skipped: full E2E "play through every level" — too slow, redundant with
328	  Layers 1+2.
329	
330	## Acceptance Criteria
331	
332	- Selecting any level 1..8 in the Simple form produces a playable round
333	  pool (≥ 4 words).
334	- Selecting a level shows the cumulative phoneme chips for levels 1..N,
335	  not just level N.
336	- The Advanced modal for WordSpell shows Level + Phonemes controls at the
337	  top, plus the existing 8 fields, plus a new `distractorCount` field
338	  that appears only when `tileBankMode === 'distractors'`.
339	- Saving from Simple and Advanced both produce equivalent
340	  `WordSpellConfig` objects when the same options are set.
341	- No-config WordSpell launch defaults to `mode: 'recall'`,
342	  `roundsInOrder: false`.
343	- Simple-form launch defaults to `mode: 'recall'`.
344	- No `scramble` references remain in production code; type union is
345	  narrowed to `'picture' | 'recall' | 'sentence-gap'`.
346	- `yarn typecheck`, `yarn lint`, `yarn test` pass.
347	- VR baselines stable (re-recorded once for the new defaults; subsequent
348	  runs match without drift).
349	- Curriculum-invariant test exists and passes for every
350	  `(region, level)`.
351	
352	## Open Questions Deferred to Plan
353	
354	- Exact seed-injection point for the round-order shuffler — confirm by
355	  reading `buildRoundOrder` and the engine `INIT_ROUND` / `RESUME_ROUND`
356	  paths.
357	- Whether to keep `WordSpellSimpleConfigForm` at all once Advanced has
358	  parity, or whether the Simple form remains as a streamlined onboarding
359	  view. Spec assumes the latter (status **quo).**
360
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/db/hooks/useSeenWordsStore.ts`

```text
1	import { useMemo } from 'react';
2	import { useRxDB } from './useRxDB';
3	import type { SeenWordsStore } from '@/data/words';
4	import { createInMemorySeenWordsStore } from '@/data/words';
5	import { ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID } from '@/db/last-session-game-config';
6	
7	/**
8	 * Per-profile seen-words store backed by RxDB. When the database isn't
9	 * ready yet, returns an in-memory store so the first session plays
10	 * correctly; subsequent sessions pick up persisted state once the db
11	 * hydrates.
12	 */
13	export const useSeenWordsStore = (): SeenWordsStore => {
14	  const { db } = useRxDB();
15	  const profileId = ANONYMOUS_PROFILE_ID;
16	
17	  const fallback = useMemo(() => createInMemorySeenWordsStore(), []);
18	
19	  return useMemo<SeenWordsStore>(() => {
20	    if (!db) return fallback;
21	
22	    const docId = (signature: string): string =>
23	      `${profileId}__${signature}`;
24	
25	    return {
26	      get: async (signature) => {
27	        const doc = await db.word_spell_seen_words
28	          .findOne(docId(signature))
29	          .exec();
30	        return new Set(doc?.words);
31	      },
32	      addSeen: async (signature, words) => {
33	        const id = docId(signature);
34	        const existing = await db.word_spell_seen_words
35	          .findOne(id)
36	          .exec();
37	        const merged = [
38	          ...new Set([...(existing?.words ?? []), ...words]),
39	        ];
40	        const now = new Date().toISOString();
41	        await (existing
42	          ? existing.incrementalPatch({
43	              words: merged,
44	              updatedAt: now,
45	            })
46	          : db.word_spell_seen_words.insert({
47	              id,
48	              profileId,
49	              signature,
50	              words: merged,
51	              updatedAt: now,
52	            }));
53	      },
54	      resetSeen: async (signature, words) => {
55	        const id = docId(signature);
56	        const existing = await db.word_spell_seen_words
57	          .findOne(id)
58	          .exec();
59	        const now = new Date().toISOString();
60	        await (existing
61	          ? existing.incrementalPatch({ words, updatedAt: now })
62	          : db.word_spell_seen_words.insert({
63	              id,
64	              profileId,
65	              signature,
66	              words,
67	              updatedAt: now,
68	            }));
69	      },
70	    };
71	  }, [db, fallback, profileId]);
72	};
73
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-28-wordspell-multi-level-units-design.md`

```text
1	# ~~WordSpell — Multi-Select Levels & Per-Unit Phoneme Toggles~~
2	
3	> ⚠️ **SUPERSEDED on 2026-04-29** by
4	> [`2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md`](./2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md).
5	>
6	> The disjoint multi-level model below was retired after testing PR #219
7	> revealed two problems: (a) picking a single chip (e.g. only L4 `sh`)
8	> produced empty word pools because prior-level graphemes weren't in
9	> `graphemesAllowed`; (b) the design didn't address easy-word filtering
10	> for higher-level focus.
11	>
12	> **What survived from this spec:** the `selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[]`
13	> data shape, the `level-unit-selection.ts` helpers (toggle/default), and
14	> the IndexedDB migration (`migrateWordSpellConfig`, schema bumps).
15	>
16	> **What changed:** the filter contract (now cumulative graphemes +
17	> Y phoneme filter), the UI (group-click headers replace native
18	> checkboxes; single rendering for Simple and Advanced), and the
19	> mode-default invariant (recall ⇒ library source; picture ⇒ emoji
20	> rounds; never both).
21	>
22	> Read the new spec for the current decisions. The text below is kept
23	> for historical context.
24	>
25	> ~~Status: **Approved (design)** — implementation plan to follow.~~
26	> Date: 2026-04-28
27	> Owner: leocaseiro
28	
29	## Background
30	
31	PR #219 (issue #216) shipped a cumulative-phonemes Level picker for WordSpell.
32	Mid-review, two issues surfaced:
33	
34	1. **Cumulative chips overwhelm the level focus.** Selecting Level 4 surfaces
35	   every phoneme from L1–L4 as a chip, so the "level" loses pedagogical
36	   meaning — a player on Level 4 should be drilling Level-4 sounds, not
37	   re-cycling through everything they already know.
38	2. **The Level field is a single dropdown.** Players can't say "I want Levels
39	   1, 2, and 4 (skipping 3)." A child mid-progression who hasn't covered L3 yet
40	   has no way to express that.
41	
42	The phoneme data already supports a richer model — most levels have ~6–11
43	distinct `(grapheme, phoneme)` units, and 11 phonemes recur across levels via
44	different graphemes (e.g. `/s/` via `s` at L1 and `c` at L4). Treating each
45	unit as its own toggle, with levels as bulk-select presets, fits both the
46	data shape and the player's mental model.
47	
48	## Goals
49	
50	1. **Disjoint multi-level selection** — players can pick any subset of levels
51	   like `{L1, L2, L4}`. Skipping intermediate levels is allowed.
52	2. **Per-`(grapheme, phoneme)` unit toggles** — each chip / sub-checkbox is an
53	   independent unit. Toggling `s /s/` at L1 does not affect `c /s/` at L4.
54	3. **Level checkboxes as bulk-toggle presets** — checking a level row turns on
55	   all its units; unchecking turns them all off. The checkbox state itself is
56	   tri-state and **derived** from the unit selection.
57	4. **Phoneme-driven filter** — the curriculum's `level` tag is informational
58	   only. A word plays iff every one of its graphemes maps to a `(g, p)` pair
59	   in the player's `selectedUnits`.
60	5. **Last-used selection persists**; first-time launch defaults to `{L1}`.
61	6. **Visual symmetry, two renderings** — Simple form uses chip pills inside
62	   each level row; Advanced modal uses a nested `<ul>` of plain checkboxes.
63	   Same data model, same tri-state behaviour, different styling.
64	
65	## Non-Goals
66	
67	- Curriculum data changes. `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` and the per-level word lists
68	  are unchanged.
69	- Play-Again repeating sequence (issue #220). Tracked separately.
70	- Storybook visual baselines. Stories whose snapshots include the affected
71	  forms will be re-recorded as part of implementation.
72	
73	## What changes vs. PR #219
74	
75	PR #219 introduced the shared `WordSpellLibrarySource` shell and the
76	`getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` registry hook, plus several unrelated polish
77	changes. The architectural pieces survive; the form internals get rewritten:
78	
79	| PR #219 piece                                   | Status                                                           |
80	| ----------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
81	| `WordSpellLibrarySource` component shell + slot | Keep (rewrite internals)                                         |
82	| `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` registry hook       | Keep                                                             |
83	| `cumulativeGraphemes` helper in `levels.ts`     | Keep (used by `graphemePool`)                                    |
84	| `levels.test.ts` (cumulativeGraphemes contract) | Keep                                                             |
85	| `curriculum-invariant.test.ts`                  | **Rewrite** — assert per-level playability instead of cumulative |
86	| `source-emits-playable.test.tsx`                | **Rewrite** — drive the new multi-select widget                  |
87	| `recall` default mode                           | Keep                                                             |
88	| `roundsInOrder: false` default                  | Keep                                                             |
89	| `scramble` mode removed                         | Keep                                                             |
90	| `distractorCount` field in Advanced             | Keep                                                             |
91	| Form's use of `cumulativeGraphemes`             | **Remove** — superseded by per-level unit lookups                |
92	
93	## Selection model
94	
95	The authoritative state is a list of `(grapheme, phoneme)` units the player
96	has opted into:
97	
98	```ts
99	type LevelGraphemeUnit = { g: string; p: string };
100	
101	type WordSpellSimpleConfig = {
102	  configMode: 'simple';
103	  selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[]; // ← replaces { level, phonemesAllowed }
104	  region: Region;
105	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
106	};
107	```
108	
109	Each unit comes from `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` and is uniquely identified by its
110	`(g, p)` pair. The order of units in the array is not significant.
111	
112	### Why `(g, p)` and not just `p`
113	
114	Eleven phonemes appear at multiple levels in the AUS curriculum via different
115	graphemes:
116	
117	| phoneme | graphemes (and levels)            |
118	| ------- | --------------------------------- |
119	| `/s/`   | `s` (L1), `c` (L4)                |
120	| `/f/`   | `f` (L3), `ph` (L4)               |
121	| `/dʒ/`  | `j` (L3), `g` (L4), `dge` (L8)    |
122	| `/w/`   | `w` (L3), `wh` (L4)               |
123	| `/tʃ/`  | `ch` (L4), `tch` (L8)             |
124	| `/eɪ/`  | `ai` (L5), `ay` (L5), `a_e` (L7)  |
125	| `/iː/`  | `ea` (L5), `ee` (L5), `e_e` (L7)  |
126	| `/aɪ/`  | `ie` (L5), `igh` (L5), `i_e` (L7) |
127	| `/oʊ/`  | `oa` (L5), `ow` (L5), `o_e` (L7)  |
128	| `/juː/` | `ew` (L5), `ue` (L5), `u_e` (L7)  |
129	| `/ɔː/`  | `or` (L6), `aw` (L8), `ore` (L8)  |
130	
131	A player who has learned `s` at L1 may not yet recognise `c` as also spelling
132	`/s/`. Toggling them as one unit would conflate two distinct teaching events.
133	The `(g, p)` pair is the smallest unit a player can identify with.
134	
135	## Filter contract — phoneme-only, no level gate
136	
137	`filterWords` derives both fields from `selectedUnits`:
138	
139	```ts
140	const phonemesAllowed = [...new Set(selectedUnits.map((u) => u.p))];
141	const graphemesAllowed = [...new Set(selectedUnits.map((u) => u.g))];
142	```
143	
144	A candidate word plays iff **every** one of its graphemes matches a
145	`(g, p)` pair in `selectedUnits` — equivalent to: the grapheme is in
146	`graphemesAllowed` AND the phoneme it teaches in this word is in
147	`phonemesAllowed`.
148	
149	The curriculum's `level` tag is **not** part of the filter. A word tagged at
150	L4 can play for a player with only L1–L2 selected, provided the word happens
151	to use only L1–L2 phonemes (rare in practice — most "advanced" words feature
152	their level's headline phoneme, which won't be in the player's selection).
153	
154	The existing `WordFilter` shape already supports both `graphemesAllowed` and
155	`phonemesAllowed`. The plan verifies they AND together correctly inside
156	`entryMatches` (`src/data/words/filter.ts:30`).
157	
158	## UI — Simple form
159	
160	Stacked rows, one per level (L1 through L8). Each row contains a tri-state
161	checkbox, a level label, and the level's `(g, p)` units rendered as toggleable
162	chip pills.
163	
164	```text
165	☑ Level 1
166	  [s /s/] [a /æ/] [t /t/] [p /p/] [i /ɪ/] [n /n/]    ← all chips filled
167	
168	▣ Level 2          ← indeterminate (some on, some off)
169	  [m /m/] [d /d/] [g /g/] [o /ɒ/]
170	  [c, k, ck /k/] [e /ɛ/] [u /ʌ/] [r /r/]
171	
172	☐ Level 3          ← unchecked, row dimmed
173	  [b /b/] [h /h/] [f /f/] [l /l/] [j /dʒ/]
174	  [v /v/] [w /w/] [x /ks/] [y /j/] [z /z/] [qu /kw/]
175	```
176	
177	### Interaction
178	
179	- **Click row checkbox** → bulk-toggle every chip in that row.
180	- **Click a chip** → toggle just that `(g, p)` unit.
181	- **Tri-state derivation**: row is `checked` iff every unit in that level is
182	  in `selectedUnits`; `unchecked` iff none are; `indeterminate` otherwise.
183	- **Validation**: `selectedUnits` must be non-empty. The existing "Pick at
184	  least one sound to play." error surfaces when the form would otherwise
185	  emit an empty selection.
186	
187	### Chip composition rules (within one level row)
188	
189	- Multiple graphemes that teach the **same phoneme at the same level** still
190	  collapse into one chip — for example, L2 has `c`, `k`, and `ck` all teaching
191	  `/k/`, so the chip is labelled `c, k, ck /k/`. This preserves the existing
192	  per-level dedup behaviour and keeps the chip strip readable.
193	- Toggling a multi-grapheme chip toggles **all** of its `(g, p)` units as a
194	  single atomic operation (e.g. tapping `c, k, ck /k/` flips all three units
195	  on or off together).
196	- Across levels, multi-grapheme phonemes are separate chips: L1 `s /s/` and
197	  L4 `c /s/` are two independent chips, never collapsed.
198	
199	## UI — Advanced modal
200	
201	Same data model, plain rendering: nested checkbox list. Lives in the existing
202	`WordSpellLibrarySource` registry slot above the standard Advanced fields.
203	
204	```html
205	<ul>
206	  <li>
207	    <input type="checkbox" id="lvl-1" /><label for="lvl-1">
208	      Level 1</label
209	    >
210	    <ul>
211	      <li>
212	        <label><input type="checkbox" /> s /s/</label>
213	      </li>
214	      <li>
215	        <label><input type="checkbox" /> a /æ/</label>
216	      </li>
217	      …
218	    </ul>
219	  </li>
220	  <li>
221	    <input type="checkbox" id="lvl-2" /><label for="lvl-2">
222	      Level 2</label
223	    >
224	    <ul>
225	      <li>
226	        <label><input type="checkbox" /> m /m/</label>
227	      </li>
228	      …
229	    </ul>
230	  </li>
231	  …
232	</ul>
233	```
234	
235	- Top-level checkbox (`#lvl-N`) is the same tri-state as the Simple form's
236	  row checkbox: derived, bulk-toggle.
237	- Each leaf checkbox represents one `(g, p)` unit (or one collapsed
238	  multi-grapheme unit, same rule as Simple).
239	- No chip styling. Plain HTML lists with native checkboxes.
240	
241	The component decides which rendering to use based on a `variant` prop
242	(`'chips' | 'checkbox-tree'`). `WordSpellSimpleConfigForm` passes
243	`variant="chips"`; the registry's `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` returns a
244	wrapper that passes `variant="checkbox-tree"`.
245	
246	## Defaults & persistence
247	
248	- **First launch (no saved config):** `selectedUnits = all units in L1`.
249	  Equivalent to "L1 fully checked, all others unchecked".
250	- **Subsequent launches:** restore the last persisted selection from
251	  `last-session-game-config` (RxDB-backed; existing infrastructure).
252	- **Deep-link route loads** still default to L1 for first-time visitors —
253	  the per-route loader does not consult last-session state for the no-config
254	  fallback.
255	- `resolveSimpleConfig` builds `source.filter` from `selectedUnits` by
256	  computing `phonemesAllowed` and `graphemesAllowed` as derived fields.
257	
258	## Component shape
259	
260	`WordSpellLibrarySource` (existing file, internals rewritten):
261	
262	```ts
263	type Variant = 'chips' | 'checkbox-tree';
264	
265	type Props = {
266	  config: Record<string, unknown>;
267	  onChange: (config: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
268	  variant?: Variant; // default 'chips'
269	};
270	```
271	
272	The component:
273	
274	- Reads `config.selectedUnits` (or applies the default `{L1}` selection if
275	  absent).
276	- Renders one row per level 1–8.
277	- Within each row, computes the chip / sub-checkbox set from
278	  `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[level]`, applying the same-phoneme-same-level dedup.
279	- Derives the row checkbox state from the intersection between
280	  `selectedUnits` and the level's units.
281	- Dispatches `onChange` with an updated `selectedUnits` array on every
282	  toggle.
283	
284	A small helper file `level-unit-selection.ts` (or co-located inside the
285	component file) holds the pure functions:
286	
287	```ts
288	type Tri = 'checked' | 'unchecked' | 'indeterminate';
289	
290	export const triStateForLevel = (
291	  level: number,
292	  selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[],
293	): Tri;
294	
295	export const toggleLevel = (
296	  level: number,
297	  selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[],
298	  next: 'checked' | 'unchecked',
299	): LevelGraphemeUnit[];
300	
301	export const toggleUnit = (
302	  unit: LevelGraphemeUnit,
303	  selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[],
304	): LevelGraphemeUnit[];
305	
306	export const defaultSelection = (): LevelGraphemeUnit[]; // → L1 units
307	```
308	
309	Pure functions; trivially unit-testable.
310	
311	## IndexedDB migration
312	
313	Existing players have WordSpell configs persisted in IndexedDB under the old
314	shape (`source.filter.{level, phonemesAllowed}` for full configs;
315	`{configMode: 'simple', level, phonemesAllowed}` for simple configs). The
316	new code reads `selectedUnits`. Without a migration, those rows would either
317	crash the form on read or fall back to defaults — both unacceptable per
318	"don't break current games."
319	
320	### Affected collections
321	
322	- `saved_game_configs` — schema **bumps to v3**. Holds the resume-last-session
323	  row (id `last:anonymous:<gameId>`) plus any legacy named saves not yet
324	  migrated to `custom_games`.
325	- `custom_games` — schema **bumps from v0 to v1** (first migration on this
326	  collection). Holds the user's named custom games. Same `config: object`
327	  shape as `saved_game_configs`.
328	
329	`session_history_index` is NOT affected — it stores resolved rounds and round
330	order, not the simple config.
331	
332	### Migration strategy (shared by both collections)
333	
334	A pure helper, `migrateWordSpellConfig`, transforms a single doc's `config`
335	field. The RxDB `migrationStrategies[N]` per collection wraps it.
336	
337	```ts
338	// src/db/migrations/word-spell-multi-level.ts
339	import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL } from '@/data/words';
340	import type { LevelGraphemeUnit } from '@/data/words';
341	
342	const buildSelectedUnits = (
343	  level: number,
344	  phonemesAllowed: readonly string[],
345	): LevelGraphemeUnit[] => {
346	  const units: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
347	  // Scan levels 1..N (covers both pre-fix and cumulative-fix shapes from PR #219)
348	  for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= level; lvl++) {
349	    for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
350	      if (phonemesAllowed.includes(u.p)) units.push(u);
351	    }
352	  }
353	  return units;
354	};
355	
356	export const migrateWordSpellConfig = (
357	  doc: Record<string, unknown>,
358	): Record<string, unknown> => {
359	  if (doc.gameId !== 'word-spell') return doc;
360	
361	  const config = (doc.config as Record<string, unknown>) ?? {};
362	  if (Array.isArray(config.selectedUnits)) return doc; // already new shape
363	
364	  // Simple-shape: { configMode: 'simple', level, phonemesAllowed }
365	  if (
366	    config.configMode === 'simple' &&
367	    typeof config.level === 'number' &&
368	    Array.isArray(config.phonemesAllowed)
369	  ) {
370	    return {
371	      ...doc,
372	      config: {
373	        ...config,
374	        selectedUnits: buildSelectedUnits(
375	          config.level,
376	          config.phonemesAllowed as string[],
377	        ),
378	      },
379	    };
380	  }
381	
382	  // Full-shape: { source: { filter: { level, phonemesAllowed } } }
383	  const source = config.source as
384	    | { filter?: { level?: number; phonemesAllowed?: string[] } }
385	    | undefined;
386	  if (
387	    source?.filter &&
388	    typeof source.filter.level === 'number' &&
389	    Array.isArray(source.filter.phonemesAllowed)
390	  ) {
391	    return {
392	      ...doc,
393	      config: {
394	        ...config,
395	        selectedUnits: buildSelectedUnits(
396	          source.filter.level,
397	          source.filter.phonemesAllowed,
398	        ),
399	      },
400	    };
401	  }
402	
403	  // Unrecognised shape (e.g. hand-authored rounds with no source) — leave alone.
404	  return doc;
405	};
406	```
407	
408	### Schema bumps
409	
410	In `src/db/create-database.ts`:
411	
412	```ts
413	saved_game_configs: {
414	  schema: savedGameConfigsSchema, // version: 3
415	  migrationStrategies: {
416	    1: ...existing,
417	    2: ...existing,
418	    3: migrateWordSpellConfig,
419	  },
420	},
421	custom_games: {
422	  schema: customGamesSchema, // version: 1
423	  migrationStrategies: {
424	    1: migrateWordSpellConfig,
425	  },
426	},
427	```
428	
429	The schema files (`savedGameConfigsSchema`, `customGamesSchema`) are updated
430	to set `version: 3` / `version: 1` respectively. The `config` field stays
431	typed as `Record<string, unknown>` — no schema-level field changes needed.
432	
433	### Belt-and-suspenders: tolerant reader
434	
435	`resolveSimpleConfig` and the new component's selection-derivation also
436	accept the legacy shape at read-time, falling back to `buildSelectedUnits`
437	if `selectedUnits` is missing. This protects against:
438	
439	- A doc that escaped the migration (e.g. test fixtures, mocked DBs).
440	- Future schemas where the migration runs lazily.
441	
442	In production code: prefer `selectedUnits`; fall back to deriving from
443	`level + phonemesAllowed`; default to L1 only if neither is present.
444	
445	### Migration tests
446	
447	`src/db/migrations/word-spell-multi-level.test.ts`:
448	
449	- Migrates a simple-shape doc (`level=4`, `phonemesAllowed=['s','m']`) →
450	  `selectedUnits` contains every `(g, /s/)` unit and every `(g, /m/)` unit
451	  in L1–L4.
452	- Migrates a full-shape doc (`source.filter.{level, phonemesAllowed}`) →
453	  same result.
454	- Idempotent: a doc that already has `selectedUnits` is returned unchanged.
455	- Non-WordSpell doc returned unchanged.
456	- Hand-authored full config (no `source`) returned unchanged.
457	- Edge case: `phonemesAllowed=[]` → empty `selectedUnits` (the form will
458	  flag this as invalid on read, prompting the user to pick at least one).
459	- Cross-level phoneme: `phonemesAllowed=['/s/']` at `level=4` produces both
460	  `{g:'s',p:'s'}` (from L1) **and** `{g:'c',p:'s'}` (from L4) — preserves
461	  the player's previous reach.
462	
463	`src/db/migrate-collections.test.ts` (or extension of the existing
464	collection-migration tests):
465	
466	- Insert a v2 `saved_game_configs` doc with the legacy shape; bump to v3;
467	  re-read; assert `selectedUnits` is present and correct.
468	- Same for `custom_games` v0 → v1.
469	
470	## Testing strategy
471	
472	Three layers, mirroring the previous spec's discipline:
473	
474	1. **Pure-logic unit tests** (`level-unit-selection.test.ts`)
475	   - `triStateForLevel` returns `checked` / `unchecked` / `indeterminate`
476	     correctly for empty, full, and partial selections.
477	   - `toggleLevel` adds / removes exactly the level's units.
478	   - `toggleUnit` toggles one unit, preserves others.
479	   - `defaultSelection` returns the L1 unit set.
480	2. **Component tests** (`WordSpellLibrarySource.test.tsx`)
481	   - Renders 8 level rows in `chips` mode.
482	   - Renders 8 level rows in `checkbox-tree` mode.
483	   - Tri-state checkbox reflects current selection.
484	   - Clicking a row checkbox bulk-toggles its chips.
485	   - Clicking a chip toggles one unit and updates the row checkbox.
486	   - L1 `s /s/` and L4 `c /s/` are independent chips.
487	   - Multi-grapheme dedup: L2 chip labelled `c, k, ck /k/` toggles all three
488	     graphemes atomically.
489	   - Empty `selectedUnits` surfaces the "Pick at least one sound to play."
490	     error.
491	3. **Curriculum invariant test**
492	   `src/data/words/curriculum-invariant.test.ts` rewritten to:
493	   - Parameterise over every `(region, level)`.
494	   - Build `selectedUnits` from that level's units alone.
495	   - Assert `filterWords` returns ≥ `MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS` (currently 4) hits.
496	   - Confirms each level is independently playable; previous version asserted
497	     cumulative selection only.
498	4. **Form-emits-playable** `source-emits-playable.test.tsx` rewritten to:
499	   - Render the new multi-select widget.
500	   - Toggle levels via the row checkbox; assert `source.filter` is well-formed.
501	   - Toggle individual chips; assert phonemes / graphemes match.
502	   - Verify the L1-only default produces non-empty `filterWords` results.
503	
504	E2E coverage isn't expanded — the form-driven tests above exercise the
505	contract end-to-end up through `filterWords`. If a regression slips through,
506	the curriculum-invariant test catches the data side and the component test
507	catches the UI side.
508	
509	## Risk & mitigation
510	
511	**Saved Custom Games under the old shape break on read.** Addressed by the
512	RxDB migration above: `saved_game_configs` v3 and `custom_games` v1 both run
513	`migrateWordSpellConfig` on every legacy doc, populating `selectedUnits` from
514	the saved `level + phonemesAllowed`. `resolveSimpleConfig` also has a
515	read-time coercion fallback as belt-and-suspenders.
516	
517	**Visual regression in Storybook.** Plan re-records affected baselines and
518	includes a manual diff review step.
519	
520	**Performance — `filterWords` runs without a level prefilter.** The phoneme
521	test is fast; the existing chunked indexes already load lazily. Plan
522	benchmarks if any test exceeds 2s.
523	
524	**Tri-state derivation drift when `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` changes.** Derived
525	from data on every render; no cached state.
526	
527	## Implementation order (preview — full plan separate)
528	
529	1. Add `level-unit-selection.ts` pure helpers + tests
530	2. Add `migrateWordSpellConfig` helper + unit tests; bump
531	   `saved_game_configs` schema to v3 and `custom_games` schema to v1; wire
532	   the migration strategies in `create-database.ts`; collection-level
533	   migration tests
534	3. Rewrite `WordSpellLibrarySource` with the `chips` variant; tests
535	4. Add the `checkbox-tree` variant; tests
536	5. Wire `getAdvancedHeaderRenderer` to pass `variant="checkbox-tree"`
537	6. Update `WordSpellSimpleConfig` and `resolveSimpleConfig` for
538	   `selectedUnits` (with read-time fallback to `level + phonemesAllowed`)
539	7. Rewrite `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` and `source-emits-playable.test.tsx`
540	8. Hook last-session-game-config restore for the simple form (verifies the
541	   migrated row drives the new UI on first read)
542	9. Re-record affected Storybook baselines; run VR
543	10. Manual smoke test in dev server, including a pre-seeded legacy doc that
544	    must round-trip through the migration without crashing or losing the
545	    user's selection
546	
547	The plan elaborates each step into red-green TDD pairs with the exact files
548	to touch.
549
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
176	  region: Region,
177	): WordHit[] =>
178	  curriculum.flatMap((entry) => {
179	    const c = core.get(entry.word);
180	    if (!c) return [];
181	    return [
182	      {
183	        word: entry.word,
184	        region,
185	        level: entry.level,
186	        syllableCount: c.syllableCount,
187	        syllables: c.syllables,
188	        variants: c.variants,
189	        ipa: entry.ipa || undefined,
190	        graphemes: entry.graphemes,
191	        emoji: entry.emoji,

193	        provenance: 'shipped',
194	      } as WordHit,
195	    ];
196	  });
197	
198	const sortByWord = (hits: WordHit[]): WordHit[] =>
199	  hits.toSorted((a, b) => a.word.localeCompare(b.word));

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:23Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-23-word-authoring-design.md`

```text
1	# Word Authoring (Make Up New Words) — Design
2	
3	> **Status:** Draft v1 · **Owner:** @leocaseiro · **Region focus:** AUS only (v1) · **Builds on:** [2026-04-11-phonics-word-library-design.md](./2026-04-11-phonics-word-library-design.md) (P2 evolution)
4	> **Scope:** Curator-only authoring of new words via the in-app `WordLibraryExplorer`. Drafts persist to IndexedDB so authoring works on the deployed GitHub Pages build, on mobile, or in dev. A separate `yarn words:import` CLI merges exports into the canonical curriculum JSON files for PR review.
5	
6	## 1. Goal
7	
8	Today the `WordLibraryExplorer` is read-only. Searching for a word that isn't in the seeded corpus (~650 AUS words) silently returns no results. The curator (currently the project owner) can't add the missing word from inside the app — they'd have to hand-edit two JSON files, compute IPA + grapheme alignment manually, and pick the right level file by hand.
9	
10	This spec adds a **"Make up word"** flow inside the explorer that:
11	
12	1. Auto-generates an IPA + syllables + grapheme→phoneme breakdown for any typed word, using RitaJS for phonemes/syllables and a greedy aligner that learns from the existing curriculum.
13	2. Lets the curator review/edit the breakdown in a focused authoring UI.
14	3. Saves the result as a draft in IndexedDB, viewable alongside shipped words in the explorer.
15	4. Exports drafts as a portable JSON file the curator can carry to their dev machine.
16	5. Provides a Node CLI (`yarn words:import`) that merges exported drafts into the canonical `core/levelN.json` and `curriculum/aus/levelN.json` files for PR review.
17	
18	The corpus grows from real authoring sessions (including impromptu ones during demos), without forcing the curator back to a code editor for every entry.
19	
20	## 2. Project context
21	
22	This is the **P2 evolution** of the original phonics word library design. The earlier P2 sketch ("Storybook form + Vite middleware") is superseded by this spec. Key shifts from the original sketch:
23	
24	| Original P2 sketch                 | This spec                                                |
25	| ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
26	| Storybook-only authoring form      | In-app authoring inside `WordLibraryExplorer`            |
27	| Vite middleware writes JSON in dev | IndexedDB drafts + portable export + offline CLI import  |
28	| Dev-machine only                   | Works in GH Pages prod, on mobile, anywhere the app runs |
29	| Manual breakdown entry             | Auto-generated breakdown via RitaJS + greedy aligner     |
30	
31	**P3 (user word bags for parents/teachers)** remains a separate, deferred spec. It will reuse this spec's `engine.ts`, `aligner.ts`, and `draftStore.ts` modules — they are designed with that reuse in mind.
32	
33	## 3. User flow
34	
35	### 3.1 Trigger points
36	
37	Two ways to enter the authoring flow:
38	
39	1. **Empty-state CTA** — when search returns 0 hits, the empty state shows _"No matches for **`<word>`**. ✨ Make up this word?"_ Clicking opens the authoring modal pre-filled with the typed word.
40	2. **Always-visible button** — a `+ New word` button next to the search bar opens the authoring modal with an empty word field.
41	
42	### 3.2 Authoring modal
43	
44	Presentation: centered modal with backdrop on desktop; full-screen on mobile (no visible backdrop, feels like a page). ESC / system back closes after a confirm-discard prompt if the form is dirty.
45	
46	Top-to-bottom layout:
47	
48	1. **Word field** — pre-filled if entered via empty-state CTA; editing it re-runs the engine (debounced 400 ms).
49	2. **Unknown-word banner** — appears iff `RiTa.isKnownWord(word) === false`:
50	   _"RitaJS doesn't know **`<word>`**. Look it up → [Open in dictionary.com](https://www.dictionary.com/browse/<word>)"_ (opens in a new tab). When this banner shows, IPA and graphemes are **not pre-filled** — curator types IPA manually, then the aligner runs on the manual phonemes.
51	3. **IPA field** — text input, free-edit. Pre-filled from RitaJS when known.
52	4. **Syllable chips** — `[put][·][ting]` with drag-to-move split markers.
53	5. **Level selector** — dropdown 1–8. Auto-suggested from highest grapheme level present in the alignment (using existing `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL`). Label shows _"(suggested L3 — highest grapheme used: sh)"_. Curator can override.
54	6. **Grapheme breakdown row** — horizontal chips, each showing `g` over `p`. Tap a chip to open an inline editor with:
55	   - Letter-range adjusters (extend/shrink the grapheme).
56	   - Phoneme picker (dropdown sourced from existing `PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA` inventory).
57	   - Low-confidence chips (greedy aligner couldn't find a strong corpus match) get an amber border.
58	7. **Variants** — optional comma-separated input.
59	8. **Save draft** / **Cancel** buttons. `Save draft` is disabled (with an inline hint) if the typed word already exists in shipped data — see §5.2.
60	
61	### 3.3 Save → draft visible in explorer
62	
63	`Save draft` writes to IndexedDB. The modal closes; a toast confirms _"Draft saved: `<word>`"_. The word now appears in subsequent search results, sorted naturally and badged `✏️ draft (unsynced)` distinct from shipped words' `📚 shipped` badge.
64	
65	### 3.4 Drafts management
66	
67	A `Drafts (N)` link in the explorer header opens a drafts panel listing all unsynced drafts (word · level · last edited time · `[Edit]` · `[Delete]`). At the top of the panel: an **`Export drafts (N)`** button that downloads `wordlib-export-<ISO timestamp>.json` via `URL.createObjectURL`.
68	
69	### 3.5 Sync: bringing drafts into the canonical corpus
70	
71	The export → import loop is intentionally manual and explicit:
72	
73	1. Curator clicks **Export drafts** (in any environment — prod, mobile, dev). Browser downloads a JSON file.
74	2. Curator transfers the file to their dev machine (AirDrop, email-to-self, iCloud Drive, etc.).
75	3. On their dev machine inside the project repo:
76	
77	   ```bash
78	   yarn words:import ~/Downloads/wordlib-export-2026-04-23T22-14-00Z.json
79	   ```
80
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import { inputMethodLabel } from './simple-labels';
2	import type { InputMethod } from './simple-labels';
3	
4	const chipsForWordSpell = (
5	  config: Record<string, unknown>,
6	): string[] => {
7	  const source = config.source as
8	    | {
9	        filter?: {
10	          level?: number;
11	          phonemesAllowed?: string[];
12	          graphemesAllowed?: { g: string; p: string }[];
13	        };
14	      }
15	    | undefined;
16	  const filter = source?.filter;
17	  const chips: string[] = [];
18	  if (typeof filter?.level === 'number') {
19	    chips.push(`Level ${filter.level}`);
20	  }
21	  const phonemes = filter?.phonemesAllowed;
22	  const graphemes = filter?.graphemesAllowed?.map((u) => u.g);
23	  const sounds = phonemes ?? graphemes ?? [];
24	  if (sounds.length > 0) {
25	    const shown = sounds.slice(0, 3).join(', ');
26	    chips.push(sounds.length > 3 ? `${shown}…` : shown);
27	  }
28	  return chips;
29	};
30	
31	const chipsForNumberMatch = (
32	  config: Record<string, unknown>,
33	): string[] => {
34	  const chips: string[] = [];
35	  if (typeof config.mode === 'string') {
36	    chips.push(config.mode.replace('-to-', ' → ').replaceAll('-', ' '));
37	  }
38	  const range = config.range as
39	    | { min?: number; max?: number }
40	    | undefined;
41	  if (
42	    range &&
43	    typeof range.min === 'number' &&
44	    typeof range.max === 'number'
45	  ) {
46	    chips.push(`${range.min}–${range.max}`);
47	  }
48	  return chips;
49	};
50	
51	const chipsForSortNumbers = (
52	  config: Record<string, unknown>,
53	): string[] => {
54	  const chips: string[] = [];
55	  if (config.direction === 'ascending') chips.push('🚀 Up');
56	  else if (config.direction === 'descending') chips.push('🛝 Down');
57	  if (typeof config.quantity === 'number') {
58	    chips.push(`${config.quantity} numbers`);
59	  }
60	  const skip = config.skip as { step?: number } | undefined;
61	  if (skip && typeof skip.step === 'number') {
62	    chips.push(`${skip.step}s`);
63	  }
64	  return chips;
65	};
66	
67	export const configToChips = (
68	  gameId: string,
69	  config: Record<string, unknown>,
70	): string[] => {
71	  const chips = (() => {
72	    switch (gameId) {
73	      case 'word-spell': {
74	        return chipsForWordSpell(config);
75	      }
76	      case 'number-match': {
77	        return chipsForNumberMatch(config);
78	      }
79	      case 'sort-numbers': {
80	        return chipsForSortNumbers(config);
81	      }
82	      default: {
83	        return [];
84	      }
85	    }
86	  })();
87	
88	  const input = config.inputMethod as InputMethod | undefined;
89	  if (input === 'drag' || input === 'type' || input === 'both') {
90	    const { emoji, label } = inputMethodLabel(input);
91	    chips.push(`${emoji} ${label}`);
92	  }
93	  return chips;
94	};
95

1	export type HomeScreenCardSortRow =
2	  | { kind: 'default'; index: number; bookmarked: boolean }

4	
5	export const compareHomeScreenCardRows = (
6	  a: HomeScreenCardSortRow,
7	  b: HomeScreenCardSortRow,
8	): number => {
9	  const byBookmark = Number(b.bookmarked) - Number(a.bookmarked);
10	  if (byBookmark !== 0) return byBookmark;
11	  if (a.kind !== b.kind) return a.kind === 'default' ? -1 : 1;
12	  return a.index - b.index;
13	};

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:30Z)**_

So the `SeenWordsStore` is backed by RxDB. The `filterSignature` change means all existing persisted seen-words entries (keyed by the old format without `|hasVisual=` at the end) will be orphaned. New requests will generate signatures with `|hasVisual=` appended, which won't match any existing entries. This means users will lose their "seen words" tracking -- the recycling algorithm will treat all words as unseen.

This is a one-time data migration issue. The impact is that after upgrade, the seen-words recycling will reset -- users might see recently-seen words again. It's not a crash or functional failure, just a behavioral regression. However, this is a known consequence of adding fields to `filterSignature` and has likely been acceptable in the past when other fields were added. Let me check if any of the existing fields were added after the initial implementation.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`

```text
1	---
2	date: 2026-05-01
3	topic: consolidate-word-emoji
4	---
5	
6	# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data
7	
8	## Summary
9	
10	Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields directly into curriculum JSON entries so
11	that any word in the library can carry a visual. Picture mode switches from a
12	hardcoded 8-word pool to library-sourced rounds filtered by visual availability.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Problem Frame
17	
18	WordSpell's picture mode shows an emoji or image alongside each word, but the
19	visual data lives in a hardcoded array (`WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`) of 8 words in
20	the route loader — completely disconnected from the curriculum word library. This
21	means picture mode is locked to those 8 words regardless of what the teacher
22	configures, and adding new picture-mode words requires a code change rather than
23	a data edit.
24	
25	The word library already powers recall mode with rich filtering (region, level,
26	graphemes, phonemes), but none of that infrastructure is available to picture
27	mode because the visual data isn't part of the word data.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## Requirements
32	
33	**Data structure**
34	
35	- R1. `CurriculumEntry` gains optional `emoji?: string` and `image?: string`
36	  fields. Both are optional — most words will have neither.
37	- R2. `WordHit` gains matching optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields, populated
38	  from the curriculum entry during filtering. Specifically, `joinHits` in
39	  `filter.ts` must propagate `emoji` and `image` from `CurriculumEntry` into
40	  the constructed `WordHit`, and `draftToHit` must propagate them from
41	  `DraftEntry`.
42	- R3. `DraftEntry` gains optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields for authoring
43	  parity.
44	- R4. The `toWordSpellRound` adapter maps `emoji` and `image` from `WordHit`
45	  through to `WordSpellRound`.
46	
47	**Curriculum data**
48	
49	- R5. The 7 existing hardcoded emoji words that already have curriculum entries
50	  (ant, can, cat, dog, pin, sad, sun) gain an `emoji` field in their respective
51	  `aus/level1.json` or `aus/level2.json` curriculum entries.
52	- R6. "mum" is added as a new `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` (level 2
53	  covers graphemes `m` and `u`) with its emoji `🤱`. A corresponding `WordCore`
54	  entry must also be added to `core/level2.json` (with `word` and
55	  `syllableCount`) — without it, `joinHits` will silently discard the
56	  curriculum entry.
57	
58	**Filtering**
59	
60	- R7. `WordFilter` gains a `hasVisual?: boolean` option. When `true`,
61	  `filterWords` returns only words where `emoji` or `image` is set.
62	
63	**Picture mode behavior**
64	
65	- R8. When WordSpell is in picture mode, the config resolver uses
66	  library-sourced rounds with `hasVisual: true` in the filter, instead of
67	  falling back to the hardcoded pool. This requires inverting the existing mode
68	  invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` (which currently deletes `source` for
69	  picture mode) so that picture mode becomes `source` defined with
70	  `hasVisual: true`. The existing test asserting source is dropped for picture
71	  mode must be updated to assert the new behavior. If a saved config has
72	  explicit `rounds` with emoji/image data, those are honored as-is (no library
73	  fetch). If `hasVisual` filtering returns zero results, the game falls back to
74	  recall mode (audio-only) rather than displaying a blank screen.
75	- R9. The hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds` are
76	  removed.
77	
78	---
79	
80	## Acceptance Examples
81	
82	- AE1. **Covers R4, R5, R8.** Given a WordSpell game in picture mode with
83	  default config, when the game loads, it pulls rounds from `aus/level2.json`
84	  (and `level1.json`) curriculum entries that have an `emoji` field set, and the
85	  emoji is rendered via `EmojiQuestion`.
86	- AE2. **Covers R7.** Given a `WordFilter` with `hasVisual: true` and
87	  `region: 'aus'`, when `filterWords` runs, only entries with a non-empty
88	  `emoji` or `image` field are returned.
89	- AE3. **Covers R1, R2.** Given a curriculum entry without `emoji` or `image`,
90	  when it appears in a `WordHit`, `emoji` and `image` are `undefined` and the
91	  word works normally in recall mode.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## Success Criteria
96	
97	- Picture mode loads rounds from the word library instead of a hardcoded array.
98	- Adding a new picture-mode word requires only a data edit (adding `emoji` or
99	  `image` to a curriculum JSON entry), not a code change.
100	- Existing recall mode and sentence-gap mode are unaffected.
101	
102	---
103	
104	## Scope Boundaries
105	
106	- Tagging additional words beyond the existing 8 with emojis
107	- Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel
108	- Emoji display in WordLibraryExplorer cards
109	- Image upload or asset management
110	- Multi-region emoji support (only `aus` curriculum entries are tagged in this
111	  pass)
112	
113	---
114	
115	## Key Decisions
116	
117	- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup:** single source of truth per
118	  word, no join at query time. Trades separation of concerns for simplicity.
119	- **Both emoji and image fields:** `WordSpellRound` already supports both; the
120	  curriculum entry should mirror that capability even though only emoji is used
121	  today.
122	- **Auto-filter, not configurable:** picture mode always filters to words with
123	  visuals. No teacher toggle for text-only fallback in picture mode.
124	- **No IndexedDB migration:** all new fields are optional. Existing drafts and
125	  saved configs remain valid without a schema version bump.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Outstanding Questions
130	
131	### Deferred to Planning
132	
133	- Affects R6 · Needs research — What are the correct IPA transcription and
134	  grapheme mappings for "mum" in `aus` region?
135	- Affects R8 · Technical — Should the default picture-mode `WordFilter` constrain
136	  to specific levels/graphemes, or pull from all levels that have visuals?
137
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:36Z)**_

```text
1	# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
6	
7	**Architecture:** Add `emoji?` and `image?` to the type chain (`CurriculumEntry` → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound`), add `hasVisual` filtering, tag 7 existing curriculum entries with emojis and add "mum" as the 8th entry, then invert the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` to use a library source with `hasVisual: true` instead of a hardcoded round pool. Remove the hardcoded pool entirely.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Vitest, React (hook tests via `@testing-library/react`)
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`
12	
13	---
14	
15	## File Map
16	
17	| Action | File                                                          | Responsibility                                                                                         |
18	| ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
19	| Modify | `src/data/words/types.ts`                                     | Add `emoji?`, `image?` to `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`; add `hasVisual?` to `WordFilter` |
20	| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:30-86`                              | Add `hasVisual` predicate to `entryMatches`                                                            |
21	| Modify | `src/data/words/filter.ts:156-190`                            | Propagate `emoji`/`image` in `draftToHit` and `joinHits`                                               |
22	| Modify | `src/data/words/adapters.ts:4-6`                              | Map `emoji`/`image` from `WordHit` through to `WordSpellRound`                                         |
23	| Modify | `src/data/words/seen-words.ts:26-44`                          | Add `hasVisual` to `filterSignature`                                                                   |
24	| Modify | `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level1.json`                   | Add `emoji` to `ant`, `pin` entries                                                                    |
25	| Modify | `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json`                   | Add `emoji` to `can`, `cat`, `dog`, `sad`, `sun`; add `mum` entry                                      |
26	| Modify | `src/data/words/core/level2.json`                             | Add `mum` `WordCore` entry                                                                             |
27	| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-298`             | Invert picture-mode invariant, remove `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds`               |

31	| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts` | Update picture-mode tests                                                                              |
32	| Modify | `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/mode-default-invariant.test.ts` | Update picture-mode invariant assertions                                                               |
33	| Modify | `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`              | Test hasVisual fallback and emoji propagation                                                          |

35	---
36

131	
132	```typescript
133	export interface WordFilter {
134	  region: Region;

148	
149	- [ ] **Step 2: Add `hasVisual` to `filterSignature`**
150	
151	In `src/data/words/seen-words.ts:26-44`, add a `hasVisual` part to the signature array:
152	
153	```typescript
154	export const filterSignature = (filter: WordFilter): string => {
155	  const parts: string[] = [
156	    `region=${filter.region}`,
157	    `level=${filter.level ?? ''}`,
158	    `levels=${sortedCsv(filter.levels?.map(String))}`,
159	    `levelRange=${filter.levelRange ? filter.levelRange.join('-') : ''}`,
160	    `syllableCountEq=${filter.syllableCountEq ?? ''}`,
161	    `syllableCountRange=${
162	      filter.syllableCountRange
163	        ? filter.syllableCountRange.join('-')
164	        : ''
165	    }`,
166	    `phonemesAllowed=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesAllowed)}`,
167	    `phonemesRequired=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesRequired)}`,
168	    `graphemesAllowed=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesAllowed)}`,
169	    `graphemesRequired=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesRequired)}`,
170	    `hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}`,
171	  ];
172	  return parts.join('|');
173	};
174	```

308	
309	In `src/data/words/filter.ts:169-190`, add `emoji` and `image` to the returned `WordHit`:
310	
311	```typescript
312	const joinHits = (
313	  curriculum: CurriculumEntry[],
314	  core: Map<string, WordCore>,
315	  region: Region,
316	): WordHit[] =>
317	  curriculum.flatMap((entry) => {
318	    const c = core.get(entry.word);
319	    if (!c) return [];
320	    return [
321	      {
322	        word: entry.word,
323	        region,
324	        level: entry.level,
325	        syllableCount: c.syllableCount,
326	        syllables: c.syllables,
327	        variants: c.variants,
328	        ipa: entry.ipa || undefined,
329	        graphemes: entry.graphemes,
330	        emoji: entry.emoji,
331	        image: entry.image,
332	        provenance: 'shipped',
333	      } as WordHit,
334	    ];
335	  });
336	```

628	### Task 7: Invert picture-mode invariant in resolveWordSpellConfig (R8, R9)
629	
630	**Files:**
631	
632	- Modify: `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-298`
633	- Test: `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts`
634	- Test: `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/mode-default-invariant.test.ts`
635	
636	This is the largest task. The picture-mode invariant currently says "picture → rounds defined, source undefined". It must change to "picture default → source with hasVisual:true, rounds undefined" while still honoring saved configs that have explicit rounds.
637	
638	- [ ] **Step 1: Update `mode-default-invariant.test.ts` — change `assertPictureInvariant`**
639	
640	The old picture invariant asserted `rounds` defined and `source` undefined. The new invariant asserts `source` defined with `hasVisual: true` and `rounds` undefined (for default configs). Update the helper and affected tests:
641	
642	```typescript
643	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
644	import { resolveWordSpellConfig } from './$gameId';
645	import type { WordSpellConfig } from '@/games/word-spell/types';
646	
647	const assertRecallInvariant = (cfg: WordSpellConfig) => {
648	  expect(cfg.mode).toBe('recall');
649	  expect(cfg.source).toBeDefined();
650	  expect(cfg.source?.type).toBe('word-library');
651	  expect(cfg.rounds).toBeUndefined();
652	};
653	
654	const assertPictureInvariant = (cfg: WordSpellConfig) => {
655	  expect(cfg.mode).toBe('picture');
656	  expect(cfg.source).toBeDefined();
657	  expect(cfg.source?.type).toBe('word-library');
658	  expect(cfg.source?.filter.hasVisual).toBe(true);
659	  expect(cfg.rounds).toBeUndefined();
660	};
661	
662	describe('mode-default invariant', () => {
663	  it('null saved config → recall + library, no emoji', () => {
664	    assertRecallInvariant(resolveWordSpellConfig(null));
665	  });
666	
667	  it('saved with only mode=recall → recall + library', () => {
668	    assertRecallInvariant(
669	      resolveWordSpellConfig({
670	        component: 'WordSpell',
671	        configMode: 'advanced',
672	        mode: 'recall',
673	      }),
674	    );
675	  });
676	
677	  it('saved with only mode=picture → picture + source with hasVisual', () => {
678	    assertPictureInvariant(
679	      resolveWordSpellConfig({
680	        component: 'WordSpell',
681	        configMode: 'advanced',
682	        mode: 'picture',
683	      }),
684	    );
685	  });
686	
687	  it('saved with mode=recall + leaked rounds → rounds dropped', () => {
688	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({
689	      component: 'WordSpell',
690	      configMode: 'advanced',
691	      mode: 'recall',
692	      rounds: [{ word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' }],
693	      source: {
694	        type: 'word-library',
695	        filter: {
696	          region: 'aus',
697	          graphemesAllowed: [{ g: 's', p: 's' }],
698	          phonemesRequired: ['s'],
699	        },
700	      },
701	    });
702	    assertRecallInvariant(cfg);
703	  });
704	
705	  it('saved with mode=picture + explicit rounds → honors saved rounds', () => {
706	    const rounds = [{ word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' }];
707	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({
708	      component: 'WordSpell',
709	      configMode: 'advanced',
710	      mode: 'picture',
711	      rounds,
712	    });
713	    expect(cfg.mode).toBe('picture');
714	    expect(cfg.rounds).toEqual(rounds);
715	    expect(cfg.source).toBeUndefined();
716	  });
717	
718	  it('saved with mode=picture + rounds + leaked source → honors rounds, drops source', () => {
719	    const rounds = [{ word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' }];
720	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({
721	      component: 'WordSpell',
722	      configMode: 'advanced',
723	      mode: 'picture',
724	      rounds,
725	      source: {
726	        type: 'word-library',
727	        filter: {
728	          region: 'aus',
729	          graphemesAllowed: [{ g: 's', p: 's' }],
730	        },
731	      },
732	    });
733	    expect(cfg.mode).toBe('picture');
734	    expect(cfg.rounds).toEqual(rounds);
735	    expect(cfg.source).toBeUndefined();
736	  });
737	
738	  it('simple-mode saved config always resolves to recall + library', () => {
739	    const cfg = resolveWordSpellConfig({
740	      component: 'WordSpell',
741	      configMode: 'simple',
742	      selectedUnits: [{ g: 's', p: 's' }],
743	      region: 'aus',
744	      inputMethod: 'drag',
745	    });
746	    assertRecallInvariant(cfg);
747	  });
748	});
749	```

835	- [ ] **Step 6: Rewrite the picture-mode branch of `resolveWordSpellConfig`**
836	
837	Replace the picture-mode branch (lines 286-298) with:
838	
839	```typescript
840	// mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
841	// picture with explicit rounds ⇒ honor them, drop source
842	if (Array.isArray(merged.rounds) && merged.rounds.length > 0) {
843	  const { source: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
844	  return rest as WordSpellConfig;
845	}
846	// picture without explicit rounds ⇒ source with hasVisual, drop rounds
847	const picture = { ...merged };
848	if (picture.rounds) {
849	  delete picture.rounds;
850	}
851	if (!picture.source) {
852	  picture.source = {
853	    type: 'word-library',
854	    filter: { region: 'aus', hasVisual: true },
855	  };
856	} else {
857	  picture.source = {
858	    ...picture.source,
859	    filter: { ...picture.source.filter, hasVisual: true },
860	  };
861	}
862	return picture;
863	```

1035	
1036	## Spec Coverage Matrix
1037	
1038	| Requirement                            | Task(s)                    | Acceptance Example |
1039	| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------ |
1040	| R1 — CurriculumEntry emoji/image       | Task 1                     | AE3                |
1041	| R2 — WordHit emoji/image propagation   | Task 3                     | AE3                |
1042	| R3 — DraftEntry emoji/image            | Task 1                     | —                  |
1043	| R4 — toWordSpellRound maps emoji/image | Task 4                     | AE1                |
1044	| R5 — Existing 7 words get emoji        | Task 5                     | AE1                |
1045	| R6 — "mum" added to curriculum + core  | Task 6                     | —                  |
1046	| R7 — hasVisual filter                  | Task 2                     | AE2                |
1047	| R8 — Picture mode uses library source  | Task 7                     | AE1                |
1048	| R8 — Saved explicit rounds honored     | Task 7 (Step 1, test case) | —                  |
1049	| R8 — Zero-results fallback             | Task 8                     | —                  |
1050	| R9 — Remove hardcoded pool             | Task 7 (Step 4)            | —                  |
1051	
1052	## Outstanding Questions Resolved
1053	
1054	- **"mum" IPA/graphemes:** `/mʌm/` with graphemes `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `m/m`. This follows the same pattern as existing level 2 words with the `ʌ` vowel (e.g. "mug" = `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `g/g`).
1055	- **Default picture-mode filter scope:** Pull from all levels with visuals (no level/grapheme constraint). The default `WordFilter` uses `region: 'aus'` and `hasVisual: true` with no level restriction, so any curriculum word tagged with emoji or image across any level is included.
1056	
1057	## Known Limitations
1058	
1059	- **Region hardcoded to `aus`:** The default picture-mode filter and `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` hardcode `region: 'aus'`. This mirrors the existing behavior (the hardcoded pool was also aus-only), but means adding picture-mode words for other regions requires a code change to the default filter, not just a data edit. Multi-region picture-mode support is out of scope for this pass — when other regions are introduced, the default should derive region from the user's locale or a config setting.
1060
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import type { AnswerGameConfig } from '@/components/answer-game/types';
2	import type {
3	  LevelGraphemeUnit,
4	  Region,
5	  WordSpellSource,
6	} from '@/data/words';
7	import type { ConfigField } from '@/lib/config-fields';
8	
9	export type WordSpellSimpleConfig = {
10	  configMode: 'simple';
11	  selectedUnits: LevelGraphemeUnit[];
12	  region: Region;
13	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
14	  /**
15	   * Simple form supports two kid-friendly modes:
16	   *  - 'recall': pick one or more level grapheme units; word library filtered to that scope
17	   *  - 'picture': image-prompted rounds, no level selection required
18	   */
19	  mode?: 'picture' | 'recall';
20	};
21	
22	export interface GapDefinition {
23	  word: string;
24	  distractors?: string[];
25	}
26	
27	export interface WordSpellRound {
28	  word: string;
29	  /**
30	   * Native emoji (or ZWJ sequence) for picture mode. When set, this is shown instead of `image` / `sceneImage`.
31	   */
32	  emoji?: string;
33	  /** Fluent Emoji SVG path, URL, or `/public` asset path (picture/sentence-gap modes) */
34	  image?: string;
35	  /** Sentence with a blank for sentence-gap mode: "The ___ sat on the mat." */
36	  sentence?: string;
37	  /** Scene illustration for sentence-gap mode */
38	  sceneImage?: string;
39	  /** Optional custom audio override instead of TTS */
40	  audioOverride?: string;
41	  /** Gap definitions for sentence-gap mode. Each entry maps to a {N} placeholder. */
42	  gaps?: GapDefinition[];
43	}
44	
45	export interface WordSpellConfig extends AnswerGameConfig {
46	  component: 'WordSpell';
47	  configMode?: 'simple' | 'advanced';
48	  selectedUnits?: LevelGraphemeUnit[];
49	  mode: 'picture' | 'recall' | 'sentence-gap';
50	  /** @default 'letter' */
51	  tileUnit: 'letter' | 'syllable' | 'word';
52	  /** Explicit hand-authored rounds. Wins over `source` when both are present. */
53	  rounds?: WordSpellRound[];
54	  /** Library-driven rounds. Resolved at WordSpell mount time. */
55	  source?: WordSpellSource;
56	}
57	
58	export const wordSpellConfigFields: ConfigField[] = [
59	  {
60	    type: 'select',
61	    key: 'inputMethod',
62	    label: 'Input method',
63	    options: [
