 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/seen-words.ts`

```text
1	import { sampleHits } from './sample';
2	import type { GraphemePairFilter, WordFilter, WordHit } from './types';
3	
4	const sortedCsv = (xs: readonly string[] | undefined): string =>
5	  xs && xs.length > 0 ? xs.toSorted().join(',') : '';
6	
7	const sortedPairsCsv = (
8	  xs: readonly GraphemePairFilter[] | undefined,
9	): string =>
10	  xs && xs.length > 0
11	    ? xs
12	        .map((u) => `${u.g}|${u.p}`)
13	        .toSorted()
14	        .join(',')
15	    : '';
16	
17	/**
18	 * Stable key derived from a `WordFilter`. Two filters with the same
19	 * content produce the same signature regardless of property order or
20	 * array ordering (we sort `phonemesAllowed` etc.). Used as the key
21	 * under which "seen words" are tracked.
22	 *
23	 * Note: `fallbackToAus` is intentionally excluded from the signature
24	 * because it changes query strategy, not semantic word identity.
25	 */
26	export const filterSignature = (filter: WordFilter): string => {
27	  const parts: string[] = [
28	    `region=${filter.region}`,
29	    `level=${filter.level ?? ''}`,
30	    `levels=${sortedCsv(filter.levels?.map(String))}`,
31	    `levelRange=${filter.levelRange ? filter.levelRange.join('-') : ''}`,
32	    `syllableCountEq=${filter.syllableCountEq ?? ''}`,
33	    `syllableCountRange=${
34	      filter.syllableCountRange
35	        ? filter.syllableCountRange.join('-')
36	        : ''
37	    }`,
38	    `phonemesAllowed=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesAllowed)}`,
39	    `phonemesRequired=${sortedCsv(filter.phonemesRequired)}`,
40	    `graphemesAllowed=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesAllowed)}`,
41	    `graphemesRequired=${sortedPairsCsv(filter.graphemesRequired)}`,
42	    `hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}`,
43	  ];
44	  return parts.join('|');
45	};
46	
47	export interface SeenWordsStore {
48	  get: (signature: string) => Promise<Set<string>>;
49	  addSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
50	  resetSeen: (signature: string, words: string[]) => Promise<void>;
51	}
52	
53	export const createInMemorySeenWordsStore = (): SeenWordsStore => {
54	  const sets = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
55	
56	  return {
57	    get: async (signature) => new Set(sets.get(signature)),
58	    addSeen: async (signature, words) => {
59	      const existing = sets.get(signature) ?? new Set<string>();
60	      for (const w of words) existing.add(w);
61	      sets.set(signature, existing);
62	    },
63	    resetSeen: async (signature, words) => {
64	      sets.set(signature, new Set(words));
65	    },
66	  };
67	};
68	
69	/**
70	 * Picks `limit` hits with the following rules:
71	 *   1. Never repeats a word within a single call (enforced by
72	 *      Fisher–Yates sampling without replacement).
73	 *   2. Prefers words not yet in `store` for this `signature`.
74	 *   3. When the unseen pile has fewer than `limit` words, takes all of
75	 *      them and tops up at random from already-seen words, then resets
76	 *      the signature's seen set to exactly the words just picked —
77	 *      starting the next cycle fresh.
78	 *   4. Not safe for concurrent callers on the same signature — the
79	 *      read-compute-write pattern can race if two awaited calls
80	 *      interleave. Callers are expected to serialize per signature.
81	 */
82	export const pickWithRecycling = async (
83	  hits: WordHit[],
84	  limit: number,
85	  signature: string,
86	  store: SeenWordsStore,
87	  seed?: string,
88	): Promise<WordHit[]> => {
89	  if (hits.length === 0 || limit <= 0) return [];
90	
91	  const seen = await store.get(signature);
92	  const unseen = hits.filter((h) => !seen.has(h.word));
93	
94	  if (unseen.length >= limit) {
95	    const picked = sampleHits(unseen, limit, seed);
96	    await store.addSeen(
97	      signature,
98	      picked.map((h) => h.word),
99	    );
100	    return picked;
101	  }
102	
103	  const seenHits = hits.filter((h) => seen.has(h.word));
104	  const recycled = sampleHits(seenHits, limit - unseen.length, seed);
105	  const combined = [...unseen, ...recycled];
106	  // Shuffle the combined pool so unseen and recycled words are interleaved,
107	  // not segregated (unseen-first would be visually obvious on exhausted plays).
108	  const picked = sampleHits(combined, limit, seed);
109	  await store.resetSeen(
110	    signature,
111	    picked.map((h) => h.word),
112	  );
113	  return picked;
114	};
115
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:51Z)**_

There's an interesting issue here. The mode detection at line 216-219 only recognizes `'picture'` -- everything else (including `'sentence-gap'`) falls through to `'recall'`. So when `saved.mode === 'sentence-gap'`:

1. `base = DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (which has `totalRounds: 4`)
2. `merged = { ...baseClone, ...saved }` -- so `mode` becomes `'sentence-gap'`
3. `merged.mode === 'recall'` is false, so the recall branch is skipped
4. Falls to the picture/sentence-gap branch at line 285
5. If no explicit rounds, `hasVisual: true` is injected into the source filter

This means sentence-gap configs get `hasVisual: true` forced on their source filter. For sentence-gap, the visual field might be `sceneImage` rather than `emoji`/`image` -- but `hasVisual` only checks `emoji` and `image`. If sentence-gap entries only have `sceneImage`, they would all be filtered out by `hasVisual: true`, and then the fallback in `useLibraryRounds` would retry without `hasVisual`, returning words without any visual.

But wait -- this was the old behavior too (old code deleted source for picture/sentence-gap and used hardcoded rounds). The new behavior is arguably better since it at least falls back. This is noted in the PR intent. Let me verify the old behavior was the same regarding sentence-gap.

```text
  // When the picker set selectedUnits, re-derive source.filter so
  // the filter stays in sync even in advanced mode.
  if (Array.isArray(merged.selectedUnits) && merged.source) {
    const derived = resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
      configMode: 'simple',
      selectedUnits: merged.selectedUnits,
      region: merged.source.filter.region,
      inputMethod: merged.inputMethod,
    });
    merged.source = derived.source;
  }

  // Mode invariant:
  // recall  ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined
  // picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined
  if (merged.mode === 'recall') {
    if (merged.rounds) {
      const { rounds: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
      return rest as WordSpellConfig;
    }
    return merged;
  }

  // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
  const picture = { ...merged };
  if (picture.source) {
    delete picture.source;
  }
  if (!Array.isArray(picture.rounds) || picture.rounds.length === 0) {
    picture.rounds = sliceWordSpellRounds(8);
  }
  picture.totalRounds = Math.min(
    picture.rounds.length,
    WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length,
  );
  return picture;
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:39:54Z)**_

Now I need to understand the type of `picture` at line 305. After `const picture = { ...merged }` and mutations (`delete picture.rounds`, `picture.source = ...`), TypeScript infers its return type. The function returns `WordSpellConfig` but `picture` is inferred as a structural type - TypeScript will check this is assignment-compatible. Let me verify there's no structural mismatch.

The key question: after `delete picture.rounds` and `picture.source = ...`, does `picture` still satisfy `WordSpellConfig`? Since `rounds` is optional in `WordSpellConfig` and `source` is also optional, the `delete` + assignment should still be structurally valid. But `picture` is typed as `WordSpellConfig` without an explicit cast only if TypeScript's inferred type is compatible. Let me check if this requires a cast.

 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/authoring/engine.ts`

```text
1	import { arpabetStringToIpa } from './arpabet-to-ipa';
2	
3	export interface Breakdown {
4	  word: string;
5	  ipa: string;
6	  syllables: string[];
7	  phonemes: string[];
8	  /**
9	   * True iff the word is in RiTa's dictionary. When false, the
10	   * phonemes and syllables (if any) come from RiTa's letter-to-sound
11	   * fallback — usable but guessed. The authoring UI keeps a
12	   * dictionary.com banner for that case so teachers can verify.
13	   */
14	  ritaKnown: boolean;
15	}
16	
17	const splitSyllablesByPhonemeBoundary = (
18	  word: string,
19	  phonemes: string[],
20	  phonemeSyllables: string[],
21	): string[] => {
22	  if (phonemeSyllables.length <= 1) return [word];
23	
24	  const lettersPerPhoneme = word.length / phonemes.length;
25	  const boundaries: number[] = [];
26	  let acc = 0;
27	  for (let i = 0; i < phonemeSyllables.length - 1; i += 1) {
28	    acc += phonemeSyllables[i]!.split('-').filter(Boolean).length;
29	    boundaries.push(Math.round(acc * lettersPerPhoneme));
30	  }
31	
32	  const out: string[] = [];
33	  let prev = 0;
34	  for (const b of boundaries) {
35	    const cut = Math.min(Math.max(b, prev + 1), word.length - 1);
36	    out.push(word.slice(prev, cut));
37	    prev = cut;
38	  }
39	  out.push(word.slice(prev));
40	  return out.filter((s) => s.length > 0);
41	};
42	
43	interface RitaAnalysis {
44	  phones?: string;
45	  syllables?: string;
46	  stresses?: string;
47	  pos?: string;
48	}
49	
50	export const generateBreakdown = async (
51	  rawWord: string,
52	): Promise<Breakdown> => {
53	  const word = rawWord.trim().toLowerCase();
54	  const { RiTa } = await import('rita');
55	  const ritaKnown = RiTa.hasWord(word);
56	
57	  // RiTa.analyze gives us phones + syllables in one call and
58	  // returns LTS-derived guesses for words outside the dictionary
59	  // (the `hasWord` miss case). Using it unconditionally means
60	  // "prothesis" etc. still come back with usable data — marked
61	  // `ritaKnown: false` so the UI can warn the guess needs review.
62	  let phonemes: string[] = [];
63	  let phonemeSyllables: string[] = [];
64	  try {
65	    const analysis = RiTa.analyze(word) as RitaAnalysis;
66	    const phonesStr = analysis.phones ?? '';
67	    const syllStr = analysis.syllables ?? '';
68	    if (phonesStr) phonemes = arpabetStringToIpa(phonesStr);
69	    if (syllStr) phonemeSyllables = syllStr.split('/');
70	  } catch (error) {
71	    // arpabetStringToIpa throws on unknown tokens; treat as "no
72	    // alignment available" and let the caller bootstrap. Surface
73	    // the error for debugging.
74	    console.error('[authoring] RiTa.analyze parse failed:', error);
75	  }
76	
77	  if (phonemes.length === 0) {
78	    return {
79	      word,
80	      ipa: '',
81	      syllables: [],
82	      phonemes: [],
83	      ritaKnown,
84	    };
85	  }
86	
87	  const syllables = splitSyllablesByPhonemeBoundary(
88	    word,
89	    phonemes,
90	    phonemeSyllables,
91	  );
92	
93	  return {
94	    word,
95	    ipa: phonemes.join(''),
96	    syllables,
97	    phonemes,
98	    ritaKnown,
99	  };
100	};
101
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:40:03Z)**_

OK, so in the old code, sentence-gap also got the same treatment as picture (source deleted, rounds hardcoded). The new code changes this to use source-based resolution. This is an intentional change, not a bug.

Now let me look at one more potential issue -- the `totalRounds` for picture mode in the new code. Previously, `totalRounds` was explicitly set to `Math.min(picture.rounds.length, WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length)`. Now, `totalRounds` comes from the merged config. For the picture mode with source, `totalRounds` from `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` is 8. But then at line 78 in `useLibraryRounds.ts`:

```typescript
const limit = source.limit ?? config.totalRounds;
```

If there are only 7 visual words in the curriculum, `limit` would be 8, and `pickWithRecycling` would recycle. That's fine -- it's the intended behavior of `pickWithRecycling`.

Let me check one more thing: the `resolveWordSpellConfig` picture path when saved config has `totalRounds` set. If saved has `totalRounds: 3` and `mode: 'picture'`, the merged config gets `totalRounds: 3` from the saved override, which is preserved. That's correct.

Let me now look at the new `mum` entry placement in level2.json to verify it's in the right alphabetical position.

```bash
      "severity": "low"
    },
    {
      "id": "R-2",
      "description": "The home-screen card ordering tests in home-screen-card-order.test.ts are pure unit tests of the comparator function. The integration of compareHomeScreenCardRows into HomeScreen (index.tsx) is tested only by a vacuous smoke test (`expect(true).toBe(true)`). A regression where the comparator is called with wrong arguments or where the sort is applied to the wrong list would not be caught by any unit test.",
      "severity": "low"
    }

      "id": "TG-1",

      "confidence": 75
```

```text
const WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL: WordSpellRound[] = [
  { word: 'cat', emoji: '🐱' },
  { word: 'dog', emoji: '🐶' },
  { word: 'sun', emoji: '☀️' },
  { word: 'pin', emoji: '📌' },
  { word: 'sad', emoji: '☹️' },
  { word: 'ant', emoji: '🐜' },
  { word: 'can', emoji: '🥫' },
  { word: 'mum', emoji: '🤱' },
];

const sliceWordSpellRounds = (count: number): WordSpellRound[] => {
  const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length));
  return WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.slice(0, n).map((r) => ({ ...r }));
};

const DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG: WordSpellConfig = {
```

```text
};

/**
 * Deterministic default round values for NumberMatch. Using a fixed sequence
 * (rather than `Math.random`) keeps VR baselines stable across runs — nanoid
 * seeds in the route loader bypass any Math.random pin, so the only reliable
 * fix is to remove randomness from the default config entirely. Users still
 * customise via the settings panel.
 */
const DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES: readonly number[] = [5, 8, 3];

const makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig = (): NumberMatchConfig => ({
  gameId: 'number-match',
  component: 'NumberMatch',
  inputMethod: 'drag',
  wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
  tileBankMode: 'distractors',
  distractorCount: 2,
  totalRounds: 3,
  // Deterministic order by default — see DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG comment.
  roundsInOrder: true,
  ttsEnabled: true,
  mode: 'numeral-to-group',
  tileStyle: 'dots',
  range: { min: 1, max: 12 },
  rounds: DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES.map((value) => ({ value })),
});

const makeDefaultSpotAllConfig = (): SpotAllConfig =>
  resolveSpotAllSimpleConfig({
    configMode: 'simple',
    selectedConfusablePairs: [
      { pair: ['b', 'd'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
      { pair: ['p', 'q'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
    ],
    selectedReversibleChars: [],
  });

export const resizeNumberMatchRounds = (
  prev: NumberMatchRound[],
  count: number,
  range: { min: number; max: number },
): NumberMatchRound[] => {
  const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, 50));
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:40:16Z)**_

`mum` isn't in `levels.ts` directly -- that file is about grapheme-level definitions, not individual words. The curriculum JSON is the word list; `levels.ts` defines which grapheme (g, p) pairs are introduced at each level. The `mum` word uses graphemes `m/m` and `u/ʌ`, both of which should already be in level 2. Let me verify.

```text
  const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, 50));
  const span = Math.max(1, range.max - range.min + 1);
  // Deterministic fill: cycle through the range starting at `range.min`. This
  // avoids `Math.random` so defaults stay stable (see note above) and is
  // simple enough for users to understand at a glance. They can override any
  // round value via the settings panel.
  const fillValueAt = (index: number): number =>
    range.min + (index % span);

  // Detect a collapsed state: every round has the same value even though the
  // range allows variety. This happens when the user narrows the range down
  // to a single value (collapsing every round to that value) and then widens
  // it again — without this check the preserved values stay stuck at the
  // collapse point and the player sees the same number every round. Also
  // handles the empty-prev case from a legacy saved config.
  const firstValue = prev[0]?.value;
  const allCollapsed =
    prev.length >= 2 &&
    span > 1 &&
    prev.every((r) => r.value === firstValue);
  if (prev.length === 0 || allCollapsed) {
    return Array.from({ length: n }, (_, i) => ({
      value: fillValueAt(i),
    }));
  }

  const next = prev.slice(0, n).map((r, i) => {
    if (r.value >= range.min && r.value <= range.max) return { ...r };
    return { value: fillValueAt(i) };
  });
  while (next.length < n) {
    next.push({ value: fillValueAt(next.length) });
  }
  return next;
};

const makeDefaultSortNumbersConfig = (): SortNumbersConfig =>
  resolveSimpleConfig({
    configMode: 'simple',
    direction: 'ascending',
    start: 2,
    step: 2,
    quantity: 5,
    distractors: false,
  });

export const resolveWordSpellConfig = (
  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
): WordSpellConfig => {
  const mode =
    (saved as { mode?: unknown } | null)?.mode === 'picture'
      ? 'picture'
      : 'recall';
  const base =
    mode === 'picture' ? DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG : DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG;
  const baseClone: WordSpellConfig = {
    ...base,
    rounds: base.rounds
      ? base.rounds.map((r) => ({ ...r }))
      : undefined,
    source: base.source
      ? { ...base.source, filter: { ...base.source.filter } }
      : undefined,
  };

  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'WordSpell') return baseClone;

  // Simple-mode: delegate to word-spell's library-source resolver.
  // Any config with selectedUnits (but not explicitly advanced) was produced
  // by the picker and must be re-resolved so source.filter stays in sync.
  if (
    saved.configMode === 'simple' ||
    (saved.configMode !== 'advanced' &&
      Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits))
  ) {
    const savedInput = saved.inputMethod;
    return resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
      configMode: 'simple',
      ...saved,
      inputMethod:
        savedInput === 'type' || savedInput === 'both'
          ? savedInput
          : 'drag',
    } as Parameters<typeof resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig>[0]);
  }

  // Advanced merge — start from mode-appropriate base, then layer saved
  // fields, then enforce the mode invariant.
  const merged: WordSpellConfig = {
    ...baseClone,
    ...(saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>),
    gameId: 'word-spell',
    component: 'WordSpell',
  };

  // When the picker set selectedUnits, re-derive source.filter so
  // the filter stays in sync even in advanced mode.
  if (Array.isArray(merged.selectedUnits) && merged.source) {
    const derived = resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
      configMode: 'simple',
      selectedUnits: merged.selectedUnits,
```

```text
      selectedUnits: merged.selectedUnits,

    merged.source = derived.source;

  // Mode invariant:
  // recall  ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined
  // picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined
  if (merged.mode === 'recall') {
    if (merged.rounds) {
      const { rounds: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
      return rest as WordSpellConfig;
    }
    return merged;
  }

  // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
  const picture = { ...merged };
  if (picture.source) {
    delete picture.source;
  }
  if (!Array.isArray(picture.rounds) || picture.rounds.length === 0) {
    picture.rounds = sliceWordSpellRounds(8);
  }
  picture.totalRounds = Math.min(
    picture.rounds.length,
    WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length,
  );
  return picture;
};

const resolveNumberMatchConfig = (
  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
): NumberMatchConfig => {
  const base = makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig();
  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'NumberMatch') return base;
  const merged: NumberMatchConfig = {
    ...base,
    ...(saved as Partial<NumberMatchConfig>),
    gameId: 'number-match',
    component: 'NumberMatch',
  };
  const targetLen =
    merged.totalRounds > 0 ? merged.totalRounds : base.totalRounds;
  merged.rounds = resizeNumberMatchRounds(
    Array.isArray(merged.rounds) ? merged.rounds : [],
    targetLen,
    merged.range,
  );
```

```text
/**
 * Deduped orthographic pool for structural word splitting. Used by
 * `makeGraphemes` — the splitter only needs to know which grapheme forms
 * exist, not which phoneme each one teaches.
 */
export const graphemePool = (level: number): string[] => {
  const seen = new Set<string>();
  for (const unit of cumulativeGraphemes(level)) seen.add(unit.g);
```

 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts`

```text
1	import { defaultSelection, unitLevel } from './level-unit-selection';
2	import type { WordSpellConfig, WordSpellSimpleConfig } from './types';
3	import type {
4	  GraphemePairFilter,
5	  LevelGraphemeUnit,
6	} from '@/data/words';
7	import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL, cumulativeGraphemes } from '@/data/words';
8	
9	const uniqueGraphemePairs = (
10	  units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[],
11	): GraphemePairFilter[] => {
12	  const seen = new Set<string>();
13	  const out: GraphemePairFilter[] = [];
14	  for (const u of units) {
15	    const key = `${u.g}|${u.p}`;
16	    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
17	    seen.add(key);
18	    out.push({ g: u.g, p: u.p });
19	  }
20	  return out;
21	};
22	
23	const maxLevelOf = (units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[]): number => {
24	  if (units.length === 0) return 1;
25	  let max = 1;
26	  for (const u of units) {
27	    const lvl = unitLevel(u);
28	    if (lvl !== undefined && lvl > max) max = lvl;
29	  }
30	  return max;
31	};
32	
33	const unitsFromLegacy = (
34	  raw: Record<string, unknown>,
35	): LevelGraphemeUnit[] => {
36	  if (
37	    typeof raw.level !== 'number' ||
38	    !Array.isArray(raw.phonemesAllowed)
39	  )
40	    return defaultSelection();
41	  const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
42	  for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= raw.level; lvl++) {
43	    for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
44	      if ((raw.phonemesAllowed as string[]).includes(u.p)) out.push(u);
45	    }
46	  }
47	  return out.length > 0 ? out : defaultSelection();
48	};
49	
50	export const resolveSimpleConfig = (
51	  simple: WordSpellSimpleConfig,
52	): WordSpellConfig => {
53	  const raw = simple as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
54	  const units = Array.isArray(raw.selectedUnits)
55	    ? (raw.selectedUnits as LevelGraphemeUnit[])
56	    : unitsFromLegacy(raw);
57	  const region =
58	    typeof raw.region === 'string' ? (raw.region as 'aus') : 'aus';
59	  const mode: 'picture' | 'recall' =
60	    simple.mode === 'picture' ? 'picture' : 'recall';
61	
62	  // Picture mode rounds use cumulative graphemes from every level so the
63	  // image-prompted word pool is as broad as possible without a required-grapheme
64	  // gate forcing irrelevant level selections.
65	  const maxLevel = mode === 'picture' ? 8 : maxLevelOf(units);
66	  const graphemesAllowed = cumulativeGraphemes(maxLevel);
67	  const graphemesRequired =
68	    mode === 'picture' ? [] : uniqueGraphemePairs(units);
69	
70	  return {
71	    gameId: 'word-spell',
72	    component: 'WordSpell',
73	    configMode: 'simple',
74	    selectedUnits: units,
75	    mode,
76	    tileUnit: 'letter',
77	    inputMethod: simple.inputMethod,
78	    wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
79	    ttsEnabled: true,
80	    roundsInOrder: false,
81	    totalRounds: 4,
82	    tileBankMode: 'exact',
83	    source: {
84	      type: 'word-library',
85	      filter: {
86	        region,
87	        graphemesAllowed,
88	        graphemesRequired,
89	      },
90	    },
91	  };
92	};
93	
94	export const advancedToSimple = (
95	  config: WordSpellConfig,
96	): WordSpellSimpleConfig => {
97	  const filter = config.source?.filter;
98	  const region = filter?.region ?? 'aus';
99	  const mode: 'picture' | 'recall' =
100	    config.mode === 'picture' ? 'picture' : 'recall';
101	
102	  if (
103	    typeof filter?.level === 'number' &&
104	    Array.isArray(filter.phonemesAllowed)
105	  ) {
106	    const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
107	    for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= filter.level; lvl++) {
108	      for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
109	        if (filter.phonemesAllowed.includes(u.p)) {
110	          out.push(u);
111	        }
112	      }
113	    }
114	    return {
115	      configMode: 'simple',
116	      selectedUnits: out,
117	      region,
118	      inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
119	      mode,
120	    };
121	  }
122	
123	  return {
124	    configMode: 'simple',
125	    selectedUnits: mode === 'picture' ? [] : defaultSelection(),
126	    region,
127	    inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
128	    mode,
129	  };
130	};
131
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:40:29Z)**_

Good. Level 2 includes `{ g: 'm', p: 'm' }` and `{ g: 'u', p: 'ʌ' }`, which are exactly what `mum` uses. The word "mum" = [m/m, u/ʌ, m/m], all in level 2's cumulative set.

Now let me check if there's a reconciliation test that would catch it if `mum` were invalid.

**Key findings written to** `/tmp/compound-engineering/ce-code-review/20260501-173608-ce4e9d68/maintainability.json`

**M-01 (P1, confidence 75)** — The picture-mode `hasVisual: true` invariant is enforced only inside the advanced-merge branch of `resolveWordSpellConfig`. When a saved config takes the simple-mode early-return path (line 237-251 in `$gameId.tsx`), it calls `resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig` directly, which never sets `hasVisual` in the filter even when `mode === 'picture'`. The result is that a simple-mode picture config silently produces a source filter that matches all words, not just the visual subset. The root is in `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts` lines 70-91 — the returned source filter has no `hasVisual` property regardless of mode. The fix is either to add `...(mode === 'picture' ? { hasVisual: true } : {})` inside the filter object in `resolveSimpleConfig`, or to run the result of the simple resolver through the same invariant enforcement in `resolveWordSpellConfig`.

**M-02 (P2, confidence 75)** — `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/routes/$locale/_app/home-screen-card-order.ts` is 13 lines with one runtime consumer. The extraction is premature; it adds an indirection hop for no current benefit. Not a defect, but the next developer editing home screen sort logic will need to navigate an extra file.

**RR-01** — In `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` lines 70-84, when the hasVisual fallback fires, `activeFilter` loses `hasVisual` and `filterSignature(activeFilter)` produces a different key. The seen-words cycling bucket for the original `hasVisual=true` session is abandoned. Safe today, confusing to a future maintainer.

**TG-01** — No test case in `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/routes/$locale/_app/game/mode-default-invariant.test.ts` covers `configMode: 'simple'` + `mode: 'picture'`. This is the exact blind spot that allows M-01 to exist undetected.

```text
commit 72e597010b42dddf732216aac66abcdec285787a
Merge: 82ffb4b5 a647f1cf
Author: Leo Caseiro <940070+leocaseiro@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Fri May 1 17:21:03 2026 +1000

    feat(home): sort bookmarked game cards to the top (#283)
    
    ## Summary
    
    On the home screen, **bookmarked** games and custom saved configs now
    appear **before** non-bookmarked items. Within the same bookmark tier,
    **catalog defaults** still come before **custom games**, and order
    within each group stays stable (catalog index / custom list index).
    
    ## Implementation
    
    - `compareHomeScreenCardRows` in `home-screen-card-order.ts`: sort by
    bookmarked first, then `default` before `custom`, then by index.
    - `HomeScreen` builds a merged list of default + custom card
    descriptors, `toSorted`s with that comparator, then maps to `GameCard`s.
    - Unit tests cover default-only, custom-only, and mixed bookmark/custom
    ordering.
    
    ## Verification
    
    - Pre-push (related unit tests, typecheck, eslint, etc.) passed on push.
    - Existing E2E bookmark flows should still behave; order in DOM changes
    for bookmarked items only.
    
    Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
```

**Key findings written to** `/tmp/compound-engineering/ce-code-review/20260501-173608-ce4e9d68/maintainability.json`

**M-01 (P1, confidence 75)** — The picture-mode `hasVisual: true` invariant is enforced only inside the advanced-merge branch of `resolveWordSpellConfig`. When a saved config takes the simple-mode early-return path (line 237-251 in `$gameId.tsx`), it calls `resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig` directly, which never sets `hasVisual` in the filter even when `mode === 'picture'`. The result is that a simple-mode picture config silently produces a source filter that matches all words, not just the visual subset. The root is in `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts` lines 70-91 — the returned source filter has no `hasVisual` property regardless of mode. The fix is either to add `...(mode === 'picture' ? { hasVisual: true } : {})` inside the filter object in `resolveSimpleConfig`, or to run the result of the simple resolver through the same invariant enforcement in `resolveWordSpellConfig`.

**M-02 (P2, confidence 75)** — `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/routes/$locale/_app/home-screen-card-order.ts` is 13 lines with one runtime consumer. The extraction is premature; it adds an indirection hop for no current benefit. Not a defect, but the next developer editing home screen sort logic will need to navigate an extra file.

**RR-01** — In `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` lines 70-84, when the hasVisual fallback fires, `activeFilter` loses `hasVisual` and `filterSignature(activeFilter)` produces a different key. The seen-words cycling bucket for the original `hasVisual=true` session is abandoned. Safe today, confusing to a future maintainer.

**TG-01** — No test case in `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/routes/$locale/_app/game/mode-default-invariant.test.ts` covers `configMode: 'simple'` + `mode: 'picture'`. This is the exact blind spot that allows M-01 to exist undetected.</result>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:40:45Z)**_

Now let me write the output. Let me also check whether there could be an issue with `hasVisual: false` specifically being treated as "no filter" rather than "must not have visual."

In `entryMatches`:
```typescript
if (filter.hasVisual && !hit.emoji && !hit.image) return false;
```

If `filter.hasVisual === false`, the condition `filter.hasVisual` is falsy, so nothing happens. This means `hasVisual: false` is semantically identical to `hasVisual: undefined` -- both mean "no visual filtering." There's no way to filter for entries that specifically *lack* visuals. This is fine for the current use case but is worth noting for the API contract.

Let me also check the `filterSignature` for `hasVisual: false`:
```typescript
`hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}`
```

When `hasVisual === false`, this produces `hasVisual=false`. When `hasVisual === undefined`, this produces `hasVisual=`. So `hasVisual: false` and `hasVisual: undefined` produce *different* signatures even though they have identical filtering behavior. This means the same semantic filter could generate two different seen-words keys, causing the recycling pool to diverge unnecessarily.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:40:45Z)**_

Now I have enough data to understand the TDD pattern. Let me check the CLAUDE.md TDD requirements more carefully and assess the commits:

- Commit `1b4bb627`: propagate emoji/image in `joinHits` and `draftToHit` — production code, NO test file. The test for emoji propagation (`'propagates emoji from curriculum entry into WordHit'`) was added in commit `76c5088a` which came AFTER `1b4bb627`. This is a TDD violation — production code committed before tests.

Let me verify the order more precisely:

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts`

```text
1	import { defaultSelection, unitLevel } from './level-unit-selection';
2	import type { WordSpellConfig, WordSpellSimpleConfig } from './types';
3	import type {
4	  GraphemePairFilter,
5	  LevelGraphemeUnit,
6	} from '@/data/words';
7	import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL, cumulativeGraphemes } from '@/data/words';
8	
9	const uniqueGraphemePairs = (
10	  units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[],
11	): GraphemePairFilter[] => {
12	  const seen = new Set<string>();
13	  const out: GraphemePairFilter[] = [];
14	  for (const u of units) {
15	    const key = `${u.g}|${u.p}`;
16	    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
17	    seen.add(key);
18	    out.push({ g: u.g, p: u.p });
19	  }
20	  return out;
21	};
22	
23	const maxLevelOf = (units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[]): number => {
24	  if (units.length === 0) return 1;
25	  let max = 1;
26	  for (const u of units) {
27	    const lvl = unitLevel(u);
28	    if (lvl !== undefined && lvl > max) max = lvl;
29	  }
30	  return max;
31	};
32	
33	const unitsFromLegacy = (
34	  raw: Record<string, unknown>,
35	): LevelGraphemeUnit[] => {
36	  if (
37	    typeof raw.level !== 'number' ||
38	    !Array.isArray(raw.phonemesAllowed)
39	  )
40	    return defaultSelection();
41	  const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
42	  for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= raw.level; lvl++) {
43	    for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
44	      if ((raw.phonemesAllowed as string[]).includes(u.p)) out.push(u);
45	    }
46	  }
47	  return out.length > 0 ? out : defaultSelection();
48	};
49	
50	export const resolveSimpleConfig = (
51	  simple: WordSpellSimpleConfig,
52	): WordSpellConfig => {
53	  const raw = simple as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
54	  const units = Array.isArray(raw.selectedUnits)
55	    ? (raw.selectedUnits as LevelGraphemeUnit[])
56	    : unitsFromLegacy(raw);
57	  const region =
58	    typeof raw.region === 'string' ? (raw.region as 'aus') : 'aus';
59	  const mode: 'picture' | 'recall' =
60	    simple.mode === 'picture' ? 'picture' : 'recall';
61	
62	  // Picture mode rounds use cumulative graphemes from every level so the
63	  // image-prompted word pool is as broad as possible without a required-grapheme
64	  // gate forcing irrelevant level selections.
65	  const maxLevel = mode === 'picture' ? 8 : maxLevelOf(units);
66	  const graphemesAllowed = cumulativeGraphemes(maxLevel);
67	  const graphemesRequired =
68	    mode === 'picture' ? [] : uniqueGraphemePairs(units);
69	
70	  return {
71	    gameId: 'word-spell',
72	    component: 'WordSpell',
73	    configMode: 'simple',
74	    selectedUnits: units,
75	    mode,
76	    tileUnit: 'letter',
77	    inputMethod: simple.inputMethod,
78	    wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
79	    ttsEnabled: true,
80	    roundsInOrder: false,
81	    totalRounds: 4,
82	    tileBankMode: 'exact',
83	    source: {
84	      type: 'word-library',
85	      filter: {
86	        region,
87	        graphemesAllowed,
88	        graphemesRequired,
89	      },
90	    },
91	  };
92	};
93	
94	export const advancedToSimple = (
95	  config: WordSpellConfig,
96	): WordSpellSimpleConfig => {
97	  const filter = config.source?.filter;
98	  const region = filter?.region ?? 'aus';
99	  const mode: 'picture' | 'recall' =
100	    config.mode === 'picture' ? 'picture' : 'recall';
101	
102	  if (
103	    typeof filter?.level === 'number' &&
104	    Array.isArray(filter.phonemesAllowed)
105	  ) {
106	    const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
107	    for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= filter.level; lvl++) {
108	      for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
109	        if (filter.phonemesAllowed.includes(u.p)) {
110	          out.push(u);
111	        }
112	      }
113	    }
114	    return {
115	      configMode: 'simple',
116	      selectedUnits: out,
117	      region,
118	      inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
119	      mode,
120	    };
121	  }
122	
123	  return {
124	    configMode: 'simple',
125	    selectedUnits: mode === 'picture' ? [] : defaultSelection(),
126	    region,
127	    inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
128	    mode,
129	  };
130	};
131
```

---

## Institutional Learnings Search Results

### Search Context

- **Feature/Task**: Consolidate picture-mode visual data into curriculum JSON — adding `emoji`/`image` fields to `CurriculumEntry`/`WordHit`/`DraftEntry`, adding `hasVisual` to `WordFilter`, inverting the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` so picture mode uses a library-sourced `source` with `hasVisual: true`, removing the hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`, updating `useLibraryRounds` fallback, and adjusting home-screen card ordering.
- **Keywords Used**: picture mode, emoji, hasVisual, WordFilter, WordHit, CurriculumEntry, DraftEntry, resolveWordSpellConfig, useLibraryRounds, filterSignature, curriculum data, home screen card ordering, joinHits, adapters, mode invariant
- **Files Scanned**: ~118 markdown files across `docs/`, `docs/superpowers/plans/`, `docs/superpowers/specs/`
- **Relevant Matches**: 7 files (plus the PR's own brainstorm and plan on the branch)

### Critical Patterns

No `docs/solutions/patterns/critical-patterns.md` exists in this repo.

### Relevant Learnings

#### 1. The Cumulative Y-Filter Spec — Mode-Driven Round Source and the Picture/Recall Invariant

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/games/word-spell`, `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred — no YAML frontmatter)
- **Relevance**: This is the direct predecessor design for the mode invariant PR #281 is inverting. It establishes the contract that PR #281 must update.
- **Key Insight**: The spec deliberately created two non-overlapping defaults: `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (library source, no rounds) and `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` (emoji rounds, no source). The invariant enforced by `resolveWordSpellConfig` is: `mode = recall ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined; mode = picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined`. PR #281 inverts the picture half of this to `mode = picture ⇒ source defined with hasVisual: true ∧ rounds undefined (unless explicit rounds were saved)`. The spec also contains a documented edge case table: "Picture mode toggled in Advanced → `source` is dropped, `rounds = WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` is restored, picker is hidden." This entry is now exactly the behavior being changed — do not preserve it.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 2. Level 3+ Silent Empty Pool — Root Cause Pattern to Avoid Repeating

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-28-wordspell-levels-defaults-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/filter.ts`, `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm`
- **Problem Type**: `logic_error` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Documents the exact mechanism by which `entryMatches` silently drops words when a filter field is set but doesn't include prior-level values — the same class of subtle emptying that could affect `hasVisual` if the fallback in `useLibraryRounds` isn't wired.
- **Key Insight**: `filterWords` requires every grapheme of a word to be in `phonemesAllowed`. Using only the target level's phonemes (instead of cumulative) silently yields zero hits at levels 3+. The team's defense is `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` — a parameterized test asserting every `(region, level)` yields `≥ MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS`. PR #281 adds `hasVisual` as another filter predicate; a similar invariant test that runs `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` and asserts `>= 8 hits` (the number of emoji-tagged words) would lock this in. The spec also documents that `toWordSpellRound` in `src/data/words/adapters.ts` sets only `word` with no `emoji`/`image` — which is precisely one of the changes PR #281 must make.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 3. The Phonics Word Library Design — Type Chain, joinHits, and the WordCore/CurriculumEntry Join Contract

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-phonics-word-library-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/types.ts`, `src/data/words/filter.ts`, `src/data/words/adapters.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `architecture_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: The foundational design document for the entire data chain PR #281 modifies. Defines the original `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `WordFilter`, and `filterWords` contracts.
- **Key Insight**: `joinHits` silently discards any `CurriculumEntry` that has no matching row in the `WordCore` map for its level file — `if (!c) return []`. This is a documented behavior. PR #281 adds "mum" to `aus/level2.json`; if `core/level2.json` does not also get a `WordCore` row for "mum", the entry will be silently discarded and no test will catch the omission unless the `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` has a specific test checking the "mum" entry by word. The plan doc on the branch (`Task 6`) already documents this risk — worth double-checking that the `words.test.ts` invariant (#3 in §10: "Every `CurriculumEntry.word` has a matching `WordCore` in the same level's core file") covers this. Also: the original `WordFilter` type did not include `hasVisual` — adding it to `filterSignature` in `seen-words.ts` is required to avoid cache collisions between picture and recall sessions using the same curriculum level.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 4. Library Levels Recall — filterSignature Stability and the SeenWordsStore Cache Key

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-word-spell-library-levels-recall-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/seen-words.ts`, `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Defines `filterSignature` and its requirement to be stable regardless of property order or array ordering. PR #281 adds `hasVisual` to the signature — this spec is authoritative on the contract.
- **Key Insight**: `filterSignature` must sort all array fields and must include every filter field; omitting `hasVisual` would cause a picture-mode session (`hasVisual: true`) and a recall-mode session (same level, no `hasVisual`) to share the same `SeenWordsStore` key in RxDB — meaning seen-word tracking would be polluted across modes. The spec also notes the fallback behavior: when the unseen pile is smaller than `limit`, it takes all unseen + tops up from seen — this logic happens inside `pickWithRecycling` and must receive the `activeFilter` signature (the fallback-without-hasVisual filter), not the original. The PR's Task 8 implementation passes `filterSignature(activeFilter)` to `pickWithRecycling`, which correctly uses the fallback filter's signature when the hasVisual filter returned zero hits.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 5. Kid-Friendly Homepage — Card Sort Order and Cover/Emoji Architecture

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-kid-friendly-homepage-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/routes/$locale/_app/index.tsx`, `src/games/catalog-utils.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: The homepage card sort order (last-played first, then game defaults in registry order, then other custom games grouped by gameId) was designed in this spec and is implemented in `src/routes/$locale/_app/home-screen-card-order.ts` — a file that appears in the PR's diff.
- **Key Insight**: The sort is three-tier: last-played first (reading `session_history`), then game defaults in registry order, then other custom games by gameId. The spec documents this as a pure `sortByLastPlayed` helper in `src/games/catalog-utils.ts`. Any change to the home-screen card ordering must continue to respect this three-tier rule. The spec also documents that game defaults use `defaultCover: Cover` from `GAME_CATALOG` — the WordSpell default was `{ kind: 'emoji', emoji: '🔤', gradient: ['#fde68a', '#fb923c'] }`. This is separate from the `emoji` fields being added to curriculum words; the two emoji systems (`GameCover.emoji` for the card cover vs `CurriculumEntry.emoji` for individual word rounds) must not be conflated.

---

### Recommendations

1. **Confirm `core/level2.json` gets a `mum` entry.** The `joinHits` contract silently discards any `CurriculumEntry` whose word isn't in the same level's `WordCore` map. The build-time invariant in `words.test.ts` (assertion #3) should catch this, but verify that test runs as part of the PR CI. If the "mum" `WordCore` row is missing, the emoji-tagged curriculum entry for "mum" will be silently filtered out and picture mode will only show 7 words — one fewer than the hardcoded pool.

2. **`filterSignature` must include `hasVisual`.** Omitting it would cause `pickWithRecycling` to collide the SeenWordsStore RxDB key between picture-mode (`hasVisual: true`) and recall-mode sessions using the same curriculum. The plan already specifies this (Task 2, Step 2), but it is a load-bearing correctness requirement: when the `hasVisual` fallback triggers in `useLibraryRounds`, the `filterSignature(activeFilter)` call must use the fallback filter (without `hasVisual`), not the original filter, so seen-word recycling tracks against the right pool.

3. **The existing `mode-default-invariant.test.ts` tests the old picture invariant (rounds defined, source undefined).** Those tests must be updated before the implementation lands — they will fail as soon as `resolveWordSpellConfig` is changed. The PR's plan (Task 7, Step 1) handles this correctly by rewriting `assertPictureInvariant`. Watch for the edge case: a saved config that has explicit `rounds` (e.g. a user-authored custom game with hand-picked emoji words from before this PR) must be honored as-is, with `source` dropped — not overwritten with `hasVisual`.

4. **Do not confuse two emoji systems.** `GameCover.emoji` (the card-cover big emoji like `🔤`) is a UI display artifact stored in `GAME_CATALOG`. `CurriculumEntry.emoji` (like `🐱` for "cat") is the per-word visual for a round. They are independent. The home-screen card ordering change in `home-screen-card-order.ts` touches the former; the data additions in `curriculum/aus/level*.json` touch the latter.

5. **VR baselines will need re-recording.** Picture mode previously showed the hardcoded 8-word pool in a fixed deterministic order (`roundsInOrder: true` was set in the default). The new `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` uses a library source with `hasVisual: true`. If `roundsInOrder: true` is preserved (as shown in the plan's Task 7, Step 5 snippet), the VR output will be deterministic, but the round content changes (e.g. "mum" 🤱 is new). Run `yarn test:vr:update` after the implementation is complete; Docker must be running.

6. **`GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` must stay reconciled with the curriculum JSON files** — this is a pre-existing team invariant (see memory note `project_graphemes_by_level_invariant`). Adding "mum" to `level2.json` is fine because `m` and `u` are already in `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[2]`; no `levels.ts` changes are needed. But if any future emoji word were added at a level whose graphemes it uses aren't in `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL`, the curriculum-invariant test would fail.

Good — so the TDD cycle was: test added (skipped) in `76c5088a`, production code added in `1b4bb627`, then `62f811c8` un-skips (enables) the test. This is a valid TDD approach — the test was written as `.it.skip` (red, though technically not failing due to skip), then the code was added, then the test was un-skipped to go green.

However, CLAUDE.md says "Write a failing test that reproduces the bug **before** touching production code" — using `it.skip` is arguably not "a failing test" since it doesn't actually fail. But this is feature work, not a bug fix. For features: "Write tests first; implement only enough code to make them pass." The `.it.skip` is a valid way to write the test first without failing — this is borderline but the project accepted this pattern.

## Institutional Learnings Search Results

### Search Context

- **Feature/Task**: Consolidate picture-mode visual data into curriculum JSON — adding `emoji`/`image` fields to `CurriculumEntry`/`WordHit`/`DraftEntry`, adding `hasVisual` to `WordFilter`, inverting the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` so picture mode uses a library-sourced `source` with `hasVisual: true`, removing the hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`, updating `useLibraryRounds` fallback, and adjusting home-screen card ordering.
- **Keywords Used**: picture mode, emoji, hasVisual, WordFilter, WordHit, CurriculumEntry, DraftEntry, resolveWordSpellConfig, useLibraryRounds, filterSignature, curriculum data, home screen card ordering, joinHits, adapters, mode invariant
- **Files Scanned**: ~118 markdown files across `docs/`, `docs/superpowers/plans/`, `docs/superpowers/specs/`
- **Relevant Matches**: 7 files (plus the PR's own brainstorm and plan on the branch)

### Critical Patterns

No `docs/solutions/patterns/critical-patterns.md` exists in this repo.

### Relevant Learnings

#### 1. The Cumulative Y-Filter Spec — Mode-Driven Round Source and the Picture/Recall Invariant

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/games/word-spell`, `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred — no YAML frontmatter)
- **Relevance**: This is the direct predecessor design for the mode invariant PR #281 is inverting. It establishes the contract that PR #281 must update.
- **Key Insight**: The spec deliberately created two non-overlapping defaults: `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (library source, no rounds) and `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` (emoji rounds, no source). The invariant enforced by `resolveWordSpellConfig` is: `mode = recall ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined; mode = picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined`. PR #281 inverts the picture half of this to `mode = picture ⇒ source defined with hasVisual: true ∧ rounds undefined (unless explicit rounds were saved)`. The spec also contains a documented edge case table: "Picture mode toggled in Advanced → `source` is dropped, `rounds = WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` is restored, picker is hidden." This entry is now exactly the behavior being changed — do not preserve it.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 2. Level 3+ Silent Empty Pool — Root Cause Pattern to Avoid Repeating

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-28-wordspell-levels-defaults-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/filter.ts`, `src/games/word-spell/WordSpellSimpleConfigForm`
- **Problem Type**: `logic_error` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Documents the exact mechanism by which `entryMatches` silently drops words when a filter field is set but doesn't include prior-level values — the same class of subtle emptying that could affect `hasVisual` if the fallback in `useLibraryRounds` isn't wired.
- **Key Insight**: `filterWords` requires every grapheme of a word to be in `phonemesAllowed`. Using only the target level's phonemes (instead of cumulative) silently yields zero hits at levels 3+. The team's defense is `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` — a parameterized test asserting every `(region, level)` yields `≥ MIN_PLAYABLE_HITS`. PR #281 adds `hasVisual` as another filter predicate; a similar invariant test that runs `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` and asserts `&gt;= 8 hits` (the number of emoji-tagged words) would lock this in. The spec also documents that `toWordSpellRound` in `src/data/words/adapters.ts` sets only `word` with no `emoji`/`image` — which is precisely one of the changes PR #281 must make.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 3. The Phonics Word Library Design — Type Chain, joinHits, and the WordCore/CurriculumEntry Join Contract

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-phonics-word-library-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/types.ts`, `src/data/words/filter.ts`, `src/data/words/adapters.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `architecture_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: The foundational design document for the entire data chain PR #281 modifies. Defines the original `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `WordFilter`, and `filterWords` contracts.
- **Key Insight**: `joinHits` silently discards any `CurriculumEntry` that has no matching row in the `WordCore` map for its level file — `if (!c) return []`. This is a documented behavior. PR #281 adds "mum" to `aus/level2.json`; if `core/level2.json` does not also get a `WordCore` row for "mum", the entry will be silently discarded and no test will catch the omission unless the `curriculum-invariant.test.ts` has a specific test checking the "mum" entry by word. The plan doc on the branch (`Task 6`) already documents this risk — worth double-checking that the `words.test.ts` invariant (#3 in §10: "Every `CurriculumEntry.word` has a matching `WordCore` in the same level's core file") covers this. Also: the original `WordFilter` type did not include `hasVisual` — adding it to `filterSignature` in `seen-words.ts` is required to avoid cache collisions between picture and recall sessions using the same curriculum level.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 4. Library Levels Recall — filterSignature Stability and the SeenWordsStore Cache Key

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-word-spell-library-levels-recall-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/data/words/seen-words.ts`, `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: Defines `filterSignature` and its requirement to be stable regardless of property order or array ordering. PR #281 adds `hasVisual` to the signature — this spec is authoritative on the contract.
- **Key Insight**: `filterSignature` must sort all array fields and must include every filter field; omitting `hasVisual` would cause a picture-mode session (`hasVisual: true`) and a recall-mode session (same level, no `hasVisual`) to share the same `SeenWordsStore` key in RxDB — meaning seen-word tracking would be polluted across modes. The spec also notes the fallback behavior: when the unseen pile is smaller than `limit`, it takes all unseen + tops up from seen — this logic happens inside `pickWithRecycling` and must receive the `activeFilter` signature (the fallback-without-hasVisual filter), not the original. The PR's Task 8 implementation passes `filterSignature(activeFilter)` to `pickWithRecycling`, which correctly uses the fallback filter's signature when the hasVisual filter returned zero hits.
- **Severity**: (not in frontmatter)

#### 5. Kid-Friendly Homepage — Card Sort Order and Cover/Emoji Architecture

- **File**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-kid-friendly-homepage-design.md`
- **Module**: `src/routes/$locale/_app/index.tsx`, `src/games/catalog-utils.ts`
- **Problem Type**: `design_pattern` (inferred)
- **Relevance**: The homepage card sort order (last-played first, then game defaults in registry order, then other custom games grouped by gameId) was designed in this spec and is implemented in `src/routes/$locale/_app/home-screen-card-order.ts` — a file that appears in the PR's diff.
- **Key Insight**: The sort is three-tier: last-played first (reading `session_history`), then game defaults in registry order, then other custom games by gameId. The spec documents this as a pure `sortByLastPlayed` helper in `src/games/catalog-utils.ts`. Any change to the home-screen card ordering must continue to respect this three-tier rule. The spec also documents that game defaults use `defaultCover: Cover` from `GAME_CATALOG` — the WordSpell default was `{ kind: 'emoji', emoji: '🔤', gradient: ['#fde68a', '#fb923c'] }`. This is separate from the `emoji` fields being added to curriculum words; the two emoji systems (`GameCover.emoji` for the card cover vs `CurriculumEntry.emoji` for individual word rounds) must not be conflated.

---

### Recommendations

1. **Confirm `core/level2.json` gets a `mum` entry.** The `joinHits` contract silently discards any `CurriculumEntry` whose word isn't in the same level's `WordCore` map. The build-time invariant in `words.test.ts` (assertion #3) should catch this, but verify that test runs as part of the PR CI. If the "mum" `WordCore` row is missing, the emoji-tagged curriculum entry for "mum" will be silently filtered out and picture mode will only show 7 words — one fewer than the hardcoded pool.

2. **`filterSignature` must include `hasVisual`.** Omitting it would cause `pickWithRecycling` to collide the SeenWordsStore RxDB key between picture-mode (`hasVisual: true`) and recall-mode sessions using the same curriculum. The plan already specifies this (Task 2, Step 2), but it is a load-bearing correctness requirement: when the `hasVisual` fallback triggers in `useLibraryRounds`, the `filterSignature(activeFilter)` call must use the fallback filter (without `hasVisual`), not the original filter, so seen-word recycling tracks against the right pool.

3. **The existing `mode-default-invariant.test.ts` tests the old picture invariant (rounds defined, source undefined).** Those tests must be updated before the implementation lands — they will fail as soon as `resolveWordSpellConfig` is changed. The PR's plan (Task 7, Step 1) handles this correctly by rewriting `assertPictureInvariant`. Watch for the edge case: a saved config that has explicit `rounds` (e.g. a user-authored custom game with hand-picked emoji words from before this PR) must be honored as-is, with `source` dropped — not overwritten with `hasVisual`.

4. **Do not confuse two emoji systems.** `GameCover.emoji` (the card-cover big emoji like `🔤`) is a UI display artifact stored in `GAME_CATALOG`. `CurriculumEntry.emoji` (like `🐱` for "cat") is the per-word visual for a round. They are independent. The home-screen card ordering change in `home-screen-card-order.ts` touches the former; the data additions in `curriculum/aus/level*.json` touch the latter.

5. **VR baselines will need re-recording.** Picture mode previously showed the hardcoded 8-word pool in a fixed deterministic order (`roundsInOrder: true` was set in the default). The new `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` uses a library source with `hasVisual: true`. If `roundsInOrder: true` is preserved (as shown in the plan's Task 7, Step 5 snippet), the VR output will be deterministic, but the round content changes (e.g. "mum" 🤱 is new). Run `yarn test:vr:update` after the implementation is complete; Docker must be running.

6. **`GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` must stay reconciled with the curriculum JSON files** — this is a pre-existing team invariant (see memory note `project_graphemes_by_level_invariant`). Adding "mum" to `level2.json` is fine because `m` and `u` are already in `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[2]`; no `levels.ts` changes are needed. But if any future emoji word were added at a level whose graphemes it uses aren't in `GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL`, the curriculum-invariant test would fail.</result>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:41:25Z)**_

Now I have everything I need. Let me compile the findings:

**Summary of analysis:**

1. **`filter.ts` line 194 — `as WordHit` cast in `joinHits`** — Pre-existing, not introduced by this PR. The new `emoji`/`image` fields are both optional in `WordHit` so there's no structural gap introduced. This is a pre-existing minor weakness (the object literal fully satisfies `WordHit` without the cast) but not a PR regression.

2. **`resolveWordSpellConfig` line 289 — `return rest as WordSpellConfig`** — New cast introduced by this PR. After `const { source: _ignored, ...rest } = merged`, `rest` is `Omit<WordSpellConfig, 'source'>`, which is assignment-compatible with `WordSpellConfig` since `source` is optional. The cast silences what would otherwise be a clean return — TypeScript would accept the implicit assignment without the cast. This is a P2-style smell: the cast papers over what would be a valid implicit assignment and is unnecessary. At confidence 75 because it's directly visible in the diff, it's not dangerous (the type is actually safe) but it's a lazy `as` instead of a no-cost fix.

3. **`resolveWordSpellConfig` — `saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>` merge pattern** — Pre-existing, not introduced by this PR.

4. **`useLibraryRounds` fallback — silent signature change** — When the `hasVisual` fallback fires, `filterSignature(activeFilter)` is passed to `pickWithRecycling` instead of `filterSignature(source.filter)`. This is intentional and correct (you want the recycling key to match the actual filter used), but it means a user who sees zero visual hits will have their seen-words store keyed under the non-`hasVisual` signature. If they switch back to a fully-loaded dataset later, the recycling cycle restarts from a different namespace. This is a design tradeoff, not a bug, but it's not called out in the code. Medium confidence, surface as residual risk.

5. **`home-screen-card-order.ts` discriminated union** — The type `HomeScreenCardSortRow` uses two variants that differ only in `kind`, which is a valid discriminated union. The comparator is clean and fully typed. No issues.

6. **No `hasVisual: false` case handled** — `WordFilter.hasVisual` is `boolean | undefined`, but the predicate `if (filter.hasVisual && ...)` only handles the truthy case. If someone passes `hasVisual: false`, the condition is falsy (skipped), meaning "explicitly no visual" is treated identically to "don't care". This is likely intentional (false means "don't filter") but the semantics are not obvious. Low-confidence issue.

7. **`filterSignature` for hasVisual** — `hasVisual=${filter.hasVisual ?? ''}` — when `hasVisual: false`, this encodes as `hasVisual=false`, distinct from `hasVisual=` (undefined). This is consistent: the signature correctly distinguishes the three states even if the predicate treats false and undefined the same way.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:41:42Z)**_

Now I have a comprehensive picture of the codebase. Let me compile the complete analysis.

---

## Agent-Native Architecture Review

### Summary

BaseSkill is a React/TanStack Router educational games app (word spelling, number matching, etc.) with no LLM agent integration in the traditional sense -- there are no `tool()` registrations, no `streamText`/`generateText` calls, no MCP server exposing app actions, and no LLM API dependencies. The only "agent" story is Claude Code itself operating as a developer-level coding agent, governed by `AGENTS.md`, `.claude/skills/`, and `CLAUDE.md`. There is no runtime agent that plays or configures games on behalf of users.

This PR (issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji) adds `emoji`/`image` fields to the type chain, `hasVisual` filtering to `WordFilter`, inverts the picture-mode config invariant, and adds home-screen card ordering logic. The question then is: can a coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor) access and exercise all of these new data pathways the same way a developer/user can? The answer is largely yes, with three meaningful gaps identified below.

### Capability Map

| UI Action / User Capability | Location | Programmatic Equivalent | In AGENTS.md / Skills? | Priority | Status |
|-----------------------------|----------|------------------------|------------------------|----------|--------|
| Filter words with `hasVisual: true` | `filter.ts:36` | `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` -- exported from `src/data/words/index.ts` | No | Must have | PASS -- exported, testable |
| `hasVisual` included in `filterSignature` (seen-words dedup) | `seen-words.ts:42` | Same `filterSignature()` call -- exported | No | Must have | PASS |
| `emoji`/`image` flow from curriculum JSON → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound` | `filter.ts:182-196`, `adapters.ts:4-8` | All functions exported | No | Must have | PASS |
| Picture mode uses library source with `hasVisual` (not hardcoded pool) | `$gameId.tsx:106-124`, `resolveWordSpellConfig` | `resolveWordSpellConfig` exported from `$gameId.tsx` | No | Must have | PASS |
| hasVisual zero-results fallback | `useLibraryRounds.ts:70-76` | Hook only; no standalone function exposed | No | Should have | OBSERVATION |
| Set `emoji`/`image` on a `DraftEntry` via authoring | `AuthoringPanel.tsx` | `draftStore.saveDraft()` / `updateDraft()` accept `DraftEntry` which now has `emoji?`/`image?` -- but `AuthoringPanel` has no UI fields for them | No | Must have | GAP -- see Finding 1 |
| View `hasVisual` as an active filter pill in WordLibraryExplorer | `active-filter-pills.ts`, `WordLibraryExplorer.tsx` | `deriveActiveFilterPills()` does not handle `hasVisual` | No | Should have | GAP -- see Finding 2 |
| See picture mode indicated on home-screen GameCard chips | `config-chips.ts:4-29` | `chipsForWordSpell` reads `source.filter` but ignores `mode` and `hasVisual` | No | Should have | GAP -- see Finding 3 |
| Sort home-screen cards by bookmark + kind + index | `home-screen-card-order.ts:5-13` | `compareHomeScreenCardRows` exported, fully testable | No | Must have | PASS |
| `hasVisual` filter in `WordFilter` typed correctly for `WordSpellSource` | `types.ts:80`, `types.ts:90` | `WordSpellSource.filter` is typed as `WordFilter` -- `hasVisual` is accessible | No | Must have | PASS |

### Findings

#### Warnings (Should Fix)

1. **`hasVisual` missing from `deriveActiveFilterPills`** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/active-filter-pills.ts:15-63` -- The `deriveActiveFilterPills` function generates the dismissible filter-pill badges displayed in `WordLibraryExplorer`. It handles `levels`, `levelRange`, `syllableCountEq`, `syllableCountRange`, and grapheme pairs, but has no branch for `hasVisual`. When a coding agent (or a future automated test) calls `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` and then reflects the active filter state through `deriveActiveFilterPills`, the `hasVisual: true` filter is invisible -- there is no pill to clear and no indication the visual filter is active. Recommendation: add a `hasVisual` pill: `if (filter.hasVisual) pills.push({ id: 'has-visual', label: 'has visual', clear: 'hasVisual' })`.

   Confidence: 75 -- directly visible from the code; `hasVisual` is the only new `WordFilter` field not covered in the pill function.

2. **`chipsForWordSpell` does not reflect picture mode or `hasVisual`** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/config-chips.ts:4-29` -- The `chipsForWordSpell` function produces the summary chips shown on `GameCard` tiles on the home screen. After this PR, picture mode is identified by `source.filter.hasVisual === true`, but `chipsForWordSpell` reads `source.filter.level`, `phonemesAllowed`, and `graphemesAllowed` only -- it does not read `filter.hasVisual` or `config.mode`. A coding agent constructing a saved config for picture mode and inspecting `configToChips('word-spell', config)` gets no indication that the config is in picture mode. This asymmetry means the home screen card is silent about mode for picture configs. Recommendation: add `if (filter.hasVisual) chips.push('Picture')` (or equivalent) in `chipsForWordSpell`, parallel to how `chipsForNumberMatch` surfaces `config.mode`.

   Confidence: 75 -- mechanically traceable: `chipsForNumberMatch` at line 35 reads `config.mode`; `chipsForWordSpell` does not.

#### Observations

3. **`AuthoringPanel` has no `emoji`/`image` input fields** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx` -- `DraftEntry` now carries `emoji?` and `image?` (per `types.ts:41-42`), and `draftStore.saveDraft`/`updateDraft` accept the full `DraftEntry`. However, `AuthoringPanel` has no form fields for these new fields. This means a teacher using the authoring UI cannot attach an emoji to a draft word, even though the data model supports it. A coding agent that calls `draftStore.saveDraft({ ..., emoji: '🐱' })` directly can set the field, but there is no UI path. The PR scope explicitly excludes "Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel" (plan known limitation), so this is intentionally deferred, not a defect. Flagging as an Observation so the gap is tracked.

4. **`resolveSimpleConfig` does not inject `hasVisual` for picture mode** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts:70-91` -- When `mode === 'picture'`, `resolveSimpleConfig` builds a `source.filter` with broad `graphemesAllowed` and empty `graphemesRequired`, but does not set `hasVisual: true`. The advanced resolver (`resolveWordSpellConfig`) does enforce the picture-mode invariant and would add `hasVisual: true` in a second pass. However, if an agent (or test) calls `resolveSimpleConfig({ configMode: 'simple', mode: 'picture', ... })` directly, the resulting config has no `hasVisual` flag, so `useLibraryRounds` would not filter to visual words until `resolveWordSpellConfig` post-processes it. This is a latent inconsistency between the two resolver paths -- a coding agent reading `resolveSimpleConfig` output cannot predict that picture mode implies `hasVisual` without also knowing about the second-pass invariant in `$gameId.tsx`. Low priority since `resolveSimpleConfig` output always flows through `resolveWordSpellConfig` in practice, but the public API contract is ambiguous.

5. **`loadShippedWordLevels` and `loadShippedIndex` do not expose visual metadata** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/filter.ts:201-219` -- These two exported helpers return `Set<string>` (word index) and `Map<string, number>` (word-to-level map) respectively, derived from curriculum data. They do not expose `emoji`/`image` availability. A coding agent that wants to know "which shipped words have visuals at level N" must call the heavier `filterWords()` with `hasVisual: true`. This is a minor context-starvation issue, not a blocking gap; `filterWords` is fully accessible and exported.

### What's Working Well

- All core new primitives (`filterWords`, `filterSignature`, `entryMatches`, `toWordSpellRound`, `compareHomeScreenCardRows`) are exported from their modules and directly callable without a browser context, which means a coding agent can exercise every new data pathway in unit tests without UI involvement.
- The `hasVisual` field is correctly included in `filterSignature`, so seen-word deduplication keys are distinct between visual and non-visual sessions -- no silent collision.
- The picture-mode invariant inversion in `resolveWordSpellConfig` is cleanly expressed and fully covered by the new test files (`mode-default-invariant.test.ts`, `resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts`), making the logic transparent and verifiable programmatically.
- The plan document (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji.md`) explicitly documents the known limitation about `aus`-only hardcoding and deferred authoring UI, which is good context for any future agent picking up this work.
- `DraftEntry`'s new `emoji`/`image` fields are optional with no IndexedDB schema version bump required, meaning agents working with the draft store do not need migration awareness.

### Score

- **7/7 new must-have data primitives are programmatically accessible** (filter, adapter, seen-words, type chain, config resolver, home-screen sort, fallback hook logic).
- **2 secondary surface areas (filter pills, home-screen chips) are missing `hasVisual`/picture-mode awareness** -- these are display-layer gaps, not data-layer gaps.
- **Verdict: PASS with Warnings** -- the core agent-accessible data layer is complete and well-tested. The two warnings are display-layer inconsistencies that cause silent misrepresentation of the picture-mode config state in the Explorer and on home-screen cards, but do not block any programmatic workflow.

## Agent-Native Architecture Review

### Summary

BaseSkill is a React/TanStack Router educational games app (word spelling, number matching, etc.) with no LLM agent integration in the traditional sense -- there are no `tool()` registrations, no `streamText`/`generateText` calls, no MCP server exposing app actions, and no LLM API dependencies. The only "agent" story is Claude Code itself operating as a developer-level coding agent, governed by `AGENTS.md`, `.claude/skills/`, and `CLAUDE.md`. There is no runtime agent that plays or configures games on behalf of users.

This PR (issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji) adds `emoji`/`image` fields to the type chain, `hasVisual` filtering to `WordFilter`, inverts the picture-mode config invariant, and adds home-screen card ordering logic. The question then is: can a coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor) access and exercise all of these new data pathways the same way a developer/user can? The answer is largely yes, with three meaningful gaps identified below.

### Capability Map

| UI Action / User Capability | Location | Programmatic Equivalent | In AGENTS.md / Skills? | Priority | Status |
|-----------------------------|----------|------------------------|------------------------|----------|--------|
| Filter words with `hasVisual: true` | `filter.ts:36` | `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` -- exported from `src/data/words/index.ts` | No | Must have | PASS -- exported, testable |
| `hasVisual` included in `filterSignature` (seen-words dedup) | `seen-words.ts:42` | Same `filterSignature()` call -- exported | No | Must have | PASS |
| `emoji`/`image` flow from curriculum JSON → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound` | `filter.ts:182-196`, `adapters.ts:4-8` | All functions exported | No | Must have | PASS |
| Picture mode uses library source with `hasVisual` (not hardcoded pool) | `$gameId.tsx:106-124`, `resolveWordSpellConfig` | `resolveWordSpellConfig` exported from `$gameId.tsx` | No | Must have | PASS |
| hasVisual zero-results fallback | `useLibraryRounds.ts:70-76` | Hook only; no standalone function exposed | No | Should have | OBSERVATION |
| Set `emoji`/`image` on a `DraftEntry` via authoring | `AuthoringPanel.tsx` | `draftStore.saveDraft()` / `updateDraft()` accept `DraftEntry` which now has `emoji?`/`image?` -- but `AuthoringPanel` has no UI fields for them | No | Must have | GAP -- see Finding 1 |
| View `hasVisual` as an active filter pill in WordLibraryExplorer | `active-filter-pills.ts`, `WordLibraryExplorer.tsx` | `deriveActiveFilterPills()` does not handle `hasVisual` | No | Should have | GAP -- see Finding 2 |
| See picture mode indicated on home-screen GameCard chips | `config-chips.ts:4-29` | `chipsForWordSpell` reads `source.filter` but ignores `mode` and `hasVisual` | No | Should have | GAP -- see Finding 3 |
| Sort home-screen cards by bookmark + kind + index | `home-screen-card-order.ts:5-13` | `compareHomeScreenCardRows` exported, fully testable | No | Must have | PASS |
| `hasVisual` filter in `WordFilter` typed correctly for `WordSpellSource` | `types.ts:80`, `types.ts:90` | `WordSpellSource.filter` is typed as `WordFilter` -- `hasVisual` is accessible | No | Must have | PASS |

### Findings

#### Warnings (Should Fix)

1. **`hasVisual` missing from `deriveActiveFilterPills`** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/active-filter-pills.ts:15-63` -- The `deriveActiveFilterPills` function generates the dismissible filter-pill badges displayed in `WordLibraryExplorer`. It handles `levels`, `levelRange`, `syllableCountEq`, `syllableCountRange`, and grapheme pairs, but has no branch for `hasVisual`. When a coding agent (or a future automated test) calls `filterWords({ region: 'aus', hasVisual: true })` and then reflects the active filter state through `deriveActiveFilterPills`, the `hasVisual: true` filter is invisible -- there is no pill to clear and no indication the visual filter is active. Recommendation: add a `hasVisual` pill: `if (filter.hasVisual) pills.push({ id: 'has-visual', label: 'has visual', clear: 'hasVisual' })`.

   Confidence: 75 -- directly visible from the code; `hasVisual` is the only new `WordFilter` field not covered in the pill function.

2. **`chipsForWordSpell` does not reflect picture mode or `hasVisual`** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/games/config-chips.ts:4-29` -- The `chipsForWordSpell` function produces the summary chips shown on `GameCard` tiles on the home screen. After this PR, picture mode is identified by `source.filter.hasVisual === true`, but `chipsForWordSpell` reads `source.filter.level`, `phonemesAllowed`, and `graphemesAllowed` only -- it does not read `filter.hasVisual` or `config.mode`. A coding agent constructing a saved config for picture mode and inspecting `configToChips('word-spell', config)` gets no indication that the config is in picture mode. This asymmetry means the home screen card is silent about mode for picture configs. Recommendation: add `if (filter.hasVisual) chips.push('Picture')` (or equivalent) in `chipsForWordSpell`, parallel to how `chipsForNumberMatch` surfaces `config.mode`.

   Confidence: 75 -- mechanically traceable: `chipsForNumberMatch` at line 35 reads `config.mode`; `chipsForWordSpell` does not.

#### Observations

3. **`AuthoringPanel` has no `emoji`/`image` input fields** -- `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx` -- `DraftEntry` now carries `emoji?` and `image?` (per `types.ts:41-42`), and `draftStore.saveDraft`/`updateDraft` accept the full `DraftEntry`. However, `AuthoringPanel` has no form fields for these new fields. This means a teacher using the authoring UI cannot attach an emoji to a draft word, even though the data model supports it. A coding agent that calls `draftStore.saveDraft({ ..., emoji: '🐱' })` directly can set the field, but there is no UI path. The PR scope explicitly excludes "Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel" (plan known limitation), so this is intentionally deferred, not a defect.
